24	// GenerateAgentSession creates a SessionData from a Claude Code Session
25	// The session should already be parsed from JSONL with records in chronological order
26	func GenerateAgentSession(session Session, workspaceRoot string) (*SessionData, error) {
27		slog.Info("GenerateAgentSession: Starting", "sessionID", session.SessionUuid, "recordCount", len(session.Records))
28	
29		if len(session.Records) == 0 {
30			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no records")
31		}
32	
33		// Extract metadata from records
34		createdAt := extractCreatedAtFromRecords(session.Records)
35		if workspaceRoot == "" {
36			workspaceRoot = extractWorkspaceRootFromRecords(session.Records)
37		}
38	
39		// Group records into exchanges
40		exchanges, err := buildExchangesFromRecords(session.Records, workspaceRoot)
41		if err != nil {
42			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to build exchanges: %w", err)
43		}
44	
45		// Assign exchangeId to each exchange (format: sessionId:index)
46		for i := range exchanges {
47			exchanges[i].ExchangeID = fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", session.SessionUuid, i)
48		}
49	
50		slog.Info("GenerateAgentSession: Built exchanges", "count", len(exchanges))
51	
52		// Populate FormattedMarkdown for all tools
53		for i := range exchanges {
54			for j := range exchanges[i].Messages {
55				msg := &exchanges[i].Messages[j]
56				if msg.Tool != nil {
57					formattedMd := formatToolAsMarkdown(msg.Tool, workspaceRoot)
58					msg.Tool.FormattedMarkdown = &formattedMd
59				}
60			}
61		}
62	
63		sessionData := &SessionData{
64			SchemaVersion: "1.0",
65			Provider: ProviderInfo{
66				ID:      "claude-code",
67				Name:    "Claude Code",
68				Version: "unknown",
69			},
70			SessionID:     session.SessionUuid,
71			CreatedAt:     createdAt,
72			WorkspaceRoot: workspaceRoot,
73			Exchanges:     exchanges,
74		}
75	
76		return sessionData, nil
77	}
78	
79	// extractCreatedAtFromRecords extracts the earliest timestamp from JSONL records
80	func extractCreatedAtFromRecords(records []JSONLRecord) string {
81		if len(records) == 0 {
82			return time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339)
83		}
84	
85		// Get timestamp from first record
86		if timestamp, ok := records[0].Data["timestamp"].(string); ok && timestamp != "" {
87			return timestamp
88		}
89	
90		return time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339)
91	}
92	
93	// extractWorkspaceRootFromRecords extracts the workspace root from JSONL records
94	func extractWorkspaceRootFromRecords(records []JSONLRecord) string {
95		for _, record := range records {
96			if cwd, ok := record.Data["cwd"].(string); ok && cwd != "" {
97				return cwd
98			}
99		}
100		return ""
101	}
102	
103	// buildExchangesFromRecords groups JSONL records into exchanges
104	// An exchange is a user prompt followed by agent responses and tool results
105	// Records are assumed to be in chronological order from JSONL parsing
106	func buildExchangesFromRecords(records []JSONLRecord, workspaceRoot string) ([]Exchange, error) {
107		var exchanges []Exchange
108		var currentExchange *Exchange
109		var exchangeStartTime string
110	
111		for _, record := range records {
112			recordType, _ := record.Data["type"].(string)
113			timestamp, _ := record.Data["timestamp"].(string)
114	
115			// Check if this is a sidechain message (subagent conversation)
116			// Note: warmup messages are filtered earlier by filterWarmupMessages
117			isSidechain, _ := record.Data["isSidechain"].(bool)
118	
119			switch recordType {
120			case "user":
121				// Check if this is a tool result or a new user message
122				message, _ := record.Data["message"].(map[string]interface{})
123				messageContent, _ := message["content"].([]interface{})
124	
125				isToolResult := false
126				if len(messageContent) > 0 {
127					if firstContent, ok := messageContent[0].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
128						if contentType, ok := firstContent["type"].(string); ok && contentType == "tool_result" {
129							isToolResult = true
130						}
131					}
132				}
133	
134				if isToolResult {
135					// This is a tool result - merge it into the previous tool use
136					if currentExchange != nil {
137						mergeToolResultIntoExchange(currentExchange, messageContent, timestamp)
138						currentExchange.EndTime = timestamp
139					}
140				} else {
141					// This is a new user prompt
142	
143					// Sidechain messages append to current exchange (parent agent talking to subagent)
144					// rather than starting a new exchange like regular user prompts do
145					if isSidechain {
146						if currentExchange == nil {
147							slog.Warn("Sidechain user message with no current exchange", "uuid", record.Data["uuid"])
148							continue
149						}
150						msg := buildUserMessage(record, isSidechain)
151						currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, msg)
152						currentExchange.EndTime = timestamp
153						continue
154					}
155	
156					// Non-sidechain: start a new exchange
157					if currentExchange != nil {
158						exchanges = append(exchanges, *currentExchange)
159					}
160	
161					currentExchange = &Exchange{
162						StartTime: timestamp,
163						Messages:  []Message{buildUserMessage(record, isSidechain)},
164					}
165				}
166	
167			case "assistant":
168				// Agent message - add to current exchange
169				if currentExchange == nil {
170					// Create exchange if we don't have one (shouldn't happen normally)
171					exchangeStartTime = timestamp
172					currentExchange = &Exchange{
173						StartTime: exchangeStartTime,
174						Messages:  []Message{},
175					}
176				}
177	
178				msg := buildAgentMessage(record, workspaceRoot, isSidechain)
179				currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, msg)
180				currentExchange.EndTime = timestamp
181			}
182		}
183	
184		// Add the last exchange if exists
185		if currentExchange != nil && len(currentExchange.Messages) > 0 {
186			exchanges = append(exchanges, *currentExchange)
187		}
188	
189		// Records are already in chronological order from JSONL parsing,
190		// so exchanges are also in order. No sorting needed.
191	
192		return exchanges, nil
193	}
194	
195	// mergeToolResultIntoExchange finds the tool use in the exchange and adds the result to it.
196	// The resultTimestamp is the time the tool completed (from the tool_result JSONL record).
197	func mergeToolResultIntoExchange(exchange *Exchange, messageContent []interface{}, resultTimestamp string) {
198		// Extract tool_result data from message content
199		if len(messageContent) == 0 {
200			return
201		}
202	
203		toolResultMap, ok := messageContent[0].(map[string]interface{})
204		if !ok {
205			return
206		}
207	
208		// Get the tool_use_id that this result corresponds to
209		toolUseID, ok := toolResultMap["tool_use_id"].(string)
210		if !ok || toolUseID == "" {
211			return
212		}
213	
214		// Extract result content and error status
215		// Content can be either a string or an array of text objects (for Task tool results)
216		var content string
217		if contentStr, ok := toolResultMap["content"].(string); ok {
218			content = contentStr
219		} else if contentArray, ok := toolResultMap["content"].([]interface{}); ok {
220			// Array of text objects like [{"type":"text","text":"..."}]
221			var parts []string
222			for _, item := range contentArray {
223				if itemMap, ok := item.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
224					if text, ok := itemMap["text"].(string); ok {
225						parts = append(parts, text)
226					}
227				}
228			}
229			content = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
230		}
231		isError, _ := toolResultMap["is_error"].(bool)
232	
233		// Find the message with matching tool use ID and add the output
234		for i := range exchange.Messages {
235			msg := &exchange.Messages[i]
236			if msg.Tool != nil && msg.Tool.UseID == toolUseID {
237				msg.Tool.Output = map[string]interface{}{
238					"content":  content,
239					"is_error": isError,
240				}
241				// Update message timestamp to when the tool actually completed,
242				// not when the agent requested it (needed for provenance correlation)
243				msg.Timestamp = resultTimestamp
244				return
245			}
246		}
247	}
248	
249	// buildUserMessage creates a Message from a user JSONL record
250	func buildUserMessage(record JSONLRecord, isSidechain bool) Message {
251		uuid, _ := record.Data["uuid"].(string)
252		timestamp, _ := record.Data["timestamp"].(string)
253	
254		message, _ := record.Data["message"].(map[string]interface{})
255		var contentParts []ContentPart
256	
257		// Handle different content formats
258		if content, ok := message["content"].(string); ok {
259			// Simple string content
260			contentParts = append(contentParts, ContentPart{
261				Type: "text",
262				Text: content,
263			})
264		} else if contentArray, ok := message["content"].([]interface{}); ok {
265			// Array of content parts
266			for _, item := range contentArray {
267				if contentMap, ok := item.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
268					contentType, _ := contentMap["type"].(string)
269	
270					switch contentType {
271					case "text":
272						text, _ := contentMap["text"].(string)
273						contentParts = append(contentParts, ContentPart{
274							Type: "text",
275							Text: text,
276						})
277					case "thinking":
278						thinking, _ := contentMap["thinking"].(string)
279						contentParts = append(contentParts, ContentPart{
280							Type: "thinking",
281							Text: thinking,
282						})
283						// tool_result is now merged into tool use, not handled here
284					}
285				}
286			}
287		}
288	
289		msg := Message{
290			ID:        uuid,
291			Timestamp: timestamp,
292			Role:      "user",
293			Content:   contentParts,
294		}
295	
296		// For sidechain messages, treat as agent (parent agent talking to subagent, not actual user input)
297		if isSidechain {
298			msg.Role = "agent"
299			msg.Metadata = map[string]interface{}{
300				"isSidechain": true,
301			}
302		}
303	
304		return msg
305	}
306	
307	// buildAgentMessage creates a Message from an assistant JSONL record
308	func buildAgentMessage(record JSONLRecord, workspaceRoot string, isSidechain bool) Message {
309		uuid, _ := record.Data["uuid"].(string)
310		timestamp, _ := record.Data["timestamp"].(string)
311	
312		message, _ := record.Data["message"].(map[string]interface{})
313		model, _ := message["model"].(string)
314	
315		var contentParts []ContentPart
316		var toolInfo *ToolInfo
317		var pathHints []string
318	
319		// Parse message content
320		if contentArray, ok := message["content"].([]interface{}); ok {
321			for _, item := range contentArray {
322				if contentMap, ok := item.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
323					contentType, _ := contentMap["type"].(string)
324	
325					switch contentType {
326					case "text":
327						text, _ := contentMap["text"].(string)
328						if text != "" {
329							contentParts = append(contentParts, ContentPart{
330								Type: "text",
331								Text: text,
332							})
333						}
334	
335					case "thinking":
336						// Preserve thinking content with its type
337						thinking, _ := contentMap["thinking"].(string)
338						if thinking != "" {
339							contentParts = append(contentParts, ContentPart{
340								Type: "thinking",
341								Text: thinking,
342							})
343						}
344	
345					case "tool_use":
346						// Extract tool information
347						toolName, _ := contentMap["name"].(string)
348						toolUseID, _ := contentMap["id"].(string)
349						toolInput, _ := contentMap["input"].(map[string]interface{})
350	
351						toolInfo = &ToolInfo{
352							Name:  toolName,
353							Type:  classifyToolType(toolName),
354							UseID: toolUseID,
355							Input: toolInput,
356						}
357	
358						// Extract path hints using the record's cwd, which tracks the
359						// shell's actual working directory (changes after cd commands)
360						recordCwd, _ := record.Data["cwd"].(string)
361						if recordCwd == "" {
362							recordCwd = workspaceRoot
363						}
364						pathHints = extractPathHints(toolInput, recordCwd, workspaceRoot)
365					}
366				}
367			}
368		}
369	
370		// Also check for tool use result in the record
371		if toolUseResult, ok := record.Data["toolUseResult"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
372			if toolInfo != nil {
373				// Add output to existing tool info
374				toolInfo.Output = map[string]interface{}{
375					"type":     toolUseResult["type"],
376					"filePath": toolUseResult["filePath"],
377				}
378			}
379		}
380	
381		// Extract usage data if present
382		var usageInfo *Usage
383		if usage, ok := message["usage"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
384			usageInfo = &Usage{
385				InputTokens:              schema.GetIntFromMap(usage, "input_tokens"),
386				OutputTokens:             schema.GetIntFromMap(usage, "output_tokens"),
387				CacheCreationInputTokens: schema.GetIntFromMap(usage, "cache_creation_input_tokens"),
388				CacheReadInputTokens:     schema.GetIntFromMap(usage, "cache_read_input_tokens"),
389			}
390		}
391	
392		msg := Message{
393			ID:        uuid,
394			Timestamp: timestamp,
395			Role:      "agent",
396			Model:     model,
397			Content:   contentParts,
398			Tool:      toolInfo,
399			PathHints: pathHints,
400			Usage:     usageInfo,
401		}
402	
403		// Add sidechain flag to metadata if true
404		if isSidechain {
405			msg.Metadata = map[string]interface{}{
406				"isSidechain": true,
407			}
408		}
409	
410		return msg
411	}
412	
413	// classifyToolType maps tool names to standard tool types
414	func classifyToolType(toolName string) string {
415		toolName = strings.ToLower(toolName)
416	
417		switch toolName {
418		// "str_replace_editor", "write_file", "edit_file", "create_file", "append_to_file" are legacy tools, no longer in the agent
419		case "write", "edit", "multiedit", "str_replace_editor", "write_file", "edit_file", "create_file", "append_to_file":
420			return "write"
421		case "read", "webfetch", "read_file", "view_file", "cat": // "read_file", "view_file", "cat" are legacy tools, no longer in the agent
422			return "read"
423		case "grep", "glob", "websearch":
424			return "search"
425		case "bash", "taskoutput", "killshell":
426			return "shell"
427		case "notebookedit", "todowrite":
428			return "task"
429		case "enterplanmode", "exitplanmode", "askuserquestion", "skill", "lsp", "task":
430			return "generic"
431		default:
432			return "unknown"
433		}
434	}
435	
436	// extractPathHints extracts file paths from tool input.
437	// cwd is the shell's working directory at the time of the tool call (from the
438	// JSONL record's top-level cwd field), used to resolve relative paths in shell commands.
439	func extractPathHints(input map[string]interface{}, cwd, workspaceRoot string) []string {
440		var paths []string
441	
442		// Common path field names
443		pathFields := []string{"file_path", "path", "filename", "old_path", "new_path"}
444		arrayFields := []string{"paths", "files", "targets"}
445	
446		// Extract single path fields
447		for _, field := range pathFields {
448			if value, ok := input[field].(string); ok && value != "" {
449				normalizedPath := spi.NormalizePath(value, workspaceRoot)
450				if !contains(paths, normalizedPath) {
451					paths = append(paths, normalizedPath)
452				}
453			}
454		}
455	
456		// Extract array path fields
457		for _, field := range arrayFields {
458			if arr, ok := input[field].([]interface{}); ok {
459				for _, item := range arr {
460					if path, ok := item.(string); ok && path != "" {
461						normalizedPath := spi.NormalizePath(path, workspaceRoot)
462						if !contains(paths, normalizedPath) {
463							paths = append(paths, normalizedPath)
464						}
465					}
466				}
467			}
468		}
469	
470		// Extract paths from shell commands (redirect targets, file-creating commands)
471		if command, ok := input["command"].(string); ok && command != "" {
472			shellPaths := spi.ExtractShellPathHints(command, cwd, workspaceRoot)
473			for _, sp := range shellPaths {
474				if !contains(paths, sp) {
475					paths = append(paths, sp)
476				}
477			}
478		}
479	
480		return paths
481	}
482	
483	// contains checks if a string slice contains a value
484	func contains(slice []string, value string) bool {
485		for _, item := range slice {
486			if item == value {
487				return true
488			}
489		}
490		return false
491	}
492	
493	// formatToolAsMarkdown generates formatted markdown for a tool (input + output)
494	// Returns the inner content only (no <tool-use> tags)
495	func formatToolAsMarkdown(tool *ToolInfo, workspaceRoot string) string {
496		var markdown strings.Builder
497	
498		// Add _cwd to input for path normalization in formatters
499		if tool.Input == nil {
500			tool.Input = make(map[string]interface{})
501		}
502		tool.Input["_cwd"] = workspaceRoot
503	
504		// Extract description if present
505		description := ""
506		if desc, ok := tool.Input["description"].(string); ok {
507			description = desc
508		}
509	
510		// Format tool use based on tool name using existing formatters
511		var toolContent string
512		if formatter, ok := toolFormatters[tool.Name]; ok {
513			// Use tool-specific formatter
514			toolContent = formatter(tool.Name, tool.Input, description)
515		} else {
516			// Use default formatter
517			toolContent = formatDefaultTool(tool.Name, tool.Input, description)
518		}
519	
520		// Add formatted tool content
521		if toolContent != "" {
522			markdown.WriteString(toolContent)
523			markdown.WriteString("\n")
524		}
525	
526		// Add tool result/output if present
527		if tool.Output != nil {
528			// Check for error first
529			if isError, ok := tool.Output["is_error"].(bool); ok && isError {
530				// Blank line before code fence helps parsers inside <details> tags
531				markdown.WriteString("\n```text\n")
532				if content, ok := tool.Output["content"].(string); ok {
533					cleaned := stripSystemReminders(content)
534					cleaned = stripANSIEscapeSequences(cleaned)
535					markdown.WriteString(cleaned)
536				}
537				markdown.WriteString("\n```\n")
538			} else {
539				// Regular result
540				if content, ok := tool.Output["content"].(string); ok {
541					cleaned := stripSystemReminders(content)
542					cleaned = stripANSIEscapeSequences(cleaned)
543					// Only trim trailing spaces/tabs, preserve all newlines
544					cleaned = strings.TrimRight(cleaned, " \t")
545	
546					// Special handling for AskUserQuestion - parse and format the answer nicely
547					if tool.Name == "AskUserQuestion" {
548						answer := parseAskUserQuestionAnswer(cleaned)
549						if answer != "" {
550							fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, "\n**Answer:** %s\n", answer)
551						} else if cleaned != "" {
552							// Fallback to code block if parsing fails
553							markdown.WriteString("\n```text\n")
554							markdown.WriteString(cleaned)
555							markdown.WriteString("\n```\n")
556						}
557					} else if strings.TrimSpace(cleaned) != TodoWriteSuccessMessage && cleaned != "" {
558						// Skip TodoWrite success messages
559						// Blank line before code fence helps parsers inside <details> tags
560						markdown.WriteString("\n```text\n")
561						markdown.WriteString(cleaned)
562						markdown.WriteString("\n```\n")
563					}
564				}
565			}
566		}
567	
568		return markdown.String()
569	}
570
```

77	// GenerateAgentSession creates a SessionData from Codex CLI JSONL records
78	// The records should be in chronological order from the JSONL file
79	func GenerateAgentSession(records []map[string]interface{}, workspaceRoot string) (*SessionData, error) {
80		slog.Info("GenerateAgentSession: Starting", "recordCount", len(records))
81	
82		if len(records) == 0 {
83			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no records provided")
84		}
85	
86		// Extract session metadata from first record (session_meta)
87		sessionID, createdAt, cwd, err := extractSessionMetadata(records)
88		if err != nil {
89			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to extract session metadata: %w", err)
90		}
91	
92		// Use provided workspaceRoot or fall back to CWD from session metadata
93		if workspaceRoot == "" {
94			workspaceRoot = cwd
95		}
96	
97		slog.Info("GenerateAgentSession: Session metadata",
98			"sessionID", sessionID,
99			"createdAt", createdAt,
100			"workspaceRoot", workspaceRoot)
101	
102		// Build exchanges from records
103		exchanges, err := buildExchangesFromRecords(records, workspaceRoot)
104		if err != nil {
105			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to build exchanges: %w", err)
106		}
107	
108		// Assign exchangeId to each exchange (format: sessionId:index)
109		for i := range exchanges {
110			exchanges[i].ExchangeID = fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", sessionID, i)
111		}
112	
113		// Populate Summary and FormattedMarkdown for all tools
114		for i := range exchanges {
115			for j := range exchanges[i].Messages {
116				msg := &exchanges[i].Messages[j]
117				if msg.Tool != nil {
118					summary, formattedMd := formatToolWithSummary(msg.Tool, workspaceRoot)
119					if summary != "" {
120						msg.Tool.Summary = &summary
121					}
122					if formattedMd != "" {
123						msg.Tool.FormattedMarkdown = &formattedMd
124					}
125				}
126			}
127		}
128	
129		slog.Info("GenerateAgentSession: Built exchanges", "count", len(exchanges))
130	
131		sessionData := &SessionData{
132			SchemaVersion: "1.0",
133			Provider: ProviderInfo{
134				ID:      "codex-cli",
135				Name:    "Codex CLI",
136				Version: "unknown", // Codex doesn't currently include version in JSONL
137			},
138			SessionID:     sessionID,
139			CreatedAt:     createdAt,
140			WorkspaceRoot: workspaceRoot,
141			Exchanges:     exchanges,
142		}
143	
144		return sessionData, nil
145	}
146	
147	// extractSessionMetadata parses the session_meta record (first line)
148	func extractSessionMetadata(records []map[string]interface{}) (sessionID, createdAt, cwd string, err error) {
149		if len(records) == 0 {
150			return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("no records to extract metadata from")
151		}
152	
153		firstRecord := records[0]
154		recordType, ok := firstRecord["type"].(string)
155		if !ok || recordType != "session_meta" {
156			return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("first record is not session_meta: %v", recordType)
157		}
158	
159		payload, ok := firstRecord["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
160		if !ok {
161			return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("session_meta payload is missing or invalid")
162		}
163	
164		sessionID, _ = payload["id"].(string)
165		createdAt, _ = payload["timestamp"].(string)
166		cwd, _ = payload["cwd"].(string)
167	
168		if sessionID == "" {
169			return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("session_meta is missing session ID")
170		}
171	
172		if createdAt == "" {
173			// Fall back to record timestamp
174			createdAt, _ = firstRecord["timestamp"].(string)
175		}
176	
177		return sessionID, createdAt, cwd, nil
178	}
179	
180	// buildExchangesFromRecords groups JSONL records into exchanges
181	// An exchange starts with a user message and contains all subsequent agent responses and tool uses
182	func buildExchangesFromRecords(records []map[string]interface{}, workspaceRoot string) ([]Exchange, error) {
183		var exchanges []Exchange
184		var currentExchange *Exchange
185		var currentModel string
186		pendingTools := make(map[string]*PendingToolCall) // callID -> pending tool call
187	
188		for i, record := range records {
189			recordType, _ := record["type"].(string)
190			timestamp, _ := record["timestamp"].(string)
191	
192			switch recordType {
193			case "session_meta":
194				// Skip session metadata - already processed
195				continue
196	
197			case "turn_context":
198				// Update current model for subsequent agent messages
199				payload, ok := record["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
200				if ok {
201					if model, ok := payload["model"].(string); ok {
202						currentModel = model
203						slog.Debug("Updated current model", "model", currentModel)
204					}
205				}
206				continue
207	
208			case "event_msg":
209				// Handle user messages, agent messages, and agent reasoning
210				payload, ok := record["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
211				if !ok {
212					continue
213				}
214	
215				payloadType, _ := payload["type"].(string)
216	
217				switch payloadType {
218				case "user_message":
219					// Start a new exchange
220					message, ok := payload["message"].(string)
221					if !ok || message == "" {
222						continue
223					}
224	
225					// Save previous exchange if exists
226					if currentExchange != nil && len(currentExchange.Messages) > 0 {
227						exchanges = append(exchanges, *currentExchange)
228					}
229	
230					// Start new exchange
231					currentExchange = &Exchange{
232						StartTime: timestamp,
233						Messages:  []Message{},
234					}
235	
236					// Add user message
237					userMsg := Message{
238						ID:        fmt.Sprintf("u_%d", i),
239						Timestamp: timestamp,
240						Role:      "user",
241						Content: []ContentPart{
242							{Type: "text", Text: message},
243						},
244					}
245					currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, userMsg)
246	
247				case "agent_message":
248					// Agent text response - keep as separate message per user's requirement
249					message, ok := payload["message"].(string)
250					if !ok || message == "" {
251						continue
252					}
253	
254					if currentExchange == nil {
255						// Create exchange if missing (shouldn't happen)
256						currentExchange = &Exchange{
257							StartTime: timestamp,
258							Messages:  []Message{},
259						}
260					}
261	
262					agentMsg := Message{
263						ID:        fmt.Sprintf("a_%d", i),
264						Timestamp: timestamp,
265						Role:      "agent",
266						Model:     currentModel,
267						Content: []ContentPart{
268							{Type: "text", Text: message},
269						},
270					}
271					currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, agentMsg)
272					currentExchange.EndTime = timestamp
273	
274				case "agent_reasoning":
275					// Agent thinking - keep as separate message with thinking content type
276					text, ok := payload["text"].(string)
277					if !ok || text == "" {
278						continue
279					}
280	
281					if currentExchange == nil {
282						// Create exchange if missing (shouldn't happen)
283						currentExchange = &Exchange{
284							StartTime: timestamp,
285							Messages:  []Message{},
286						}
287					}
288	
289					reasoningMsg := Message{
290						ID:        fmt.Sprintf("r_%d", i),
291						Timestamp: timestamp,
292						Role:      "agent",
293						Model:     currentModel,
294						Content: []ContentPart{
295							{Type: "thinking", Text: text},
296						},
297					}
298					currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, reasoningMsg)
299					currentExchange.EndTime = timestamp
300	
301				case "token_count":
302					// Token usage event - attach to the most recent agent message
303					if currentExchange == nil || len(currentExchange.Messages) == 0 {
304						continue
305					}
306	
307					usage := extractUsageFromTokenCount(payload)
308					if usage == nil {
309						continue
310					}
311	
312					// Find the most recent agent message to attach usage to
313					for j := len(currentExchange.Messages) - 1; j >= 0; j-- {
314						if currentExchange.Messages[j].Role == "agent" {
315							currentExchange.Messages[j].Usage = usage
316							slog.Debug("Attached token usage to agent message",
317								"messageID", currentExchange.Messages[j].ID,
318								"inputTokens", usage.InputTokens,
319								"outputTokens", usage.OutputTokens,
320								"cachedInputTokens", usage.CachedInputTokens,
321								"reasoningOutputTokens", usage.ReasoningOutputTokens)
322							break
323						}
324					}
325				}
326	
327			case "response_item":
328				// Handle function calls and custom tool calls
329				payload, ok := record["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
330				if !ok {
331					continue
332				}
333	
334				payloadType, _ := payload["type"].(string)
335	
336				switch payloadType {
337				case "function_call":
338					// Function call (e.g., shell command)
339					if currentExchange == nil {
340						currentExchange = &Exchange{
341							StartTime: timestamp,
342							Messages:  []Message{},
343						}
344					}
345	
346					toolName, _ := payload["name"].(string)
347					argumentsJSON, _ := payload["arguments"].(string)
348					callID, _ := payload["call_id"].(string)
349	
350					if toolName == "" {
351						continue
352					}
353	
354					// Parse arguments to extract input
355					var input map[string]interface{}
356					if argumentsJSON != "" {
357						if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(argumentsJSON), &input); err != nil {
358							slog.Warn("Failed to parse function call arguments", "error", err, "arguments", argumentsJSON)
359							input = map[string]interface{}{"raw": argumentsJSON}
360						}
361					}
362	
363					// Create tool message
364					toolMsg := Message{
365						ID:        fmt.Sprintf("t_%d", i),
366						Timestamp: timestamp,
367						Role:      "agent",
368						Model:     currentModel,
369						Tool: &ToolInfo{
370							Name:   toolName,
371							Type:   classifyToolType(toolName),
372							UseID:  callID,
373							Input:  input,
374							Output: nil, // Will be filled when we find the output
375						},
376						PathHints: extractPathHints(toolName, input, "", workspaceRoot),
377					}
378	
379					currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, toolMsg)
380					currentExchange.EndTime = timestamp
381	
382					// Track this tool call to match with output later
383					if callID != "" {
384						pendingTools[callID] = &PendingToolCall{
385							message:   &currentExchange.Messages[len(currentExchange.Messages)-1],
386							toolInfo:  currentExchange.Messages[len(currentExchange.Messages)-1].Tool,
387							timestamp: timestamp,
388						}
389					}
390	
391				case "function_call_output":
392					// Function call output - merge into the pending tool call
393					callID, _ := payload["call_id"].(string)
394					outputJSON, _ := payload["output"].(string)
395	
396					if callID == "" || outputJSON == "" {
397						continue
398					}
399	
400					// Find the pending tool call
401					if pending, exists := pendingTools[callID]; exists {
402						// Parse the output JSON
403						var outputData map[string]interface{}
404						if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(outputJSON), &outputData); err == nil {
405							pending.toolInfo.Output = outputData
406						} else {
407							pending.toolInfo.Output = map[string]interface{}{"raw": outputJSON}
408						}
409						delete(pendingTools, callID)
410					}
411	
412				case "custom_tool_call":
413					// Custom tool call (e.g., apply_patch)
414					if currentExchange == nil {
415						currentExchange = &Exchange{
416							StartTime: timestamp,
417							Messages:  []Message{},
418						}
419					}
420	
421					toolName, _ := payload["name"].(string)
422					inputText, _ := payload["input"].(string)
423					callID, _ := payload["call_id"].(string)
424	
425					if toolName == "" {
426						continue
427					}
428	
429					// For custom tools, input is plain text, not JSON
430					input := map[string]interface{}{
431						"input": inputText,
432					}
433	
434					// Create tool message
435					toolMsg := Message{
436						ID:        fmt.Sprintf("ct_%d", i),
437						Timestamp: timestamp,
438						Role:      "agent",
439						Model:     currentModel,
440						Tool: &ToolInfo{
441							Name:   toolName,
442							Type:   classifyToolType(toolName),
443							UseID:  callID,
444							Input:  input,
445							Output: nil, // Will be filled when we find the output
446						},
447						PathHints: extractPathHints(toolName, input, inputText, workspaceRoot),
448					}
449	
450					currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, toolMsg)
451					currentExchange.EndTime = timestamp
452	
453					// Track this tool call to match with output later
454					if callID != "" {
455						pendingTools[callID] = &PendingToolCall{
456							message:   &currentExchange.Messages[len(currentExchange.Messages)-1],
457							toolInfo:  currentExchange.Messages[len(currentExchange.Messages)-1].Tool,
458							timestamp: timestamp,
459						}
460					}
461	
462				case "custom_tool_call_output":
463					// Custom tool call output - merge into the pending tool call
464					callID, _ := payload["call_id"].(string)
465					outputJSON, _ := payload["output"].(string)
466	
467					if callID == "" || outputJSON == "" {
468						continue
469					}
470	
471					// Find the pending tool call
472					if pending, exists := pendingTools[callID]; exists {
473						// Parse the output JSON
474						var outputData map[string]interface{}
475						if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(outputJSON), &outputData); err == nil {
476							pending.toolInfo.Output = outputData
477						} else {
478							pending.toolInfo.Output = map[string]interface{}{"raw": outputJSON}
479						}
480						delete(pendingTools, callID)
481					}
482				}
483			}
484		}
485	
486		// Add the last exchange if exists
487		if currentExchange != nil && len(currentExchange.Messages) > 0 {
488			exchanges = append(exchanges, *currentExchange)
489		}
490	
491		return exchanges, nil
492	}
493	
494	// formatToolWithSummary generates custom summary and formatted markdown for a Codex tool
495	// Returns (summary, formattedMarkdown) where summary is the custom content for <summary> tag
496	// and formattedMarkdown is additional content to display in the tool-use block
497	func formatToolWithSummary(tool *ToolInfo, workspaceRoot string) (string, string) {
498		var summary string
499		var formattedMd strings.Builder
500	
501		// Format tool input based on whether it's a function call or custom tool
502		if tool.Input != nil {
503			// Check if this is a custom tool (has string input in "input" field)
504			if inputStr, ok := tool.Input["input"].(string); ok {
505				// Custom tool with text input (e.g., apply_patch)
506				formattedMd.WriteString(formatCustomToolCall(tool.Name, inputStr))
507			} else if tool.Name == "shell_command" || tool.Name == "exec_command" {
508				// Shell command: normalize "cmd" → "command" for exec_command
509				toolInput := tool.Input
510				if _, ok := toolInput["cmd"]; ok {
511					if _, hasCommand := toolInput["command"]; !hasCommand {
512						toolInput["command"] = toolInput["cmd"]
513					}
514				}
515				inputJSON, _ := json.Marshal(toolInput)
516				shellSummary, shellBody := formatShellWithSummary(string(inputJSON))
517				if shellSummary != "" {
518					// Single-line command goes in summary
519					summary = fmt.Sprintf("Tool use: **%s** %s", tool.Name, shellSummary)
520				}
521				if shellBody != "" {
522					formattedMd.WriteString(shellBody)
523				}
524			} else {
525				// Other function calls with JSON arguments
526				inputJSON, _ := json.Marshal(tool.Input)
527				formattedMd.WriteString(formatToolCall(tool.Name, string(inputJSON)))
528			}
529		}
530	
531		// Format tool output if present
532		if tool.Output != nil {
533			if outputStr, ok := tool.Output["raw"].(string); ok {
534				// Clean and truncate output if needed
535				cleaned := strings.TrimSpace(outputStr)
536				// Only show output if there's actual content
537				if cleaned != "" {
538					if formattedMd.Len() > 0 {
539						formattedMd.WriteString("\n")
540					}
541					if len(cleaned) > 5000 {
542						cleaned = cleaned[:5000] + "\n... (truncated)"
543					}
544					formattedMd.WriteString("```\n" + cleaned + "\n```")
545				}
546			}
547		}
548	
549		return summary, strings.TrimSpace(formattedMd.String())
550	}
551	
552	// classifyToolType maps Codex tool names to standard tool types
553	func classifyToolType(toolName string) string {
554		switch toolName {
555		case "shell", "shell_command", "exec_command": // `shell` is legacy
556			return "shell"
557		case "update_plan":
558			return "task"
559		case "view_image":
560			return "read"
561		case "apply_patch":
562			return "write"
563		case "list_mcp_resources", "list_mcp_resource_templates", "read_mcp_resource":
564			return "generic"
565		default:
566			return "unknown"
567		}
568	}
569	
570	// extractPathHints extracts file paths from tool inputs.
571	// For apply_patch: parses patch format for file paths.
572	// For other tools: checks common path fields like "path", "file", etc.
573	func extractPathHints(toolName string, input map[string]interface{}, inputText string, workspaceRoot string) []string {
574		var paths []string
575	
576		if toolName == "apply_patch" {
577			// Parse patch format to extract file paths
578			// Look for lines like "*** Modify File: path", "*** Create File: path", "*** Delete File: path"
579			if inputText != "" {
580				paths = extractPathsFromPatch(inputText, workspaceRoot)
581			} else if inputStr, ok := input["input"].(string); ok {
582				paths = extractPathsFromPatch(inputStr, workspaceRoot)
583			}
584		} else {
585			// For other tools, check common path fields
586			pathFields := []string{"path", "file", "filename", "file_path"}
587			for _, field := range pathFields {
588				if value, ok := input[field].(string); ok && value != "" {
589					normalizedPath := spi.NormalizePath(value, workspaceRoot)
590					if !contains(paths, normalizedPath) {
591						paths = append(paths, normalizedPath)
592					}
593				}
594			}
595	
596			// Extract paths from shell commands (redirect targets, file-creating commands)
597			// exec_command uses "cmd", shell_command uses "command"
598			command, _ := input["command"].(string)
599			if command == "" {
600				command, _ = input["cmd"].(string)
601			}
602			if command != "" {
603				cwd, _ := input["workdir"].(string)
604				if cwd == "" {
605					cwd = workspaceRoot
606				}
607				shellPaths := spi.ExtractShellPathHints(command, cwd, workspaceRoot)
608				for _, sp := range shellPaths {
609					if !contains(paths, sp) {
610						paths = append(paths, sp)
611					}
612				}
613			}
614		}
615	
616		return paths
617	}
618	
619	// extractPathsFromPatch parses apply_patch input to find file paths
620	func extractPathsFromPatch(patchText string, workspaceRoot string) []string {
621		var paths []string
622		lines := strings.Split(patchText, "\n")
623	
624		for _, line := range lines {
625			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
626	
627			// Look for patch markers - both "Create File" and "Add File" are valid
628			markers := []string{
629				"*** Modify File:",
630				"*** Update File:",
631				"*** Create File:",
632				"*** Add File:",
633				"*** Delete File:",
634				"*** Rename File:",
635				"*** Remove File:",
636			}
637	
638			for _, marker := range markers {
639				if strings.HasPrefix(line, marker) {
640					path := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(line, marker))
641					if path != "" {
642						normalizedPath := spi.NormalizePath(path, workspaceRoot)
643						if !contains(paths, normalizedPath) {
644							paths = append(paths, normalizedPath)
645						}
646					}
647					break
648				}
649			}
650	
651			// For rename, also check for "*** New Name:" marker
652			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "*** New Name:") {
653				path := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(line, "*** New Name:"))
654				if path != "" {
655					normalizedPath := spi.NormalizePath(path, workspaceRoot)
656					if !contains(paths, normalizedPath) {
657						paths = append(paths, normalizedPath)
658					}
659				}
660			}
661		}
662	
663		return paths
664	}
665	
666	// contains checks if a string slice contains a value
667	func contains(slice []string, value string) bool {
668		for _, item := range slice {
669			if item == value {
670				return true
671			}
672		}
673		return false
674	}
675	
676	// extractUsageFromTokenCount extracts token usage from a token_count event's info.last_token_usage
677	// Codex CLI emits token_count events with the structure:
678	//
679	//	{
680	//	  "type": "event_msg",
681	//	  "payload": {
682	//	    "type": "token_count",
683	//	    "info": {
684	//	      "last_token_usage": { "input_tokens": N, "cached_input_tokens": N, "output_tokens": N, "reasoning_output_tokens": N, ... },
685	//	      "total_token_usage": { ... }
686	//	    }
687	//	  }
688	//	}
689	//
690	// We use last_token_usage for per-turn usage (total_token_usage is cumulative).
691	// Codex CLI uses its own token field names, stored in the Codex-specific fields of Usage.
692	func extractUsageFromTokenCount(payload map[string]interface{}) *Usage {
693		info, ok := payload["info"].(map[string]interface{})
694		if !ok {
695			return nil
696		}
697	
698		// Use last_token_usage for per-turn metrics (not cumulative)
699		lastUsage, ok := info["last_token_usage"].(map[string]interface{})
700		if !ok {
701			return nil
702		}
703	
704		return &Usage{
705			// Common fields
706			InputTokens:  schema.GetIntFromMap(lastUsage, "input_tokens"),
707			OutputTokens: schema.GetIntFromMap(lastUsage, "output_tokens"),
708			// Codex CLI specific fields
709			CachedInputTokens:     schema.GetIntFromMap(lastUsage, "cached_input_tokens"),
710			ReasoningOutputTokens: schema.GetIntFromMap(lastUsage, "reasoning_output_tokens"),
711		}
712	}

22	// getDefaultClaudeCommand returns the default claude command.
23	// It checks for installations in this order:
24	// 1. ~/.local/bin/claude (preferred native installation)
25	// 2. "claude" resolved from PATH
26	// 3. ~/.claude/local/claude (legacy npm installation)
27	// 4. "claude" bare fallback
28	func getDefaultClaudeCommand() string {
29		homeDir, homeDirErr := osUserHomeDir()
30		if homeDirErr == nil {
31			// Preferred native installation
32			nativeClaude := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".local", "bin", "claude")
33			if _, err := osStat(nativeClaude); err == nil {
34				slog.Info("Found Claude Code", "path", nativeClaude)
35				return nativeClaude
36			}
37		}
38	
39		// Check if claude is available on PATH
40		if path, err := execLookPath("claude"); err == nil {
41			slog.Info("Found Claude Code on PATH", "path", path)
42			return "claude"
43		}
44	
45		if homeDirErr == nil {
46			// Legacy npm installation
47			legacyClaude := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "local", "claude")
48			if _, err := osStat(legacyClaude); err == nil {
49				slog.Info("Found Claude Code", "path", legacyClaude)
50				return legacyClaude
51			}
52		}
53	
54		slog.Info("Using Claude Code bare fallback")
55		return "claude"
56	}

58	// expandTilde expands ~ to the user's home directory
59	func expandTilde(path string) string {
60		if strings.HasPrefix(path, "~/") {
61			homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
62			if err == nil {
63				return filepath.Join(homeDir, path[2:])
64			}
65		}
66		return path
67	}

69	// parseClaudeCommand parses a custom command string into executable and arguments.
70	// If customCommand is empty, returns the default command.
71	// If resumeSessionId is provided, appends "--resume <sessionId>" to the arguments.
72	// Supports quoted strings with spaces: claude --arg "value with spaces"
73	// Example: "claude --model gpt-4" returns ("claude", ["--model", "gpt-4"])
74	func parseClaudeCommand(customCommand string, resumeSessionId string) (string, []string) {
75		var cmd string
76		var args []string
77	
78		if customCommand == "" {
79			cmd = getDefaultClaudeCommand()
80			args = nil
81		} else {
82			parts := spi.SplitCommandLine(customCommand)
83			if len(parts) == 0 {
84				cmd = getDefaultClaudeCommand()
85				args = nil
86			} else {
87				// Expand tilde in the command path
88				cmd = expandTilde(parts[0])
89				args = parts[1:]
90			}
91		}
92	
93		// Append --resume flag if sessionId is provided
94		if resumeSessionId != "" {
95			args = append(args, "--resume", resumeSessionId)
96		}
97	
98		return cmd, args
99	}
100	
101	// ExecuteClaude runs the Claude CLI with the given arguments
102	func ExecuteClaude(customCommand string, resumeSessionId string) error {
103		// Parse the command and any custom arguments
104		claudeCmd, customArgs := parseClaudeCommand(customCommand, resumeSessionId)
105	
106		// Log if resuming a session
107		if resumeSessionId != "" {
108			slog.Info("ExecuteClaude: Resuming session", "sessionId", resumeSessionId)
109		}
110	
111		// Create the command
112		cmd := exec.Command(claudeCmd, customArgs...)
113	
114		// Set up stdin/stdout/stderr to match the parent process
115		cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
116		cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
117		cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
118	
119		// Start the command
120		slog.Info("ExecuteClaude: Starting Claude Code process")
121		if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
122			return fmt.Errorf("failed to start claude: %v", err)
123		}
124	
125		// Wait for the command to complete
126		slog.Info("ExecuteClaude: Waiting for Claude Code to exit")
127		if err := cmd.Wait(); err != nil {
128			// Don't return error if the command exited with a non-zero status
129			// This is normal for many CLI applications
130			if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
131				exitCode := exitErr.ExitCode()
132				slog.Info("ExecuteClaude: Claude Code exited", "exitCode", exitCode)
133				os.Exit(exitCode)
134			}
135			return fmt.Errorf("claude execution failed: %v", err)
136		}
137	
138		slog.Info("ExecuteClaude: Claude Code exited normally", "exitCode", 0)
139		return nil
140	}

26	// parseCodexCommand splits a custom command string into the binary path and its arguments.
27	// An empty custom command falls back to the detected default binary.
28	// Supports quoted strings with spaces: codex --arg "value with spaces"
29	func parseCodexCommand(customCommand string) (string, []string) {

31			parts := spi.SplitCommandLine(customCommand)
32			if len(parts) > 0 {
33				return expandTilde(parts[0]), parts[1:]
34			}

40	// getDefaultCodexCommand looks for the codex binary in common installation locations.
41	func getDefaultCodexCommand() string {
42		if path, ok := findHomebrewCodex(); ok {
43			return path
44		}
45	
46		if path, ok := findNpmCodex(); ok {
47			return path
48		}
49	
50		return "codex"

53	// findHomebrewCodex returns the codex binary path from Homebrew if available.
54	func findHomebrewCodex() (string, bool) {
55		// Respect HOMEBREW_PREFIX first if set.
56		if prefix := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("HOMEBREW_PREFIX")); prefix != "" {
57			candidate := filepath.Join(prefix, "bin", "codex")
58			if isExecutable(candidate) {
59				slog.Debug("Codex CLI: Found Homebrew binary via HOMEBREW_PREFIX", "path", candidate)
60				return candidate, true
61			}
62		}
63	
64		brewPath, err := execLookPath("brew")
65		if err == nil {
66			var out bytes.Buffer
67			cmd := exec.Command(brewPath, "--prefix")
68			cmd.Stdout = &out
69			cmd.Stderr = &out
70			if err := cmd.Run(); err == nil {
71				prefix := strings.TrimSpace(out.String())
72				if prefix != "" {
73					candidate := filepath.Join(prefix, "bin", "codex")
74					if isExecutable(candidate) {
75						slog.Debug("Codex CLI: Found Homebrew binary via brew --prefix", "path", candidate)
76						return candidate, true
77					}
78				}
79			}
80		}
81	
82		// Fallback to the default Homebrew prefix on Apple Silicon if brew detection failed.
83		defaultPath := "/opt/homebrew/bin/codex"
84		if isExecutable(defaultPath) {
85			slog.Debug("Codex CLI: Found Homebrew binary via default path", "path", defaultPath)
86			return defaultPath, true
87		}
88	
89		return "", false
90	}

92	// findNpmCodex returns the codex binary from global npm locations if present.
93	func findNpmCodex() (string, bool) {
94		// Prefer explicit NVM_BIN if it points to the codex executable.
95		if nvmBin := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("NVM_BIN")); nvmBin != "" {
96			candidate := filepath.Join(nvmBin, "codex")
97			if isExecutable(candidate) {
98				slog.Debug("Codex CLI: Found npm binary via NVM_BIN", "path", candidate)
99				return candidate, true
100			}
101		}
102	
103		npmPath, err := execLookPath("npm")
104		if err == nil {
105			var out bytes.Buffer
106			cmd := exec.Command(npmPath, "bin", "-g")
107			cmd.Stdout = &out
108			cmd.Stderr = &out
109			if err := cmd.Run(); err == nil {
110				binDir := strings.TrimSpace(out.String())
111				if binDir != "" {
112					candidate := filepath.Join(binDir, "codex")
113					if isExecutable(candidate) {
114						slog.Debug("Codex CLI: Found npm binary via npm bin -g", "path", candidate)
115						return candidate, true
116					}
117				}
118			}
119		}
120	
121		// Fallback to common NVM layout if we know NVM_DIR.
122		if nvmDir := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv("NVM_DIR")); nvmDir != "" {
123			candidate := filepath.Join(nvmDir, "versions", "node", "current", "bin", "codex")
124			if isExecutable(candidate) {
125				slog.Debug("Codex CLI: Found npm binary via NVM_DIR fallback", "path", candidate)
126				return candidate, true
127			}
128		}
129	
130		return "", false
131	}

133	// expandTilde expands a leading tilde in a path to the user's home directory.
134	func expandTilde(path string) string {
135		if path == "" || path[0] != '~' {
136			return path
137		}
138	
139		home, err := osUserHomeDir()
140		if err != nil {
141			return path
142		}
143	
144		if path == "~" {
145			return home
146		}
147	
148		// Handle both Unix-style (~/) and Windows-style (~\) paths even though we don't support Windows.
149		// This check exists for defensive programming but won't be exercised in practice.
150		if len(path) >= 2 && (path[1] == '/' || path[1] == '\\') {
151			return filepath.Join(home, path[2:])
152		}
153	
154		return path
155	}
156	
157	// isExecutable returns true if the file exists and has execute permissions.
158	func isExecutable(path string) bool {
159		info, err := osStat(path)
160		if err != nil {
161			return false
162		}
163	
164		return !info.IsDir() && info.Mode()&0o111 != 0
165	}
166	
167	// runCodexVersionCommand tries common version flags and returns the first successful output.
168	func runCodexVersionCommand(command string) (string, string, string, error) {
169		flags := []string{"--version", "-V"}
170		var lastErr error
171		var lastStderr string
172	
173		for idx, flag := range flags {
174			var stdout bytes.Buffer
175			var stderr bytes.Buffer
176	
177			cmd := exec.Command(command, flag)
178			cmd.Stdout = &stdout
179			cmd.Stderr = &stderr
180	
181			err := cmd.Run()
182			stderrStr := strings.TrimSpace(stderr.String())
183			if err != nil {
184				lastErr = err
185				lastStderr = stderrStr
186	
187				// For fatal errors (binary missing/permission issues) or last attempt, stop immediately.
188				if classifyCheckError(err) != "unknown" || idx == len(flags)-1 {
189					return "", flag, lastStderr, err
190				}
191				continue
192			}
193	
194			output := strings.TrimSpace(stdout.String())
195			if output == "" {
196				return "", flag, stderrStr, errNoVersionOutput
197			}
198	
199			return output, flag, stderrStr, nil
200		}
201	
202		if lastErr != nil {
203			return "", flags[len(flags)-1], lastStderr, lastErr
204		}
205	
206		return "", "", lastStderr, errors.New("failed to execute codex version command")
207	}
208	
209	// classifyCheckError buckets common error categories for user guidance and analytics.
210	func classifyCheckError(err error) string {
211		if err == nil {
212			return ""
213		}
214	
215		var execErr *exec.Error
216		if errors.As(err, &execErr) && execErr.Err == exec.ErrNotFound {
217			return "not_found"
218		}
219	
220		var pathErr *os.PathError
221		if errors.As(err, &pathErr) {
222			if errors.Is(pathErr.Err, os.ErrNotExist) {
223				return "not_found"
224			}
225			if errors.Is(pathErr.Err, os.ErrPermission) {
226				return "permission_denied"
227			}
228		}
229	
230		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrPermission) {
231			return "permission_denied"
232		}
233	
234		if errors.Is(err, errNoVersionOutput) {
235			return "no_output"
236		}
237	
238		return "unknown"
239	}
240	
241	// ExecuteCodex executes the Codex CLI in interactive mode and blocks until it exits.
242	// The customCommand parameter specifies the codex binary and optional arguments to use.
243	// If customCommand is empty, falls back to the detected default codex binary.
244	// If resumeSessionID is provided, runs "codex resume <sessionId>" to continue that session.
245	// Otherwise, starts a new codex session.
246	func ExecuteCodex(customCommand string, resumeSessionID string) error {
247		var cmd *exec.Cmd
248	
249		if resumeSessionID != "" {
250			// Parse custom command to get binary and args
251			codexCmd, customArgs := parseCodexCommand(customCommand)
252	
253			// Build args: custom args + resume subcommand + sessionID
254			args := append(customArgs, "resume", resumeSessionID)
255	
256			slog.Info("ExecuteCodex: Resuming Codex session",
257				"command", codexCmd,
258				"sessionID", resumeSessionID,
259				"customArgs", customArgs)
260			cmd = exec.Command(codexCmd, args...)
261		} else {
262			// Parse the command and get binary + args
263			codexCmd, args := parseCodexCommand(customCommand)
264	
265			slog.Info("ExecuteCodex: Starting new Codex session",
266				"command", codexCmd,
267				"args", args)
268			cmd = exec.Command(codexCmd, args...)
269		}
270	
271		// Configure interactive mode - connect to user's terminal
272		cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
273		cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
274		cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
275	
276		// Run the command and wait for it to complete
277		slog.Info("ExecuteCodex: Executing Codex CLI (blocking until exit)")
278		if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
279			slog.Error("ExecuteCodex: Codex execution failed", "error", err)
280			return fmt.Errorf("codex execution failed: %w", err)
281		}
282	
283		slog.Info("ExecuteCodex: Codex CLI exited successfully")
284		return nil
285	}
286

1	package claudecode

11	
12	// GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir returns the path to the Claude Code projects directory
13	func GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir() (string, error) {
14		// Get user's home directory
15		homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
16		if err != nil {
17			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
18		}
19	
20		// Construct the path to .claude/projects
21		projectsDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "projects")
22	
23		// Check if the directory exists
24		if _, err := os.Stat(projectsDir); err != nil {
25			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
26				return "", fmt.Errorf("claude projects directory not found at %s", projectsDir)
27			}
28			return "", fmt.Errorf("error checking Claude projects directory: %v", err)
29		}
30	
31		return projectsDir, nil
32	}
33	
34	// GetClaudeCodeProjectDir returns the Claude Code project directory for the given path.
35	// If no path is provided (empty string), it uses the current working directory.
36	// It resolves any symlinks in the path to match Claude Code's behavior, which uses the real path
37	// when creating project directories. Claude Code requires a specific naming convention to map
38	// working directories to project folders: each non-alphanumeric character is replaced with a dash
39	// to create a unique, filesystem-safe identifier
40	func GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
41		// Use provided path or get current working directory
42		var cwd string
43		var err error
44		if projectPath == "" {
45			cwd, err = os.Getwd()
46			if err != nil {
47				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
48			}
49		} else {
50			cwd = projectPath
51		}
52	
53		// Resolve any symlinks in the path to match Claude Code's behavior
54		// Claude Code uses the real path when creating project directories, so
55		// if the user's working directory contains symlinks, we need to resolve
56		// them to find the correct project directory in ~/.claude/projects/
57		realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cwd)
58		if err != nil {
59			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve symlinks in working directory: %v", err)
60		}
61	
62		// Log if the path was changed by symlink resolution
63		if realPath != cwd {
64			slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Resolved symlinks in working directory",
65				"original", cwd,
66				"resolved", realPath)
67		}
68	
69		// Convert path to project directory format using regex
70		// Replace anything that's not alphanumeric or dash with a dash (matching Claude Code's behavior)
71		// Example: "/Users/sean/My Projects(1)/app" becomes "-Users-sean-My-Projects-1--app"
72		reg := regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9-]`)
73		projectDirName := reg.ReplaceAllString(realPath, "-")
74	
75		// Add leading dash if not already there
76		if !strings.HasPrefix(projectDirName, "-") {
77			projectDirName = "-" + projectDirName
78		}
79	
80		// Log the transformation for debugging path issues
81		slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Transformed working directory to project name",
82			"cwd", cwd,
83			"projectDirName", projectDirName)
84	
85		// Get Claude projects directory
86		projectsDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir()
87		if err != nil {
88			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get Claude projects directory: %v", err)
89		}
90	
91		fullProjectPath := filepath.Join(projectsDir, projectDirName)
92		slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Constructed full project path",
93			"projectPath", fullProjectPath)
94	
95		return fullProjectPath, nil
96	}
97

15	// normalizeCodexPath resolves a path to an absolute, cleaned representation suitable for comparisons.
16	func normalizeCodexPath(path string) string {
17		if strings.TrimSpace(path) == "" {
18			return ""
19		}
20	
21		cleaned := filepath.Clean(path)
22	
23		absPath, err := filepath.Abs(cleaned)
24		if err == nil {
25			cleaned = absPath
26		}
27	
28		if resolved, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cleaned); err == nil {
29			cleaned = resolved
30		}
31	
32		return filepath.Clean(cleaned)
33	}
34	
35	// readDirSortedDesc reads directory entries and sorts them descending by name.
36	func readDirSortedDesc(path string) ([]os.DirEntry, error) {
37		entries, err := os.ReadDir(path)
38		if err != nil {
39			return nil, err
40		}
41	
42		sort.Slice(entries, func(i, j int) bool {
43			return entries[i].Name() > entries[j].Name()
44		})
45	
46		return entries, nil
47	}

I now have the full picture of both the forward conversions and the native formats. Before I lay out decisions, here's the key finding that reframes this task — because it changes what "reverse the flow" actually means in practice.

## The central insight: the reverse path is *not* a mirror of the forward path

The forward parsers read a **different projection** of each native file than the agent needs to *resume*. So reconstruction can't just "run the parser backwards" — it has to synthesize records the forward path never even looked at.

**Codex.** The forward parser (`buildExchangesFromRecords`) builds `SessionData` from the `event_msg` stream — the UI event log (`user_message`, `agent_message`, `agent_reasoning`). But `codex resume` replays the **`response_item`** stream — the actual model-facing transcript (`{type:"message", role, content:[{type:"input_text"/"output_text"}]}` plus `function_call`/`function_call_output` pairs). I confirmed both streams coexist in every rollout file. To make a resumable Codex file we must regenerate the `response_item` transcript from `SessionData`, which the parser mostly ignores — including the leading `<environment_context>` block and `call_id`-matched tool pairs.

**Claude.** The forward parser collapses the `parentUuid` linked-list into linear exchanges and keeps only the record `uuid` as `Message.ID` — it drops the API `message.id`, the `parentUuid` chain, `gitBranch`/`version`, and (critically) **thinking-block signatures**. `claude --resume` needs a valid `parentUuid` chain and API-shaped messages; and replaying unsigned `thinking` blocks back to the Anthropic API gets rejected. So reconstructed thinking has to be dropped or downgraded to text.

**Consequence:** combined with your three answers — cross-provider from day one, "gist" fidelity, resumable-into-live-store — faithful *structural* tool replay across providers is essentially intractable (a Claude `Edit` tool_use has no clean `apply_patch` equivalent, model names and usage are meaningless to the other agent, etc.). The pragmatic, robust target is a **flattened transcript in the target's native shape**: clean user/agent text turns, synthesized linear chain + IDs, tool activity reduced to readable text context. The agent only needs the gist as context for its next request — exactly your fidelity bar — and this is the only approach that avoids API-rejection risk on a foreign transcript.

## Proposed architecture

1. **New optional capability interface** (not added to the main `Provider` — that would force stubs on the other 4 providers):
   ```
   spi.SessionReconstructor interface {
       ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts ReconstructOptions) (*ReconstructedSession, error)
   }
   ```
   `ReconstructSession` is a **pure transform** (SessionData → native bytes + new native session ID + suggested filename), no filesystem — trivially unit-testable, and the natural home for a round-trip test. Only `claudecode` and `codexcli` implement it initially; consumers use a type assertion.

2. **`specstory resume` orchestrates** (reuses everything that already exists): obtain `SessionData` (from SpecStory Cloud, or a local `.specstory` artifact) → `target.ReconstructSession(...)` → write the native file into the live store using the *existing* path derivation (`GetClaudeCodeProjectDir` keyed off the **current** cwd, or `codexSessionsRoot` + today's date dir) → call the existing `ExecAgentAndWatch(resumeSessionID: newID)`. The "mutate another app's store" surface stays scoped to exactly the one session being resumed, as you wanted.

3. **Cross-provider mapping policy** (the fundamentals you said to hit now): model name → dropped (target agent picks its own); usage → dropped; thinking/reasoning → dropped or flattened to text; tool calls → flattened to a short textual note (`Edited pkg/foo.go`, `Ran: go test ./...`); source agent's system/`environment_context` preamble → **not** copied (target injects its own), optionally replaced by a one-line migration note so the agent understands the prior turns are imported context.

## The biggest risk

Whether a hand-synthesized file is actually *accepted* by `claude --resume` / `codex resume` is unverified and unverifiable from the code alone — it depends on each agent's loader strictness (required fields, chain integrity, signature handling). I'd de-risk this with a tiny spike **before** building the converter.

Here are the decisions I need from you to lock the plan:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-15 20:10:38Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**How should agent tool activity (Edit/Write/Bash, apply_patch/shell, and their outputs) be represented in the reconstructed session?**

Options:
- **Flatten to text notes** - Each tool call becomes a short textual line in the agent turn ("Edited foo.go", "Ran: go test"). No native tool_use/tool_result structures. Most robust, identical cross-provider, zero API-rejection risk. Matches 'gist' fidelity.
- **Native structural replay** - Reproduce real tool_use/tool_result (or function_call) pairs in native format for same-provider; flatten for cross-provider. Higher fidelity for machine-portability but much more work and fragile against agent loader strictness.
- **Drop tool activity** - Keep only user + agent prose turns. Simplest, but the agent loses context about what files changed / commands ran, which weakens resume quality.

**Should we validate that a synthesized file actually resumes before building the full converter?**

Options:
- **Spike resume acceptance first** - Hand-craft one minimal synthesized file per agent, confirm `claude --resume <id>` and `codex resume <id>` actually load it and continue the conversation. De-risks the #1 unknown before investing in the converter.
- **Build converter first** - Build ReconstructSession for both providers, validate resume acceptance at the end. Faster to a 'complete' artifact but risks discovering a loader-strictness blocker late.

**What session ID should the reconstructed native file carry?**

Options:
- **Mint new native-format ID** - Generate a fresh ID in the target's format (UUIDv4 for Claude, UUIDv7-style for Codex) and store the original session ID as a back-reference in metadata. Avoids collisions, clean provenance link.
- **Preserve original ID** - Reuse the source session's ID verbatim. Nice for idempotency/dedup, but risks collisions in the target store and may be a wrong-format ID for the target agent.

That's a clean refinement, and it's better than what I proposed — because the markdown rendition of each tool call **already exists** in `SessionData`. Each `ToolInfo` already carries `FormattedMarkdown` (and Codex also fills `Summary`), populated by `formatToolAsMarkdown`/`formatToolWithSummary` during the forward pass. So reconstruction doesn't re-render anything: a tool message just becomes an agent text message whose body is that pre-rendered markdown. Reconstructed sessions end up with only user turns and agent turns, tool activity living inside the agent turns as prose. Elegant, and it makes the conversion almost entirely provider-agnostic.

38	// syncCategory represents the category a session was counted in
39	type syncCategory string

42		categorySkipped syncCategory = "skipped" // Lowest precedence
43		categoryErrored syncCategory = "errored" // Replaces skipped
44		categoryUpdated syncCategory = "updated" // Replaces skipped and errored
45		categoryCreated syncCategory = "created" // Highest precedence, never replaced

103	// CloudSyncStats tracks the results of cloud sync operations
104	type CloudSyncStats struct {
105		SessionsAttempted int32    // Total sessions that started sync attempt
106		SessionsSkipped   int32    // Already up to date on server
107		SessionsUpdated   int32    // Existed but needed update
108		SessionsCreated   int32    // New sessions created
109		SessionsErrored   int32    // Failed with non-2xx response
110		SessionsTimedOut  int32    // Timed out waiting for overall sync completion (calculated at shutdown)
111		countedSessions   sync.Map // Maps session ID -> syncCategory (tracks which category each session is in)
112	}
113	
114	// calculateTimedOut computes the number of sessions that timed out during sync.
115	// This is calculated as: attempted - (skipped + updated + created + errored)
116	// Includes defensive validation to prevent negative values in case of counter bugs.
117	func (stats *CloudSyncStats) calculateTimedOut() {
118		finished := stats.SessionsSkipped + stats.SessionsUpdated + stats.SessionsCreated + stats.SessionsErrored
119	
120		// Defensive validation: finished should never exceed attempted
121		if finished > stats.SessionsAttempted {
122			slog.Error("CloudSyncStats counter inconsistency detected",
123				"attempted", stats.SessionsAttempted,
124				"finished", finished,
125				"skipped", stats.SessionsSkipped,
126				"updated", stats.SessionsUpdated,
127				"created", stats.SessionsCreated,
128				"errored", stats.SessionsErrored)
129			stats.SessionsTimedOut = 0
130			return
131		}
132	
133		stats.SessionsTimedOut = stats.SessionsAttempted - finished
134	}

166	// incrementCategory atomically increments the counter for a given category
167	func (stats *CloudSyncStats) incrementCategory(category syncCategory) {
168		switch category {
169		case categorySkipped:
170			atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsSkipped, 1)
171		case categoryErrored:
172			atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsErrored, 1)
173		case categoryUpdated:
174			atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsUpdated, 1)
175		case categoryCreated:
176			atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsCreated, 1)
177		}
178	}
179	
180	// decrementCategory atomically decrements the counter for a given category
181	func (stats *CloudSyncStats) decrementCategory(category syncCategory) {
182		switch category {
183		case categorySkipped:
184			atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsSkipped, -1)
185		case categoryErrored:
186			atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsErrored, -1)
187		case categoryUpdated:
188			atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsUpdated, -1)
189		case categoryCreated:
190			atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsCreated, -1)
191		}
192	}
193	
194	// shouldReplaceCategory determines if newCategory should replace oldCategory based on precedence
195	// Precedence (highest to lowest): created > updated > errored > skipped
196	func shouldReplaceCategory(oldCategory, newCategory syncCategory) bool {
197		// Created never gets replaced
198		if oldCategory == categoryCreated {
199			return false
200		}
201		// Updated replaces skipped and errored
202		if newCategory == categoryUpdated {
203			return oldCategory == categorySkipped || oldCategory == categoryErrored
204		}
205		// Errored replaces only skipped
206		if newCategory == categoryErrored {
207			return oldCategory == categorySkipped
208		}
209		// Created replaces everything (but we already handled oldCategory == created above)
210		if newCategory == categoryCreated {
211			return true
212		}
213		// Skipped doesn't replace anything
214		return false
215	}
216	
217	// trackSessionInCategory records a session in a category and updates counters atomically
218	// Handles precedence rules and automatically increments/decrements appropriate counters
219	func (stats *CloudSyncStats) trackSessionInCategory(sessionID string, newCategory syncCategory) {
220		for {
221			// Try to load existing category
222			existing, loaded := stats.countedSessions.Load(sessionID)
223	
224			if !loaded {
225				// No existing entry, try to store
226				actual, loaded := stats.countedSessions.LoadOrStore(sessionID, newCategory)
227				if !loaded {
228					// Successfully stored new category - increment counter
229					stats.incrementCategory(newCategory)
230					return
231				}
232				// Someone else stored it first, loop to check precedence
233				existing = actual
234			}
235	
236			oldCategory := existing.(syncCategory)
237	
238			// Check if we should replace the old category
239			if !shouldReplaceCategory(oldCategory, newCategory) {
240				// Don't replace - higher precedence category already exists
241				return
242			}
243	
244			// Try to replace old category with new category
245			if stats.countedSessions.CompareAndSwap(sessionID, oldCategory, newCategory) {
246				// Successfully replaced - decrement old counter and increment new counter
247				stats.decrementCategory(oldCategory)
248				stats.incrementCategory(newCategory)
249				return
250			}
251			// CompareAndSwap failed, someone else modified it, loop again
252		}
253	}

255	// acquireHTTPSemaphore acquires the HTTP semaphore (blocks until available)
256	// Returns a release function that should be called with defer
257	//
258	// No timeout - blocks indefinitely until semaphore is available. Goroutines that don't acquire
259	// before the overall sync timeout (CloudSyncTimeout) are orphaned, but this is acceptable because
260	// the process terminates after sync timeout. The overall timeout handles cleanup and stats reporting.
261	func (syncMgr *SyncManager) acquireHTTPSemaphore(sessionID, requestType string) (releaseFunc func()) {
262		// Block until semaphore slot is available
263		syncMgr.httpSemaphore <- struct{}{}
264	
265		slog.Debug("Acquired HTTP semaphore",
266			"sessionId", sessionID,
267			"requestType", requestType,
268			"currentConcurrency", len(syncMgr.httpSemaphore))
269	
270		releaseFunc = func() {
271			<-syncMgr.httpSemaphore
272			slog.Debug("Released HTTP semaphore",
273				"sessionId", sessionID,
274				"requestType", requestType,
275				"currentConcurrency", len(syncMgr.httpSemaphore))
276		}
277		return releaseFunc
278	}

280	// fetchBulkSessionSizes fetches markdown sizes for all sessions in a project from the SpecStory Cloud API.
281	// This enables batch sync optimization by avoiding individual HEAD requests for each session.
282	//
283	// The function makes a GET request to /api/v1/projects/{projectID}/sessions/sizes with automatic
284	// retry logic (3 attempts with 1 second delay between retries) to handle transient network failures.
285	// This function is only called once during batch sync initialization, so no HTTP semaphore is needed.
286	//
287	// Parameters:
288	//   - projectID: The project identifier (git_id, workspace_id, or directory name)
289	//
290	// Returns:
291	//   - map[string]int: Map of sessionID to markdown content size in bytes. Empty map if project has no sessions.
292	//   - error: Non-nil if all retry attempts fail, including request creation, network, parsing, or API errors.
293	//
294	// The function logs at Debug level for normal operation and Error level for failures. On success, it logs
295	// at Info level with the count of sessions fetched.
296	func (syncMgr *SyncManager) fetchBulkSessionSizes(projectID string) (map[string]int, error) {
297		const maxAttempts = 3
298		const retryDelay = 1 * time.Second
299	
300		apiURL := GetAPIBaseURL() + "/api/v1/projects/" + projectID + "/sessions/sizes"
301	
302		for attempt := 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++ {
303			// Log attempt
304			if attempt == 1 {
305				slog.Debug("Fetching bulk session sizes",
306					"projectId", projectID,
307					"url", apiURL,
308					"attempt", attempt)
309			} else {
310				slog.Debug("Retrying bulk session sizes fetch",
311					"projectId", projectID,
312					"url", apiURL,
313					"attempt", attempt)
314			}
315	
316			// Make GET request
317			req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", apiURL, nil)
318			if err != nil {
319				slog.Error("Failed to create GET request for bulk sizes",
320					"projectId", projectID,
321					"attempt", attempt,
322					"error", err)
323				if attempt < maxAttempts {
324					time.Sleep(retryDelay)
325					continue
326				}
327				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create request after %d attempts: %w", maxAttempts, err)
328			}
329	
330			// Add auth headers
331			cloudToken := GetCloudToken()
332			req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+cloudToken)
333			req.Header.Set("User-Agent", GetUserAgent())
334	
335			// Create HTTP client with timeout
336			client := &http.Client{
337				Timeout: 30 * time.Second,
338			}
339	
340			// Execute request
341			resp, err := client.Do(req)
342	
343			if err != nil {
344				slog.Debug("Bulk sizes GET request failed",
345					"projectId", projectID,
346					"attempt", attempt,
347					"error", err)
348				if attempt < maxAttempts {
349					time.Sleep(retryDelay)
350					continue
351				}
352				return nil, fmt.Errorf("GET request failed after %d attempts: %w", maxAttempts, err)
353			}
354	
355			// Read response body
356			defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }()
357			respBody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
358			if err != nil {
359				slog.Error("Failed to read bulk sizes response body",
360					"projectId", projectID,
361					"attempt", attempt,
362					"status", resp.StatusCode,
363					"error", err)
364				if attempt < maxAttempts {
365					time.Sleep(retryDelay)
366					continue
367				}
368				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read response body after %d attempts: %w", maxAttempts, err)
369			}
370	
371			// Check status code
372			if resp.StatusCode != 200 {
373				slog.Debug("Bulk sizes GET returned non-200 status",
374					"projectId", projectID,
375					"attempt", attempt,
376					"status", resp.StatusCode,
377					"responseBody", string(respBody))
378				if attempt < maxAttempts {
379					time.Sleep(retryDelay)
380					continue
381				}
382				return nil, fmt.Errorf("GET request returned status %d after %d attempts", resp.StatusCode, maxAttempts)
383			}
384	
385			// Parse JSON response
386			var bulkResponse BulkSizesResponse
387			err = json.Unmarshal(respBody, &bulkResponse)
388			if err != nil {
389				slog.Error("Failed to parse bulk sizes JSON response",
390					"projectId", projectID,
391					"attempt", attempt,
392					"error", err,
393					"responseBody", string(respBody))
394				if attempt < maxAttempts {
395					time.Sleep(retryDelay)
396					continue
397				}
398				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse JSON after %d attempts: %w", maxAttempts, err)
399			}
400	
401			// Check success field
402			if !bulkResponse.Success {
403				slog.Warn("Bulk sizes response indicated failure",
404					"projectId", projectID,
405					"attempt", attempt)
406				if attempt < maxAttempts {
407					time.Sleep(retryDelay)
408					continue
409				}
410				return nil, fmt.Errorf("API returned success=false after %d attempts", maxAttempts)
411			}
412	
413			// Success - log and return
414			sessionCount := len(bulkResponse.Data.Sessions)
415			slog.Info("Fetched bulk session sizes",
416				"projectId", projectID,
417				"sessionCount", sessionCount)
418			return bulkResponse.Data.Sessions, nil
419		}
420	
421		// Should never reach here, but just in case
422		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch bulk sizes after %d attempts", maxAttempts)
423	}

425	// PreloadSessionSizes fetches and caches session sizes for all sessions in a project
426	// This is a blocking call that should be made before syncing multiple sessions
427	// If fetch fails, logs warning and leaves cache nil (enables HEAD request fallback)
428	func (syncMgr *SyncManager) PreloadSessionSizes(projectID string) {
429		if syncMgr == nil {
430			slog.Warn("PreloadSessionSizes called with nil sync manager")
431			return
432		}
433	
434		slog.Debug("Preloading session sizes for batch sync",
435			"projectId", projectID)
436	
437		// Fetch bulk sizes (with retry logic)
438		sizes, err := syncMgr.fetchBulkSessionSizes(projectID)
439		if err != nil {
440			// Log error and leave cache nil to trigger HEAD request fallback
441			slog.Error("Failed to fetch bulk session sizes, will fall back to HEAD requests",
442				"projectId", projectID,
443				"error", err)
444			return
445		}
446	
447		// Store in cache with write lock
448		syncMgr.bulkSizesMu.Lock()
449		defer syncMgr.bulkSizesMu.Unlock()
450		syncMgr.bulkSizes = sizes
451	
452		sessionCount := len(sizes)
453		slog.Info("Preloaded session sizes from server",
454			"projectId", projectID,
455			"sessionCount", sessionCount)
456	}
