458	// requiresSync checks if a session needs to be synced to the cloud
459	// Returns true if sync is needed, false if already up-to-date
460	func (syncMgr *SyncManager) requiresSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent, projectID string, skipHeadCheck bool) (bool, error) {
461		// Skip HEAD check if requested (caller determined sync is needed)
462		if skipHeadCheck {
463			slog.Debug("Skipping HEAD check (skipHeadCheck=true)",
464				"sessionId", sessionID)
465			return true, nil
466		}
467	
468		// Get size from passed content
469		localSize := len(mdContent)
470	
471		// Check if bulk sizes are preloaded (batch sync mode)
472		syncMgr.bulkSizesMu.RLock()
473		bulkSizes := syncMgr.bulkSizes
474		syncMgr.bulkSizesMu.RUnlock()
475	
476		if bulkSizes != nil {
477			// Bulk sizes are available - use cached data instead of HEAD request
478			serverSize, exists := bulkSizes[sessionID]
479			if !exists {
480				// Session not in bulk sizes map - doesn't exist on server yet
481				slog.Debug("Using preloaded size for sync check: session not on server",
482					"sessionId", sessionID,
483					"localSize", localSize)
484				return true, nil
485			}
486	
487			// Compare sizes
488			needsSync := localSize > serverSize
489			if needsSync {
490				slog.Debug("Using preloaded size for sync check: local is larger",
491					"sessionId", sessionID,
492					"localSize", localSize,
493					"serverSize", serverSize)
494			} else {
495				slog.Info("Session already up-to-date on server (using preloaded sizes), skipping sync",
496					"sessionId", sessionID,
497					"localSize", localSize,
498					"serverSize", serverSize)
499			}
500			return needsSync, nil
501		}
502	
503		// Bulk sizes not available - fall back to HEAD request
504		slog.Debug("Using HEAD request for sync check (no bulk sizes)",
505			"sessionId", sessionID)
506	
507		// Acquire semaphore for HEAD request (blocks until available)
508		release := syncMgr.acquireHTTPSemaphore(sessionID, "HEAD")
509		defer release()
510	
511		// Make HEAD request
512		apiURL := GetAPIBaseURL() + "/api/v1/projects/" + projectID + "/sessions/" + sessionID
513		req, err := http.NewRequest("HEAD", apiURL, nil)
514		if err != nil {
515			slog.Error("Failed to create HEAD request",
516				"sessionId", sessionID,
517				"error", err)
518			return false, err
519		}
520	
521		// Add auth headers
522		cloudToken := GetCloudToken()
523		req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+cloudToken)
524		req.Header.Set("User-Agent", GetUserAgent())
525	
526		// Create HTTP client with shorter timeout for HEAD
527		client := &http.Client{
528			Timeout: 10 * time.Second,
529		}
530	
531		slog.Debug("Cloud sync HEAD request",
532			"sessionId", sessionID,
533			"url", apiURL,
534			"localMarkdownSize", localSize)
535	
536		// Execute request
537		resp, err := client.Do(req)
538		if err != nil {
539			slog.Debug("HEAD request failed, will attempt sync",
540				"sessionId", sessionID,
541				"error", err)
542			return true, nil // Err on side of syncing if HEAD fails
543		}
544		defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }()
545	
546		slog.Debug("Cloud sync HEAD response",
547			"sessionId", sessionID,
548			"status", resp.StatusCode,
549			"headers", resp.Header)
550	
551		// Check response
552		if resp.StatusCode == 404 {
553			slog.Debug("Session not found on server, will sync",
554				"sessionId", sessionID)
555			return true, nil
556		}
557	
558		if resp.StatusCode == 200 {
559			serverSizeStr := resp.Header.Get("X-Markdown-Size")
560			if serverSizeStr == "" {
561				slog.Debug("No X-Markdown-Size header, will sync",
562					"sessionId", sessionID)
563				return true, nil
564			}
565	
566			serverSize, err := strconv.Atoi(serverSizeStr)
567			if err != nil {
568				slog.Debug("Invalid X-Markdown-Size header, will sync",
569					"sessionId", sessionID,
570					"headerValue", serverSizeStr,
571					"error", err)
572				return true, nil
573			}
574	
575			if localSize > serverSize {
576				slog.Debug("Local markdown larger than server, will sync",
577					"sessionId", sessionID,
578					"localSize", localSize,
579					"serverSize", serverSize)
580				return true, nil
581			}
582	
583			slog.Info("Session already up-to-date on server, skipping sync",
584				"sessionId", sessionID,
585				"localSize", localSize,
586				"serverSize", serverSize)
587			return false, nil
588		}
589	
590		// For any other status codes, err on side of syncing
591		slog.Debug("Unexpected HEAD response status, will sync",
592			"sessionId", sessionID,
593			"status", resp.StatusCode)
594		return true, nil
595	}

625	// getDeviceID generates a unique device ID based on MAC address
626	func getDeviceID() string {
627		// Return cached value if available
628		if deviceID != "" {
629			return deviceID
630		}
631	
632		// Get all network interfaces
633		interfaces, err := net.Interfaces()
634		if err != nil {
635			slog.Warn("Failed to get network interfaces", "error", err)
636			// Fallback to hostname-based ID
637			hostname, _ := os.Hostname()
638			h := sha256.Sum256([]byte("specstory-" + hostname))
639			deviceID = hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
640			return deviceID
641		}
642	
643		// Find the first non-loopback interface with a MAC address
644		for _, iface := range interfaces {
645			if iface.Flags&net.FlagLoopback == 0 && len(iface.HardwareAddr) > 0 {
646				// Hash the MAC address
647				h := sha256.Sum256(iface.HardwareAddr)
648				deviceID = hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
649				slog.Debug("Generated device ID from interface", "interface", iface.Name)
650				return deviceID
651			}
652		}
653	
654		// Fallback if no suitable interface found
655		hostname, _ := os.Hostname()
656		h := sha256.Sum256([]byte("specstory-fallback-" + hostname))
657		deviceID = hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
658		slog.Debug("Using fallback device ID based on hostname")
659		return deviceID
660	}

662	// InitSyncManager initializes the global sync manager
663	func InitSyncManager(enabled bool) {
664		syncManagerMutex.Lock()
665		defer syncManagerMutex.Unlock()
666		globalSyncManager = &SyncManager{
667			enabled:          enabled,
668			silent:           false,
669			httpSemaphore:    make(chan struct{}, MaxConcurrentHTTPRequests),
670			debounceInterval: SessionDebounceInterval,
671		}
672		slog.Debug("Cloud sync manager initialized", "enabled", enabled, "maxConcurrentHTTPRequests", MaxConcurrentHTTPRequests)
673	}

676	func SetSilent(silent bool) {
677		syncManagerMutex.Lock()
678		defer syncManagerMutex.Unlock()
679		if globalSyncManager != nil {
680			globalSyncManager.silent = silent
681		}

691	// performSync executes the actual sync operation (HEAD check + PUT request)
692	func (syncMgr *SyncManager) performSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent string, rawData []byte, agentName string, skipHeadCheck bool) {
693		timestamp := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
694	
695		// Log the sync attempt
696		slog.Info("Cloud sync initiated",
697			"sessionId", sessionID,
698			"mdPath", mdPath,
699			"agentName", agentName,
700			"timestamp", timestamp)
701	
702		// Track that this session attempted to sync (for timeout calculation at shutdown)
703		// Only count each unique session once, even if performSync is called multiple times
704		// (e.g., due to debouncing in run mode triggering multiple syncs for the same session)
705		// Note: We check the map here but don't store yet - trackSessionInCategory() (called
706		// later in this function on every code path) will store the session in the map
707		if _, alreadyTracked := syncMgr.stats.countedSessions.Load(sessionID); !alreadyTracked {
708			atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.stats.SessionsAttempted, 1)
709		}
710	
711		// Content is now passed in as parameters, no need to read files
712	
713		// Create sync session data
714		syncData := SyncSession{
715			SessionID:    sessionID,
716			MDPath:       mdPath,
717			JSONLPath:    "", // No longer using file path
718			JSONLContent: rawData,
719			MDContent:    mdContent,
720		}
721	
722		// Read project configuration
723		projectData, err := readProjectConfig()
724		if err != nil {
725			slog.Warn("Cannot sync session to cloud: failed to read project config", "error", err, "sessionId", sessionID)
726			syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryErrored)
727			return
728		}
729	
730		// Determine project ID - use git_id if present, otherwise workspace_id
731		projectID := ""
732		if projectData != nil && projectData.GitID != "" {
733			projectID = projectData.GitID
734		} else if projectData != nil && projectData.WorkspaceID != "" {
735			projectID = projectData.WorkspaceID
736		}
737	
738		// Project ID is required for cloud sync
739		if projectID == "" {
740			slog.Warn("Cannot sync session to cloud: no project ID available", "sessionId", sessionID)
741			syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryErrored)
742			return
743		}
744	
745		// Get project name (use directory name as fallback - this is cosmetic only)
746		projectName := ""
747		if projectData != nil && projectData.ProjectName != "" {
748			projectName = projectData.ProjectName
749		} else {
750			// Fallback to extracting from mdPath for display purposes only
751			dir := filepath.Dir(filepath.Dir(mdPath))      // Go up from history to .specstory
752			projectName = filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(dir)) // Get the project directory name
753			slog.Debug("No project name in config, using directory name", "projectName", projectName)
754		}
755	
756		// Check if sync is needed using HEAD request
757		shouldSync, err := syncMgr.requiresSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent, projectID, skipHeadCheck)
758		if err != nil {
759			slog.Error("Failed to check if sync needed",
760				"sessionId", sessionID,
761				"error", err)
762			// Continue with sync attempt anyway
763		} else if !shouldSync {
764			// Track skipped session with precedence rules
765			syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categorySkipped)
766			slog.Info("Skipping cloud sync, already up-to-date",
767				"sessionId", sessionID)
768			return
769		}
770	
771		// Extract name from markdown filename (without path or extension)
772		name := filepath.Base(mdPath)
773		if ext := filepath.Ext(name); ext != "" {
774			name = name[:len(name)-len(ext)]
775		}
776	
777		// Create API request
778		metadata := APIRequestMetadata{
779			ClientName:    ClientName,
780			ClientVersion: clientVersion,
781			AgentName:     agentName, // Use the passed-in agent name
782			DeviceID:      getDeviceID(),
783		}
784		apiReq := APIRequest{
785			ProjectID:   projectID,
786			ProjectName: projectName,
787			Name:        name,
788			Markdown:    syncData.MDContent,
789			RawData:     string(syncData.JSONLContent),
790			Metadata:    metadata,
791		}
792	
793		// Marshal to JSON
794		jsonData, err := json.Marshal(apiReq)
795		if err != nil {
796			slog.Error("Cloud sync error marshaling JSON", "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
797			return
798		}
799	
800		// Acquire semaphore for PUT request (blocks until available)
801		release := syncMgr.acquireHTTPSemaphore(sessionID, "PUT")
802		defer release()
803	
804		// Make HTTP request
805		apiURL := GetAPIBaseURL() + "/api/v1/projects/" + projectID + "/sessions/" + sessionID
806		req, err := http.NewRequest("PUT", apiURL, bytes.NewBuffer(jsonData))
807		if err != nil {
808			slog.Error("Cloud sync error creating request", "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
809			return
810		}
811	
812		// Get cloud token for authorization
813		cloudToken := GetCloudToken()
814		req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+cloudToken)
815		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
816		req.Header.Set("Accept", "application/json")
817		req.Header.Set("User-Agent", GetUserAgent())
818	
819		// Create HTTP client with timeout
820		client := &http.Client{
821			Timeout: 30 * time.Second,
822		}
823	
824		slog.Debug("Cloud sync making API call",
825			"sessionId", sessionID,
826			"url", apiURL,
827			"projectId", projectID,
828			"projectName", projectName,
829			"name", name,
830			"metadata", metadata,
831			"jsonlSize", len(syncData.JSONLContent),
832			"mdSize", len(syncData.MDContent),
833			"deviceId", getDeviceID())
834	
835		// Make the request
836		resp, err := client.Do(req)
837		if err != nil {
838			// Track network/request errors with precedence rules
839			syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryErrored)
840			slog.Error("Cloud sync API error", "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
841			return
842		}
843		defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }()
844	
845		// Check response status
846		if resp.StatusCode >= 200 && resp.StatusCode < 300 {
847			// Get ETag from response header
848			etag := resp.Header.Get("ETag")
849			slog.Info("Cloud sync completed successfully",
850				"sessionId", sessionID,
851				"status", resp.StatusCode,
852				"etag", etag)
853	
854			// Track as created or updated based on status code with precedence rules
855			if resp.StatusCode == 201 {
856				syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryCreated)
857			} else {
858				syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryUpdated)
859			}
860		} else {
861			// Track as error with precedence rules
862			syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryErrored)
863	
864			// Try to read error response body
865			respBody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
866			if err != nil {
867				slog.Error("Cloud sync API returned error",
868					"sessionId", sessionID,
869					"status", resp.StatusCode,
870					"bodyReadError", err)
871			} else {
872				slog.Error("Cloud sync API returned error",
873					"sessionId", sessionID,
874					"status", resp.StatusCode,
875					"responseBody", string(respBody))
876			}
877		}
878	}
879	
880	// debouncedSync implements debouncing logic for a session
881	// Always skips HEAD check since we know content just changed in autosave mode
882	func (syncMgr *SyncManager) debouncedSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent string, rawData []byte, agentName string) {
883		// Get or create debounce state for this session
884		stateInterface, _ := syncMgr.debounceSessions.LoadOrStore(sessionID, &sessionDebounceState{})
885		state := stateInterface.(*sessionDebounceState)
886	
887		state.mu.Lock()
888		defer state.mu.Unlock()
889	
890		now := time.Now()
891		timeSinceLastSync := now.Sub(state.lastSyncTime)
892	
893		// Check if we can sync immediately (no recent sync or first sync)
894		if state.lastSyncTime.IsZero() || timeSinceLastSync >= syncMgr.debounceInterval {
895			// Sync immediately
896			state.lastSyncTime = now
897			state.pending = nil
898	
899			// Cancel any pending timer
900			if state.timer != nil {
901				state.timer.Stop()
902				state.timer = nil
903			}
904	
905			// Start sync in goroutine (always skip HEAD check in autosave mode)
906			syncMgr.wg.Add(1)
907			atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, 1)
908			go func() {
909				defer func() {
910					syncMgr.wg.Done()
911					atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, -1)
912				}()
913				syncMgr.performSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent, rawData, agentName, true)
914			}()
915	
916			// Log with timeSinceLastSync only if meaningful (not after cleanup)
917			if state.lastSyncTime.IsZero() {
918				slog.Debug("Cloud sync started immediately",
919					"sessionId", sessionID)
920			} else {
921				slog.Debug("Cloud sync started immediately",
922					"sessionId", sessionID,
923					"timeSinceLastSync", timeSinceLastSync)
924			}
925			return
926		}
927	
928		// Within debounce window - queue or replace pending request
929		state.pending = &pendingSyncRequest{
930			sessionID: sessionID,
931			mdPath:    mdPath,
932			mdContent: mdContent,
933			rawData:   rawData,
934			agentName: agentName,
935		}
936	
937		// Set timer if not already set
938		if state.timer == nil {
939			timeUntilSync := syncMgr.debounceInterval - timeSinceLastSync
940			state.timer = time.AfterFunc(timeUntilSync, func() {
941				syncMgr.flushPendingSync(sessionID)
942			})
943	
944			slog.Debug("Cloud sync queued with new timer",
945				"sessionId", sessionID,
946				"timeUntilSync", timeUntilSync)
947		} else {
948			slog.Debug("Cloud sync queued (replaced pending)",
949				"sessionId", sessionID)
950		}
951	}
952	
953	// flushPendingSync is called by timer to flush a queued sync
954	func (syncMgr *SyncManager) flushPendingSync(sessionID string) {
955		stateInterface, ok := syncMgr.debounceSessions.Load(sessionID)
956		if !ok {
957			return
958		}
959		state := stateInterface.(*sessionDebounceState)
960	
961		state.mu.Lock()
962		defer state.mu.Unlock()
963	
964		// Sync the pending request (timer is only set when pending is non-nil)
965		req := state.pending
966		state.pending = nil
967		state.timer = nil
968	
969		// Guard against nil pending request (defensive programming)
970		if req == nil {
971			slog.Warn("Timer fired but no pending request found",
972				"sessionId", sessionID)
973			syncMgr.debounceSessions.Delete(sessionID)
974			return
975		}
976	
977		// Clean up the session state from the map since we're done with this debounce cycle
978		// If another event comes later, LoadOrStore will create a fresh state
979		syncMgr.debounceSessions.Delete(sessionID)
980	
981		// Start sync in goroutine (always skip HEAD check for debounced syncs)
982		syncMgr.wg.Add(1)
983		atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, 1)
984		go func() {
985			defer func() {
986				syncMgr.wg.Done()
987				atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, -1)
988			}()
989			syncMgr.performSync(req.sessionID, req.mdPath, req.mdContent, req.rawData, req.agentName, true)
990		}()
991	
992		slog.Debug("Flushed pending sync after debounce, cleaned up session state",
993			"sessionId", sessionID)
994	}
995	
996	// flushAllPending flushes all pending debounced syncs (called on shutdown)
997	func (syncMgr *SyncManager) flushAllPending() {
998		slog.Debug("Flushing all pending debounced syncs")
999	
1000		syncMgr.debounceSessions.Range(func(key, value interface{}) bool {
1001			sessionID := key.(string)
1002			state := value.(*sessionDebounceState)
1003	
1004			state.mu.Lock()
1005			defer state.mu.Unlock()
1006	
1007			// Cancel timer
1008			if state.timer != nil {
1009				state.timer.Stop()
1010				state.timer = nil
1011			}
1012	
1013			// If there's a pending request, sync it immediately
1014			if state.pending != nil {
1015				req := state.pending
1016				state.pending = nil
1017	
1018				// Start sync in goroutine (always skip HEAD check for debounced syncs)
1019				syncMgr.wg.Add(1)
1020				atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, 1)
1021				go func() {
1022					defer func() {
1023						syncMgr.wg.Done()
1024						atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, -1)
1025					}()
1026					syncMgr.performSync(req.sessionID, req.mdPath, req.mdContent, req.rawData, req.agentName, true)
1027				}()
1028	
1029				slog.Info("Flushing pending sync on shutdown",
1030					"sessionId", sessionID)
1031			}
1032	
1033			return true
1034		})
1035	}
1036	
1037	// SyncSessionToCloud asynchronously syncs a session to the cloud
1038	// When isAutosaveMode is true (run command), syncs are debounced and skip HEAD checks for efficiency
1039	// When isAutosaveMode is false (manual sync), syncs are immediate with HEAD checks
1040	func SyncSessionToCloud(sessionID string, mdPath string, mdContent string, rawData []byte, agentName string, isAutosaveMode bool) {
1041		syncManagerMutex.RLock()
1042		syncMgr := globalSyncManager
1043		syncManagerMutex.RUnlock()
1044	
1045		if syncMgr == nil || !syncMgr.enabled {
1046			if syncMgr == nil {
1047				slog.Warn("Cloud sync not initialized, skipping sync", "sessionId", sessionID)
1048			} else {
1049				slog.Debug("Cloud sync disabled by flag, skipping sync", "sessionId", sessionID)
1050			}
1051			return
1052		}
1053	
1054		// Check authentication
1055		if !IsAuthenticated() {
1056			slog.Warn("Cloud sync skipped: user not authenticated", "sessionId", sessionID)
1057			return
1058		}
1059	
1060		// Route to debounced or immediate sync based on mode
1061		if isAutosaveMode {
1062			// Autosave mode: debounce syncs and skip HEAD checks
1063			syncMgr.debouncedSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent, rawData, agentName)
1064		} else {
1065			// Manual sync mode: immediate sync with HEAD check
1066			syncMgr.wg.Add(1)
1067			atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, 1)
1068			go func() {
1069				defer func() {
1070					syncMgr.wg.Done()
1071					atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, -1)
1072				}()
1073				syncMgr.performSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent, rawData, agentName, false)
1074			}()
1075		}
1076	}
1077	
1078	// getStats loads atomic counters and returns a CloudSyncStats with calculated timeout count.
1079	// This consolidates the pattern of loading counters + calculating timeouts used in multiple shutdown paths.
1080	func (sm *SyncManager) getStats() *CloudSyncStats {
1081		stats := &CloudSyncStats{
1082			SessionsAttempted: atomic.LoadInt32(&sm.stats.SessionsAttempted),
1083			SessionsSkipped:   atomic.LoadInt32(&sm.stats.SessionsSkipped),
1084			SessionsUpdated:   atomic.LoadInt32(&sm.stats.SessionsUpdated),
1085			SessionsCreated:   atomic.LoadInt32(&sm.stats.SessionsCreated),
1086			SessionsErrored:   atomic.LoadInt32(&sm.stats.SessionsErrored),
1087		}
1088		stats.calculateTimedOut()
1089		return stats
1090	}
1091	
1092	// Shutdown waits for all cloud sync operations to complete with a timeout and returns statistics
1093	func Shutdown(timeout time.Duration) *CloudSyncStats {
1094		syncManagerMutex.RLock()
1095		sm := globalSyncManager
1096		syncManagerMutex.RUnlock()
1097	
1098		if sm == nil || !sm.enabled {
1099			return nil
1100		}
1101	
1102		// Flush all pending debounced syncs before waiting
1103		sm.flushAllPending()
1104	
1105		// Create a channel to signal when all syncs are done
1106		done := make(chan struct{})
1107		go func() {
1108			sm.wg.Wait()
1109			close(done)
1110		}()
1111	
1112		// Give goroutines a moment to start and check if we have any work
1113		time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
1114	
1115		// Check if all work is already done (e.g., all sessions were skipped)
1116		select {
1117		case <-done:
1118			// All work completed very quickly (likely all skipped)
1119			stats := sm.getStats()
1120			// If we have stats, return them
1121			if stats.SessionsAttempted > 0 {
1122				return stats
1123			}
1124			// No work was done at all
1125			return nil
1126		default:
1127			// Work is still in progress, continue to show countdown
1128		}
1129	
1130		// Create a ticker for countdown updates
1131		ticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)
1132		defer ticker.Stop()
1133	
1134		// Track elapsed time
1135		start := time.Now()
1136	
1137		// Show initial countdown immediately
1138		if !sm.silent {
1139			fmt.Printf("Waiting for cloud sync to complete... %ds until timeout", int(timeout.Seconds()))
1140		}
1141	
1142		for {
1143			select {
1144			case <-done:
1145				// Ensure countdown is visible for at least 1 second
1146				elapsed := time.Since(start)
1147				if elapsed < 1*time.Second {
1148					time.Sleep(1*time.Second - elapsed)
1149				}
1150	
1151				// All syncs completed - clear the progress line
1152				if !sm.silent {
1153					// Clear the line by printing spaces over it, then return to beginning
1154					fmt.Printf("\r%60s\r", "")
1155				}
1156	
1157				// Return stats
1158				return sm.getStats()
1159			case <-ticker.C:
1160				// Update countdown
1161				elapsed := time.Since(start)
1162				remaining := timeout - elapsed
1163				if remaining <= 0 {
1164					// Timeout reached - clear the progress line
1165					if !sm.silent {
1166						fmt.Printf("\r%60s\r", "")
1167						fmt.Printf("Cloud sync timeout. Some syncs may not have completed.\n")
1168					}
1169					slog.Warn("Cloud sync shutdown timeout", "timeout", timeout)
1170	
1171					// Return partial stats
1172					return sm.getStats()
1173				}
1174				// Show countdown
1175				if !sm.silent {
1176					fmt.Printf("\rWaiting for cloud sync to complete... %ds until timeout", int(remaining.Seconds()))
1177				}
1178			}
1179		}
1180	}
1181	
1182	// readProjectConfig reads the .specstory/.project.json file
1183	func readProjectConfig() (*ProjectData, error) {
1184		var projectData ProjectData
1185		var err error
1186	
1187		// Read the project config file
1188		configPath := filepath.Join(utils.SPECSTORY_DIR, utils.PROJECT_JSON_FILE)
1189		data, err := os.ReadFile(configPath)
1190		if err != nil {
1191			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read project config: %w", err)
1192		}
1193	
1194		// Parse JSON
1195		err = json.Unmarshal(data, &projectData)
1196		if err != nil {
1197			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse project config: %w", err)
1198		}
1199	
1200		return &projectData, nil
1201	}

44	
45	// FormatFilenameTimestamp formats a timestamp for use in filenames.
46	// The format is filesystem-safe and matches the markdown title format.
47	func FormatFilenameTimestamp(t time.Time, useUTC bool) string {
48		if useUTC {
49			// Use UTC with Z suffix: "2006-01-02_15-04-05Z"
50			return t.UTC().Format("2006-01-02_15-04-05") + "Z"
51		}
52		// Use local timezone with offset: "2006-01-02_15-04-05-0700"
53		return t.Local().Format("2006-01-02_15-04-05-0700")
54	}
55	
56	// BuildSessionFilePath constructs the full markdown file path for a session.
57	// All callers need consistent filename generation from session metadata.
58	func BuildSessionFilePath(session *spi.AgentChatSession, historyDir string, useUTC bool) string {
59		timestamp, _ := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, session.CreatedAt)
60		timestampStr := FormatFilenameTimestamp(timestamp, useUTC)
61	
62		filename := timestampStr
63		if session.Slug != "" {
64			filename = fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", timestampStr, session.Slug)
65		}
66		return filepath.Join(historyDir, filename+".md")
67	}
68	
69	// ProcessingOptions holds flags that control session processing behavior.
70	type ProcessingOptions struct {
71		OnlyCloudSync      bool // Skip local markdown writes and only sync to cloud
72		ShowOutput         bool // Print progress to stdout
73		IsAutosave         bool // Called from run/watch (true) vs sync command (false)
74		DebugRaw           bool // Enable schema validation (only in debug mode to avoid overhead)
75		UseUTC             bool // Timestamp format: true=UTC, false=local
76		NoTelemetryPrompts bool // Exclude user prompt text from telemetry spans for privacy
77	}
78	
79	// ProcessSingleSession writes markdown and triggers cloud sync for a single session.
80	// ctx is used for OTel trace/span propagation (no-op when telemetry is disabled).
81	// Returns the size of the markdown content in bytes.
82	func ProcessSingleSession(ctx context.Context, session *spi.AgentChatSession, config utils.OutputConfig, opts ProcessingOptions) (int, error) {
83		if session == nil || session.SessionData == nil {
84			return 0, fmt.Errorf("session or session data is nil")
85		}
86	
87		// Track processing start time for metrics
88		processingStart := time.Now()
89	
90		// Create a context with a deterministic trace ID from the session ID.
91		// This groups all spans for the same session into a single trace, even
92		// across multiple invocations in autosave mode.
93		ctx = telemetry.ContextWithSessionTrace(ctx, session.SessionID)
94	
95		// Start an OTel span for this session processing (no-op when telemetry is disabled)
96		ctx, span := telemetry.Tracer("specstory").Start(ctx, "process_session")
97		defer span.End()
98	
99		// Compute session statistics
100		agentName := session.SessionData.Provider.Name
101		stats := telemetry.ComputeSessionStats(agentName, session)
102	
103		// Record metrics (no-op when telemetry is disabled)
104		defer func() {
105			telemetry.RecordSessionMetrics(ctx, stats, time.Since(processingStart))
106		}()
107	
108		// Set session span attributes
109		telemetry.SetSessionSpanAttributes(span, stats)
110	
111		// Create child spans for each exchange
112		if len(session.SessionData.Exchanges) > 0 {
113			telemetry.ProcessExchangeSpans(ctx, stats, session.SessionData.Exchanges, opts.NoTelemetryPrompts)
114		}
115	
116		ValidateSessionData(session, opts.DebugRaw)
117		WriteDebugSessionData(session, opts.DebugRaw)
118	
119		// Generate markdown from SessionData
120		markdownContent, err := GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession(session.SessionData, false, opts.UseUTC)
121		if err != nil {
122			slog.Error("Failed to generate markdown from SessionData", "sessionId", session.SessionID, "error", err)
123			return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to generate markdown: %w", err)
124		}
125	
126		// Calculate markdown size in bytes
127		markdownSize := len(markdownContent)
128	
129		// Generate filename from timestamp and slug
130		fileFullPath := BuildSessionFilePath(session, config.GetHistoryDir(), opts.UseUTC)
131	
132		if opts.ShowOutput && !log.IsSilent() {
133			fmt.Printf("Processing session %s...", session.SessionID)
134		}
135	
136		// Check if file already exists with same content
137		var outcome string
138		identicalContent := false
139		fileExists := false
140		if existingContent, err := os.ReadFile(fileFullPath); err == nil {
141			fileExists = true
142			if string(existingContent) == markdownContent {
143				identicalContent = true
144				slog.Info("Markdown file already exists with same content, skipping write",
145					"sessionId", session.SessionID,
146					"path", fileFullPath)
147			}
148		}
149	
150		// Write file if needed (skip if only-cloud-sync is enabled)
151		if !opts.OnlyCloudSync {
152			if !identicalContent {
153				// Ensure history directory exists (handles deletion during long-running watch/run)
154				if err := utils.EnsureHistoryDirectoryExists(config); err != nil {
155					return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to ensure history directory: %w", err)
156				}
157				err := os.WriteFile(fileFullPath, []byte(markdownContent), 0644)
158				if err != nil {
159					// Track write error
160					if opts.IsAutosave {
161						analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventAutosaveError, analytics.Properties{
162							"session_id":      session.SessionID,
163							"error":           err.Error(),
164							"only_cloud_sync": opts.OnlyCloudSync,
165						})
166					} else {
167						analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventSyncMarkdownError, analytics.Properties{
168							"session_id":      session.SessionID,
169							"error":           err.Error(),
170							"only_cloud_sync": opts.OnlyCloudSync,
171						})
172					}
173					return 0, fmt.Errorf("error writing markdown file: %w", err)
174				}
175	
176				// Track successful write
177				if opts.IsAutosave {
178					if !fileExists {
179						// New file created during autosave
180						analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventAutosaveNew, analytics.Properties{
181							"session_id":      session.SessionID,
182							"only_cloud_sync": opts.OnlyCloudSync,
183						})
184					} else {
185						// File updated during autosave
186						analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventAutosaveSuccess, analytics.Properties{
187							"session_id":      session.SessionID,
188							"only_cloud_sync": opts.OnlyCloudSync,
189						})
190					}
191				} else {
192					if !fileExists {
193						// New file created during manual sync
194						analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventSyncMarkdownNew, analytics.Properties{
195							"session_id":      session.SessionID,
196							"only_cloud_sync": opts.OnlyCloudSync,
197						})
198					} else {
199						// File updated during manual sync
200						analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventSyncMarkdownSuccess, analytics.Properties{
201							"session_id":      session.SessionID,
202							"only_cloud_sync": opts.OnlyCloudSync,
203						})
204					}
205				}
206	
207				slog.Info("Successfully wrote file",
208					"sessionId", session.SessionID,
209					"path", fileFullPath)
210			}
211	
212			// Determine outcome for user feedback
213			if identicalContent {
214				outcome = "up to date (skipped)"
215			} else if fileExists {
216				outcome = "updated"
217			} else {
218				outcome = "created"
219			}
220		} else {
221			// Only cloud sync mode - no local file operations
222			outcome = "synced to cloud only"
223			slog.Info("Skipping local file write (only-cloud-sync mode)",
224				"sessionId", session.SessionID)
225		}
226	
227		// Trigger cloud sync with provider-specific data
228		// In only-cloud-sync mode: always sync (no file to check for identical content)
229		// In normal mode: skip sync only if identical content AND in autosave mode
230		if opts.OnlyCloudSync || !identicalContent || !opts.IsAutosave {
231			cloud.SyncSessionToCloud(session.SessionID, fileFullPath, markdownContent, []byte(session.RawData), session.SessionData.Provider.Name, opts.IsAutosave)
232		}
233	
234		if opts.ShowOutput && !log.IsSilent() {
235			fmt.Printf(" %s\n", outcome)
236			fmt.Println() // Visual separation
237		}
238	
239		return markdownSize, nil
240	}
241
```
</details></tool-use>

Last piece confirmed — and it surfaces the one remaining architectural decision. Here's the consolidated plan, then the decision I need.

## Critical finding: the neutral `SessionData` is ephemeral today

`SessionData` is built in memory, used to render markdown, and **discarded**. Specifically:

- It's only written to disk under `--debug-raw` (`WriteDebugSessionData`), never in normal runs.
- The cloud upload (`APIRequest` in `sync.go`) carries **`Markdown` + `RawData`** (the source agent's native format) — **not** `SessionData`.

So "reconstruct from the neutral format" has a sourcing problem that splits by use case:

| Use case | Is `SessionData` available where/when we need it? |
|---|---|
| **Same-machine** (any → any) | Yes — re-parse the local native store through the existing forward path to rebuild `SessionData` on demand. Fully self-contained in this repo. |
| **Cross-machine** (agent → Cloud → agent) | **No** — the target machine has no local native store for that session. The cloud only has `Markdown` + `RawData`. |

There's a striking alignment hiding here: your chosen reconstruction model (tool calls collapse into agent text via the **existing markdown rendition**) means **the markdown the cloud already stores *is* essentially the neutral, flattened, provider-agnostic transcript** you want to reconstruct from. That opens three ways to feed the cross-machine path, which is the decision below.

## The consolidated plan (pending that decision)

**Phase 0 — Resume-acceptance spike (the gate).** Hand-craft one minimal synthesized session file for each agent and confirm the real CLIs load and continue it: a `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/<newUUID>.jsonl` with a valid `parentUuid` chain → `claude --resume <newUUID>`; and a `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-…-<newID>.jsonl` with `session_meta` + `response_item` transcript → `codex resume <newID>`. This validates the riskiest unknowns (loader strictness, thinking-signature rejection, chain integrity) before any converter is written. You run the interactive CLIs; I prepare the files.

**Phase 1 — The shared transform.** `SessionData` (or markdown — see decision) → an ordered list of `{role, text}` turns. Tool messages render to text via the already-populated `Tool.Summary`/`Tool.FormattedMarkdown`. Thinking dropped or flattened. Model/usage/pathHints dropped. Pure, unit-tested, provider-agnostic.

**Phase 2 — Per-provider serialization** behind a new **optional** `spi.SessionReconstructor` interface (type-asserted; the other 4 providers stay untouched). `claudecode` emits the `parentUuid`-chained JSONL with a fresh UUIDv4; `codexcli` emits `session_meta` + linear `response_item` transcript with a fresh UUIDv7-style ID. Each mints a new native ID and records the original as provenance. Round-trip test: reconstruct → re-parse → compare turns.

**Phase 3 — `specstory resume`** orchestration: obtain the transcript → `target.ReconstructSession(...)` → write the one file into the live store (reusing existing path derivation) → `ExecAgentAndWatch(resumeSessionID: newID)`. The "mutate another app's store" surface stays scoped to exactly the resumed session, as you specified.

Here's the one decision that determines whether this stays CLI-only or needs cloud-side work:

Locked. That settles the contract cleanly: **the reconstruction input is always `SessionData`**, and we source it by re-parsing the local native store for now (a dev/validation vehicle, not a shipped feature). The eventual shipped path — cloud serving `SessionData` — is separate future work that doesn't change the converter we build now. No markdown parser needed.

Here is the final plan.

## Reverse data flow: `SessionData → native` — plan

### Locked decisions

1. **Primary use case:** cross-agent portability (Claude ↔ Codex); cross-machine (agent → Cloud → agent) is the secondary, eventual-shipped target.
2. **Reconstruction input:** the neutral `SessionData`. First iterations obtain it by re-parsing the local native store (same-machine, dev-only, won't ship). Shipped cross-machine later = cloud persists/serves `SessionData` (option 2, out of scope now).
3. **Fidelity:** "gist" — output must be *valid* to the target loader and carry enough context for the agent's next request. No byte/structural round-trip.
4. **Tool activity:** tool calls collapse into **agent text messages** using the already-populated `Tool.Summary` / `Tool.FormattedMarkdown`. Reconstructed sessions contain only user + agent text turns — no native `tool_use`/`function_call` structures. This sidesteps all cross-provider tool-mapping and API-rejection risk.
5. **Cross-provider from day one:** model name, usage, path hints dropped; thinking/reasoning dropped or flattened to text; source agent's system/`environment_context` preamble not copied (target injects its own; optional one-line migration note).
6. **Session identity:** mint a fresh native-format ID (UUIDv4 for Claude, UUIDv7-style for Codex); store the original session ID as provenance.
7. **Live-store mutation:** only on explicit `specstory resume`, only the one session being resumed.

### Architecture

- **New optional capability interface** `spi.SessionReconstructor` — type-asserted, so the other four providers stay untouched:
  ```
  ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts ReconstructOptions) (*ReconstructedSession, error)
  ```
  Pure transform: `SessionData` → native bytes + new native session ID + suggested filename. No filesystem, fully unit-testable.

- **Shared flattening helper** (in `spi` or `session`): `SessionData` → ordered `[]{role, text}` turns. Tool messages → text via `Summary`/`FormattedMarkdown`; thinking/model/usage/pathHints handled per the cross-provider rules above. Both providers consume this, so the policy lives in one place.

- **Per-provider serialization:** `claudecode` emits `parentUuid`-chained JSONL into `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/<newUUID>.jsonl`; `codexcli` emits `session_meta` + a linear `response_item` transcript (`role:user`/`assistant` text items) into `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-…-<newID>.jsonl`. Both reuse existing path-derivation code.

- **`specstory resume` orchestration:** get `SessionData` (local re-parse now) → `target.ReconstructSession` → write the one native file → existing `ExecAgentAndWatch(resumeSessionID: newID)`.

### New files / interfaces to approve (per repo convention, asking before adding)

- `pkg/spi/reconstruct.go` — the `SessionReconstructor` interface + `ReconstructOptions`/`ReconstructedSession` types + shared flattening helper.
- `pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go` and `pkg/providers/codexcli/reconstruct.go` — plus `_test.go` round-trip tests.
- A `resume` command (likely `pkg/cmd/resume.go` + wiring in `main.go`).
- No new third-party dependencies (UUID generation: Codex IDs are already handled in-repo; I'll confirm the existing approach during the spike rather than add a dependency).

### Biggest risk → de-risk first

Whether a hand-synthesized file is actually *accepted* by `claude --resume` / `codex resume` (loader strictness, thinking-signature rejection, chain integrity) is unverifiable from code alone.

### Immediate next step — Phase 0 spike (your go-ahead)

I prepare two minimal hand-crafted session files (one per agent) with synthesized IDs/chains; **you** run `claude --resume <id>` and `codex resume <id>` and we observe whether each loads and continues. That single result determines whether the "gist transcript" approach is viable as-is or needs structural concessions — before we write any converter.

Want me to prepare those two spike files now? Note this is the first step that touches your real `~/.claude` / `~/.codex` stores (one throwaway session each), so I'll hold until you confirm.

Both transcripts and native shapes are in hand. My approach for the spike: synthesize **minimal, converter-producible** files — only what the eventual converter could derive from `SessionData` + cwd (the transcript turns, session scaffolding, and for Codex a synthesized `environment_context`). I'm deliberately **excluding** the runtime scaffolding the converter won't have (Codex `base_instructions`/developer-permissions blob, Claude `file-history-snapshot`/`mode` records, and all real tool-call records). Tool calls are flattened into agent text per the locked design. That way, if resume works, we know the minimal approach is viable; if it fails, the missing scaffolding is the prime suspect.

    return f"2026-06-16T16:{30+n//60:02d}:{n%60:02d}.000Z"

]
ENVCTX = ("<environment_context>\n  <cwd>%s</cwd>\n  <shell>zsh</shell>\n"

    codex_lines.append({"timestamp":t,"type":"event_msg","payload":{
        "type":"agent_message","message":a}})

codex_path = f"/Users/sean/.codex/sessions/2026/06/16/rollout-2026-06-16T12-30-00-{codex_new}.jsonl"

    for r in codex_lines: f.write(json.dumps(r)+"\n")

]
SID = claude_new
MODEL = "claude-opus-4-8"
def base(uuid, parent, typ):
    return {"parentUuid":parent,"isSidechain":False,"userType":"external",
            "cwd":CWD,"sessionId":SID,"version":"2.1.178","gitBranch":"HEAD",
            "type":typ,"uuid":uuid}

claude_lines = []
import uuid as _u
def nid(): return str(_u.uuid4())

prev = None
u0 = nid()
rec = base(u0, None, "user"); rec["timestamp"]=ts(0)
rec["message"]={"role":"user","content":PROMPT_D}

Here's the remaining roadmap, updated to reflect that **Phase 0 (resume-acceptance spike) is complete and passed**.

## Phase 1 — Shared core: `SessionData → turns` + the SPI seam

New file `pkg/spi/reconstruct.go`:

- `SessionReconstructor` optional interface: `ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts ReconstructOptions) (*ReconstructedSession, error)`.
- `ReconstructOptions` (target cwd, optional migration note) and `ReconstructedSession` (native bytes, new native session ID, suggested filename).
- Shared flattening helper: `SessionData` → ordered `[]Turn{Role, Text}`. Tool messages → text via the already-populated `Tool.Summary`/`Tool.FormattedMarkdown`; thinking → text or dropped; model/usage/pathHints dropped; sidechain agent turns folded in.

Pure, no filesystem, fully unit-tested. This is where the cross-provider policy lives, once.

## Phase 2 — The two native serializers (+ round-trip tests)

- `pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go` — emit the `parentUuid`-chained JSONL (1 user + N agent text records), fresh UUIDv4, original ID stored as provenance.
- `pkg/providers/codexcli/reconstruct.go` — emit `session_meta` + linear `response_item` message transcript + matching `event_msg` stream, fresh UUIDv7-shape ID.
- Each implements `SessionReconstructor`; `_test.go` round-trips reconstruct → re-parse via the existing forward parser → assert turns match. Exactly the structures the spike already proved resumable.

## Phase 3 — `specstory resume` command

`pkg/cmd/resume.go` + `main.go` wiring:

- Resolve source `SessionData` (first iteration: re-parse the local native store; type-assert the target provider to `SessionReconstructor`).
- `ReconstructSession` → write the one native file into the live store (reusing `GetClaudeCodeProjectDir` / `codexSessionsRoot` + day-dir) → hand off to existing `ExecAgentAndWatch(resumeSessionID: newID)`.
- Live-store mutation scoped to exactly the resumed session.

## Phase 4 — End-to-end validation + edge hardening

- Drive real cross-agent resumes through the actual command (not hand-built files), both directions.
- Confirm the open sub-question: does Codex need the synthesized `environment_context`, or can we drop it?
- Edge cases: empty/whitespace turns, very large tool outputs, sessions with no agent text (tool-only), slug/timestamp/filename collisions, unicode.

## Phase 5 — Docs + analytics

- README + help text for `resume`; a `EventResume*` analytics event; provenance back-reference recorded.

## Deferred (explicitly out of scope now)

- **Cross-machine shipping path**: cloud persisting/serving `SessionData` (today it stores only Markdown + RawData). Separate, cloud-side work.
- The other four providers implementing `SessionReconstructor`.

Phases 1–2 are the natural first build chunk (converter + tests, no command surface yet). Want me to start there?

=== PROVIDER-SPI.md head ===
# SpecStory CLI's Agent SPI

The SpecStory CLI's Agent SPI (Service Provider Interface) allows developers to extend the SpecStory CLI by implementing support for new AI coding agents.

## Overview

The SPI defines a standard interface that all agent providers must implement. This enables SpecStory CLI to work uniformly with multiple AI coding agents while each provider handles the specifics of its native data formats.

### Currently Supported Providers

| Provider ID | Display Name | Session Storage |
|-------------|--------------|-----------------|
| `claude` | Claude Code | `~/.claude/projects/<hash>/<session-id>.jsonl` |
| `cursor` | Cursor CLI | `~/.cursor/chats/<project-hash>/<session-id>/store.db` |
| `codex` | Codex CLI | `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/<session-id>.jsonl` |
| `gemini` | Gemini CLI | `~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/chats/<session-id>.json` |

## Quick Start: Implementing a New Provider

To add support for a new AI coding agent:

1. Create a new package under `pkg/providers/<yourprovider>/`
2. Implement the `spi.Provider` interface (see [Provider Interface](#provider-interface))
3. Register your provider in `pkg/spi/factory/registry.go`

## Architecture

### Package Structure

```
pkg/
├── spi/
│   ├── provider.go        # Provider interface and CheckResult type
│   ├── cmdline.go         # Command line parsing utilities
│   ├── path_utils.go      # Path utilities (canonical paths, slugs, debug output)
│   ├── factory/
│   │   └── registry.go    # Provider registry (imports all providers)
│   └── schema/
│       └── types.go       # Unified SessionData schema
└── providers/
    ├── claudecode/        # Claude Code provider implementation
    ├── cursorcli/         # Cursor CLI provider implementation
    ├── codexcli/          # Codex CLI provider implementation
    └── gemini/            # Gemini CLI provider implementation
```

### Dependency Flow

```
main.go
  → spi/factory (registry)
      → spi (interfaces)
      → providers/claudecode
      → providers/cursorcli
      → providers/codexcli
      → providers/gemini

providers/* → spi (interfaces and schema)
```
```
</details></tool-use>

```md
# Session Portability (Reverse Data Flow)

This document describes the design for **session portability**: reconstructing an agent's native session format *from* the SpecStory CLI's neutral session schema, so a conversation captured from one agent can be resumed in another — and, eventually, on another machine.

## Overview

Today the CLI's data flow is one-directional:

\```
agent-native format  ──►  neutral SessionData  ──►  markdown / cloud
   (per provider)            (schema.SessionData)       (rendered output)
\```

Each provider parses its agent's native session store into the unified `schema.SessionData`, which is then rendered to markdown and synced to the cloud.

Session portability **reverses** the second arrow, adding a new provider responsibility:

\```
neutral SessionData  ──►  agent-native format
 (schema.SessionData)        (per provider)
\```

A provider gains the ability to take a `SessionData` and emit a native session file that its agent can load and resume. Combined with a new `specstory resume` experience, this enables a conversation to continue in a *different* agent than the one that created it.

## Use Cases

1. **Cross-agent portability (primary).**

   Start a session in Claude Code, continue it in Codex CLI (or vice versa). The neutral `SessionData` is the interchange format; each provider reconstructs its own native format from it.

2. **Cross-machine portability (secondary, eventual).**

   A session created on machine A is carried via SpecStory Cloud to machine B and resumed there. This depends on the cloud persisting and serving `SessionData` (see [SessionData Sourcing](#sessiondata-sourcing)), which is **not** yet in place and is out of scope for the first iterations.

## Goals and Non-Goals

### Goals

- A provider can reconstruct a resumable native session file from `SessionData`.
- Cross-provider reconstruction works from day one (Claude ↔ Codex), since the tool/model/usage mapping problems are fundamental and must be confronted early.
- The reconstructed session is **valid** to the target agent's loader and conveys the **gist** of the prior conversation as context for the agent's next request.

### Non-Goals

- **Byte-for-byte or structural round-trip.** The agent only needs the prior conversation as context for subsequent model requests; it does not need a faithful replica of the original session's internal structure.
- **Faithful tool replay.** Reconstructed sessions do not reproduce native `tool_use` / `function_call` structures (see [Reconstruction Model](#reconstruction-model)).
- **Reconstruction for all providers.** Only Claude Code and Codex CLI implement reconstruction initially.

### Fidelity Bar

Between "the agent parses it without error" and "best-effort readable." The reconstructed session must be structurally valid enough that the target agent loads and resumes it, and must carry enough of the conversation that the agent has useful context going forward. Synthesized IDs, timestamps, and chains are acceptable.

## Background: Why the Reverse Path Is Not a Mirror

The forward parsers read a **different projection** of each native file than the agent needs in order to *resume*. Reconstruction therefore cannot simply "run the parser backwards" — it must synthesize records the forward path never reads.

- **Codex CLI.** The forward parser builds `SessionData` from the `event_msg` UI-event stream (`user_message`, `agent_message`, `agent_reasoning`). But `codex resume` replays the `response_item` stream — the model-facing transcript (`{type:"message", role, content:[…]}` plus tool-call items). Both streams coexist in every rollout file; reconstruction must regenerate the `response_item` transcript.

- **Claude Code.** The forward parser collapses the `parentUuid` linked-list into linear exchanges and keeps only the record `uuid`. It drops the API `message.id`, the chain, and thinking-block signatures. `claude --resume` needs a valid `parentUuid` chain and API-shaped messages.

Combined with cross-provider conversion (a Claude `Edit` has no clean Codex `apply_patch` equivalent; model names and token-usage fields are meaningless to the other agent), faithful structural tool replay is intractable. The pragmatic, robust target is a **flattened transcript** in the target's native shape.

## Reconstruction Model

`SessionData` is flattened into an ordered list of **plain user/agent text turns**, then serialized into the target's native format. Everything the agent "said," "thought," or "did" collapses into agent text:

| `SessionData` element | Becomes |
|-----------------------|---------|
| user message text | user turn |
| agent message text | agent turn |
| thinking content (`ContentTypeThinking`) | agent turn (text) |
| tool call | agent turn (text, via `Tool.Summary` / `Tool.FormattedMarkdown`) |
| `model`, `Usage`, `PathHints` | dropped |
| source agent system / `environment_context` preamble | not copied (target injects its own) |

The tool-call flattening reuses work already done on the forward pass: each `ToolInfo` already carries a pre-rendered markdown rendition (`FormattedMarkdown`, and for Codex also `Summary`). Reconstruction emits that markdown as the text of an agent turn, so reconstructed sessions contain **only user and agent text turns** — no native tool structures, no dangling tool results, no thinking signatures. This is what makes cross-provider reconstruction safe: there is nothing provider-specific or API-fragile left to mistranslate.

## Validated Spike

The core hypothesis was validated before any converter code was written (project `~/Source/SpecStory/compositions/cross-portable-1`, 2026-06-16). Hand-built cross-agent session files — a Codex session synthesized from a Claude conversation, and a Claude session synthesized from a Codex conversation — were both loaded and continued successfully by `claude --resume` and `codex resume`.

The spike files were deliberately **minimal**: only what the converter could produce from `SessionData` plus the target cwd. This confirmed:

- A minimal user/agent text transcript is sufficient to resume. The runtime scaffolding the converter cannot produce was omitted and **not** required — Codex `base_instructions` / developer-permissions / `turn_context`, and Claude `file-history-snapshot` / `mode` / attachment records. Each agent injects its own scaffolding on resume.
- Tool-calls-as-agent-text are accepted. No native `tool_use` / `function_call` and no `tool_result` pairing are needed.
- Fresh native-format session IDs work, with the ID embedded in the filename.

## Architecture

### The `SessionReconstructor` Interface

Reconstruction is an **optional capability**, expressed as a separate interface rather than added to `spi.Provider`. Only Claude Code and Codex CLI implement it initially; the other providers are untouched, and consumers discover the capability via a type assertion.

\```go
// pkg/spi/reconstruct.go

// SessionReconstructor is implemented by providers that can rebuild their
// native session format from the neutral SessionData.
type SessionReconstructor interface {
    ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts ReconstructOptions) (*ReconstructedSession, error)
}

// ReconstructOptions controls how a session is reconstructed.
type ReconstructOptions struct {
    WorkspaceRoot string // target cwd; native paths/IDs are derived from this
    MigrationNote string // optional one-line note prepended as context
}

// ReconstructedSession is the native output of reconstruction.
type ReconstructedSession struct {
    SessionID string // freshly minted, native-format ID
    Filename  string // suggested native filename (relative)
    Content   []byte // native session file bytes
}
\```

`ReconstructSession` is a **pure transform** — no filesystem access — which makes it trivially unit-testable and the natural home for round-trip tests.

### Shared Flattening Helper

The `SessionData → []Turn` flattening (the [Reconstruction Model](#reconstruction-model) table) lives in one shared place so the cross-provider policy is defined once and both providers consume it.

\```go
type Turn struct {
    Role string // schema.RoleUser or schema.RoleAgent
    Text string
}
\```

### Per-Provider Serializers

Each provider converts `[]Turn` into its native, resumable file:

- **Claude Code** (`pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go`): a `parentUuid`-chained JSONL (one `user` record followed by `assistant` text records) written to `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/<new-uuid>.jsonl`. Fresh UUIDv4.

- **Codex CLI** (`pkg/providers/codexcli/reconstruct.go`): a `session_meta` record plus a linear `response_item` message transcript (and matching `event_msg` stream) written to `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-<ts>-<new-id>.jsonl`. Fresh UUIDv7-shape ID.

Both reuse existing path-derivation code (`GetClaudeCodeProjectDir`, `codexSessionsRoot`).

### Dependency Flow

\```
cmd/resume.go
  → spi (SessionReconstructor, type-asserted)
  → providers/{claudecode,codexcli} (ReconstructSession)
  → existing ExecAgentAndWatch (resume)

providers/{claudecode,codexcli} → spi (flattening helper + schema)
\```

## Cross-Provider Mapping Policy

Because reconstruction flattens to text, most mapping problems dissolve. The explicit rules:

- **Model name** — dropped. The target agent uses its own configured model going forward.
- **Token usage** — dropped. Irrelevant to a resumed conversation.
- **Thinking / reasoning** — flattened to agent text. (Not dropped, not emitted as a signed thinking block.)
- **Tool calls** — flattened to agent text via the pre-rendered markdown.
- **System / `environment_context` preamble** — not copied from the source agent; the target injects its own on resume. An optional one-line migration note may be prepended so the agent understands the prior turns were imported.

## Session Identity and Provenance

Reconstruction always mints a **fresh native-format session ID** (UUIDv4 for Claude, UUIDv7-shape for Codex) — the session did not previously exist in the target store, and the ID must be valid for the target. The original session ID is recorded as provenance metadata so the lineage is traceable.

## The `specstory resume` Flow

\```
specstory resume <session>
  1. Obtain SessionData for <session>        (see SessionData Sourcing)
  2. Resolve the target provider; type-assert to SessionReconstructor
  3. ReconstructSession(data, opts)           → native bytes + fresh ID
  4. Write the one native file into the live agent store
  5. ExecAgentAndWatch(resumeSessionID: freshID)
\```

Writing into another application's data store is the one sensitive action. It is **scoped to exactly the single session being resumed**, and only happens when the user explicitly invokes `resume`.

## SessionData Sourcing

The reconstruction input is always `SessionData`. Where that `SessionData` comes from differs by use case, and this is the main thing that gates cross-machine support:

| Use case | Source of `SessionData` | Status |
|----------|-------------------------|--------|
| Same-machine (any → any) | Re-parse the local native store through the existing forward path | Used for first iterations; a development/validation vehicle, not a shipped feature |
| Cross-machine (agent → Cloud → agent) | Cloud persists and serves `SessionData` | **Deferred.** Cloud currently stores only rendered Markdown + native RawData, not `SessionData` |

Note that `SessionData` is currently **ephemeral** — built in memory, used to render markdown, then discarded (it is only written to disk under `--debug-raw`). Persisting/serving it is the cloud-side work that the shipped cross-machine path depends on.

## Phased Plan

- **Phase 0 — Resume-acceptance spike. _(Complete.)_** Hand-built cross-agent files resumed successfully in both agents. Validated the minimal flattened-transcript approach.

- **Phase 1 — Shared core.** `pkg/spi/reconstruct.go`: the `SessionReconstructor` interface, option/result types, and the shared `SessionData → []Turn` flattening helper. Unit-tested.

- **Phase 2 — Native serializers.** `reconstruct.go` in `claudecode` and `codexcli`, each minting a fresh ID and recording provenance. Round-trip tests: reconstruct → re-parse via the forward parser → assert turns match.

- **Phase 3 — `specstory resume` command.** `pkg/cmd/resume.go` + wiring; local re-parse to obtain `SessionData`; write to the live store; hand off to `ExecAgentAndWatch`.

- **Phase 4 — End-to-end validation and edge hardening.** Real cross-agent resumes through the command, both directions. Resolve whether Codex needs a synthesized `environment_context`. Edge cases: empty/whitespace turns, large tool outputs, tool-only sessions, filename collisions, unicode.

- **Phase 5 — Docs and analytics.** README/help for `resume`; resume analytics events; provenance recorded.

## Risks and Open Questions

- **Loader strictness across agent versions.** The spike validated current Claude Code and Codex CLI versions. Future versions could tighten their loaders. Round-trip tests guard our own parsing; real-agent validation (Phase 4) guards theirs.
- **Codex `environment_context` necessity.** The spike included a minimal synthesized one. Whether it is required (vs. injected by Codex on resume) is still open; to be settled in Phase 4.
- **Loss of tool fidelity.** By design, the resumed agent cannot inspect exact prior tool inputs/outputs beyond what the flattened text conveys. Accepted under the gist fidelity bar.

## Out of Scope

- Cloud persistence/serving of `SessionData` (the cross-machine shipping path).
- Reconstruction support in the Cursor, Gemini, Droid, and DeepSeek providers.

## Related Documents

- [PROVIDER-SPI.md](PROVIDER-SPI.md) — the provider interface and registry.
- [SPI-SESSION-DATA-SCHEMA.md](SPI-SESSION-DATA-SCHEMA.md) — the neutral `SessionData` schema.

142	    Role string // schema.RoleUser or schema.RoleAgent
143	    Text string
144	}
145	```
146	
147	### Per-Provider Serializers
148	
149	Each provider converts `[]Turn` into its native, resumable file:
150	
151	- **Claude Code** (`pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go`): a `parentUuid`-chained JSONL (one `user` record followed by `assistant` text records) written to `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/<new-uuid>.jsonl`. Fresh UUIDv4.
152	
153	- **Codex CLI** (`pkg/providers/codexcli/reconstruct.go`): a `session_meta` record plus a linear `response_item` message transcript (and matching `event_msg` stream) written to `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-<ts>-<new-id>.jsonl`. Fresh UUIDv7-shape ID.
154	
155	Both reuse existing path-derivation code (`GetClaudeCodeProjectDir`, `codexSessionsRoot`).
156	
157	### Dependency Flow
158	
159	```
160	cmd/resume.go
161	  → spi (SessionReconstructor, type-asserted)
162	  → providers/{claudecode,codexcli} (ReconstructSession)
163	  → existing ExecAgentAndWatch (resume)
164	
165	providers/{claudecode,codexcli} → spi (flattening helper + schema)
166	```
167
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
196	
197	Writing into another application's data store is the one sensitive action. It is **scoped to exactly the single session being resumed**, and only happens when the user explicitly invokes `resume`.
198	
199	## SessionData Sourcing
200	
201	The reconstruction input is always `SessionData`. Where that `SessionData` comes from is the main thing that gates each stage:
202	
203	| Stage             | Source of `SessionData`                                                               | Status                                                                |
204	|-------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
205	| 1. Cross-provider | Re-parse the local native store (origin project) through the existing forward path    | First build target                                                    |
206	| 2. Cross-project  | Re-parse the local native store (origin project); reconstruct against destination cwd | Local; follows stage 1                                                |
207	| 3. Cross-machine  | Cloud persists and serves `SessionData`                                               | Gated on cloud-side work (cloud stores only Markdown + RawData today) |
208	| 4. Cross-user     | Cloud serves `SessionData` **and** Orgs/Teams sharing + permissions                   | Gated on the cloud Orgs/Teams product                                 |
209	
210	Stages 1 and 2 are entirely local — `SessionData` is rebuilt on demand by re-parsing the native store through the existing forward path. Stages 3 and 4 need the cloud to carry `SessionData` between machines and users.
211	
212	Note that `SessionData` is currently **ephemeral** — built in memory, used to render markdown, then discarded (it is only written to disk under `--debug-raw`). Persisting/serving it is the cloud-side work that stages 3 and 4 depend on.
213	
214	> A pure same-provider, same-project, same-machine "resume" is **not** a product use case — the agent's own native session already exists, so there is nothing to reconstruct. That trivial round-trip is used only as a development and test vehicle for the reconstruction core.
215	
216	## Build Plan (Stage 1: Cross-Provider Resume)
217	
218	These phases deliver **Stage 1** — the reconstruction core, the two native serializers, and the `specstory resume` command, all with local plumbing. The same core is reused by every later stage; stages 2–4 add sourcing and sharing plumbing around it rather than changing it (see [Later Stages](#later-stages)).
219	
220	- **Phase 0 — Resume-acceptance spike. _(Complete.)_** Hand-built cross-provider files resumed successfully in both agents. Validated the minimal flattened-transcript approach.
221	
222	- **Phase 1 — Shared core.** `pkg/spi/reconstruct.go`: the `SessionReconstructor` interface, option/result types, and the shared `SessionData → []Turn` flattening helper. Unit-tested.
223	
224	- **Phase 2 — Native serializers.** `reconstruct.go` in `claudecode` and `codexcli`, each minting a fresh ID and recording provenance. Round-trip tests: reconstruct → re-parse via the forward parser → assert turns match.
225	
226	- **Phase 3 — `specstory resume` command.** `pkg/cmd/resume.go` + wiring; local re-parse to obtain `SessionData`; write to the live store; hand off to `ExecAgentAndWatch`.
227	
228	- **Phase 4 — End-to-end validation and edge hardening.** Real cross-agent resumes through the command, both directions. Resolve whether Codex needs a synthesized `environment_context`. Edge cases: empty/whitespace turns, large tool outputs, tool-only sessions, filename collisions, unicode.
229	
230	- **Phase 5 — Docs and analytics.** README/help for `resume`; resume analytics events; provenance recorded.
231	
232	## Risks and Open Questions
233	
234	- **Loader strictness across agent versions.** The spike validated current Claude Code and Codex CLI versions. Future versions could tighten their loaders. Round-trip tests guard our own parsing; real-agent validation (Phase 4) guards theirs.
235	- **Codex `environment_context` necessity.** The spike included a minimal synthesized one. Whether it is required (vs. injected by Codex on resume) is still open; to be settled in Phase 4.
236	- **Loss of tool fidelity.** By design, the resumed agent cannot inspect exact prior tool inputs/outputs beyond what the flattened text conveys. Accepted under the gist fidelity bar.
237	
238	## Later Stages
239	
240	These follow Stage 1 in the sequencing and are not part of the initial build:
```

238	## Later Stages
239	
240	These follow Stage 1 in the sequencing and are not part of the initial build:
241	
242	- **Stage 2 — Cross-project resume.** Re-point source lookup and target write at a destination workspace; derive the destination project folder / native store path from the new cwd. Local-only; reuses the Stage 1 reconstruction core unchanged.
243	- **Stage 3 — Cross-machine resume.** Requires the cloud to persist and serve `SessionData` (today it stores only Markdown + native RawData), plus a CLI path that sources `SessionData` from the cloud instead of a local re-parse.
244	- **Stage 4 — Cross-user resume.** Requires the SpecStory Cloud Orgs/Teams product — session sharing, identity, and permissions governing who may resume whose sessions.
245	
246	## Out of Scope
247	
248	- Reconstruction support in the Cursor, Gemini, Droid, and DeepSeek providers (only Claude Code and Codex CLI initially).
249	
250	## Related Documents
251	
252	- [PROVIDER-SPI.md](PROVIDER-SPI.md) — the provider interface and registry.
253	- [SPI-SESSION-DATA-SCHEMA.md](SPI-SESSION-DATA-SCHEMA.md) — the neutral `SessionData` schema.
254

56	func formatDuration(d time.Duration) string {
57		if d < time.Microsecond {
58			return fmt.Sprintf("%.0f nanoseconds", float64(d.Nanoseconds()))
59		} else if d < time.Millisecond {
60			return fmt.Sprintf("%.1f microseconds", float64(d.Nanoseconds())/1000)
61		} else if d < time.Second {
62			return fmt.Sprintf("%.1f milliseconds", float64(d.Nanoseconds())/1000000)
63		} else if d < time.Minute {

65		} else {

67		}

194	func renderToolInfo(tool *ToolInfo) string {
195		var markdown strings.Builder
196	
197		// Get the tool type for the data attribute
198		toolType := tool.Type
199		if toolType == "" {
200			toolType = "generic"
201		}
202	
203		// Open tool-use tag
204		fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, ToolUseOpenTag, toolType, tool.Name)
205		markdown.WriteString("\n")
206	
207		// Generate summary tag
208		if tool.Summary != nil && *tool.Summary != "" {
209			// Provider provided custom summary content
210			fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, "<summary>%s</summary>\n", *tool.Summary)
211		} else {
212			// Generate default summary
213			fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, "<summary>Tool use: **%s**</summary>\n", tool.Name)
214		}
215	
216		// Use pre-formatted markdown from provider if present
217		if tool.FormattedMarkdown != nil && *tool.FormattedMarkdown != "" {
218			markdown.WriteString(*tool.FormattedMarkdown)
219			// Ensure there's a newline before the closing tag
220			// Some providers include it, some don't - normalize here
221			if !strings.HasSuffix(*tool.FormattedMarkdown, "\n") {
222				markdown.WriteString("\n")
223			}
224		} else {
225			// Fallback to generic formatting
226			markdown.WriteString(renderGenericTool(tool))
227		}
228	
229		// Close tool-use tag
230		markdown.WriteString(ToolUseCloseTag)
231		markdown.WriteString("\n")
232	
233		return markdown.String()
234	}
235	
236	// renderGenericTool renders a tool without provider-specific formatting
237	// Displays all available information as key-value pairs
238	// Note: The <summary> tag is already written by the caller (renderToolMessage)
239	func renderGenericTool(tool *ToolInfo) string {
240		var markdown strings.Builder
241		markdown.WriteString("\n")
242	
243		// Display input as key-value pairs if present
244		// Sort keys for deterministic output order
245		if len(tool.Input) > 0 {
246			markdown.WriteString("**Input:**\n\n")
247	
248			// Extract and sort keys for deterministic ordering
249			keys := make([]string, 0, len(tool.Input))
250			for key := range tool.Input {
251				// Skip internal fields like _cwd
252				if !strings.HasPrefix(key, "_") {
253					keys = append(keys, key)
254				}
255			}
256			sort.Strings(keys)
257	
258			for _, key := range keys {
259				fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, "- %s: `%v`\n", key, tool.Input[key])
260			}
261			markdown.WriteString("\n")
262		}
263	
264		// Display output if present
265		if tool.Output != nil {
266			if isError, ok := tool.Output["is_error"].(bool); ok && isError {
267				markdown.WriteString("**Error:**\n\n")
268			} else {
269				markdown.WriteString("**Result:**\n\n")
270			}
271	
272			if content, ok := tool.Output["content"].(string); ok {
273				markdown.WriteString("```\n")
274				markdown.WriteString(content)
275				markdown.WriteString("\n```\n")
276			}
277		}
278	
279		return markdown.String()
280	}
281	
282	// shouldAddSeparator determines if a separator should be added between messages
283	func shouldAddSeparator(currentRole, prevRole string) bool {
284		// Add separator on role transitions
285		if prevRole == "" {
286			return false
287		}
288		return currentRole != prevRole
```

package spi

import (
	"errors"
	"strings"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

// ErrReconstructionUnsupported is returned by a provider that carries the
// ReconstructSession responsibility but does not yet have a native serializer.
// Reconstruction is on the Provider interface so every provider implements the
// method, but only some providers (initially Claude Code and Codex CLI) produce
// real native output.
var ErrReconstructionUnsupported = errors.New("session reconstruction not supported by this provider")

// ReconstructOptions controls how a session is reconstructed into native form.
type ReconstructOptions struct {
	// WorkspaceRoot is the target working directory the reconstructed session
	// should belong to. Native paths/IDs are derived from it. When empty, the
	// provider falls back to the SessionData's own WorkspaceRoot.
	WorkspaceRoot string

	// MigrationNote, when set, is prepended as a leading agent turn so the agent
	// understands the prior turns were imported from another session. Optional.
	MigrationNote string
}

// ReconstructedSession is the native output of reconstruction. It is data only;
// the caller decides where (and whether) to write it.
type ReconstructedSession struct {
	SessionID string // freshly minted, native-format session ID
	Filename  string // suggested native filename (base name, not a full path)
	Content   []byte // native session file bytes
}

// Turn is a single role-tagged text turn in the flattened transcript. The
// reconstruction model collapses everything an agent said, thought, or did into
// plain user/agent text turns (see docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md), which each
// provider then serializes into its native format.
type Turn struct {
	Role string // schema.RoleUser or schema.RoleAgent
	Text string
}

// FlattenSessionData reduces a SessionData into an ordered list of plain
// user/agent text turns. This is the single place the cross-provider flattening
// policy lives, so every provider's serializer consumes the same transcript.
//
// Rules (per the locked design):
//   - user/agent text content -> a turn of that role
//   - thinking content -> folded into the agent turn's text (not dropped)
//   - tool calls -> folded into the agent turn's text via the pre-rendered
//     Summary / FormattedMarkdown captured on the forward pass
//   - model, usage, and path hints -> dropped
//
// migrationNote, when non-empty, becomes a leading agent turn.
func FlattenSessionData(data *schema.SessionData, migrationNote string) []Turn {
	var turns []Turn

	if strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote) != "" {
		turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote)})
	}

	if data == nil {
		return turns
	}

	for i := range data.Exchanges {
		for j := range data.Exchanges[i].Messages {
			msg := &data.Exchanges[i].Messages[j]

			// Only user and agent roles produce turns; anything else is skipped.
			if msg.Role != schema.RoleUser && msg.Role != schema.RoleAgent {
				continue
			}

			text := messageTurnText(msg)
			if strings.TrimSpace(text) == "" {
				continue
			}

			turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: msg.Role, Text: text})
		}
	}

	return turns
}

// messageTurnText flattens a single message into its turn text: all text/thinking
// content parts joined, followed by the tool's pre-rendered markdown when present.
func messageTurnText(msg *schema.Message) string {
	var parts []string

	for _, part := range msg.Content {
		// Both "text" and "thinking" parts carry their content in Text; thinking
		// is intentionally treated as ordinary agent text.
		if strings.TrimSpace(part.Text) != "" {
			parts = append(parts, part.Text)
		}
	}

	text := strings.Join(parts, "\n\n")

	if msg.Tool != nil {
		toolText := toolTurnText(msg.Tool)
		if toolText != "" {
			if text != "" {
				text += "\n\n" + toolText
			} else {
				text = toolText
			}
		}
	}

	return text
}

// toolTurnText renders a tool call as agent text, reusing the markdown the
// forward pass already produced (Summary + FormattedMarkdown). Falls back to a
// minimal description when neither is available.
func toolTurnText(tool *schema.ToolInfo) string {
	var b strings.Builder

	if tool.Summary != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary) != "" {
		b.WriteString(strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary))
	}

	if tool.FormattedMarkdown != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.FormattedMarkdown) != "" {
		if b.Len() > 0 {
			b.WriteString("\n\n")
		}
		b.WriteString(strings.TrimRight(*tool.FormattedMarkdown, "\n"))
	}

	if b.Len() == 0 {
		// Nothing pre-rendered; emit a minimal, non-empty description so the tool
		// activity is at least acknowledged in the transcript.
		b.WriteString("Tool use: " + tool.Name)
	}

	return b.String()
}

```

```go
package claudecode

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

const (
	// reconstructedClaudeModel is a placeholder model label for reconstructed
	// assistant records. It is only a historical display value — on resume Claude
	// Code uses its own configured model for the next turn.
	reconstructedClaudeModel = "claude-opus-4-8"

	// reconstructedClaudeVersion mirrors the `version` field Claude Code writes on
	// its records. A concrete value matching observed sessions maximizes the
	// chance the loader accepts a synthesized file.
	reconstructedClaudeVersion = "2.1.178"
)

// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Claude Code native JSONL session from the neutral
// SessionData so `claude --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
//
// The forward parser collapses Claude's parentUuid DAG and drops the API
// message.id and thinking signatures, so reconstruction synthesizes a fresh,
// linear parentUuid chain of plain user/assistant text records (tool calls and
// thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData). This is
// the structure validated by the Stage 1 spike. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
	if data == nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
	}

	// Prefer the explicit target workspace; fall back to the session's own root.
	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
	if cwd == "" {
		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
	}

	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
	if len(turns) == 0 {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
	}

	newID := uuid.NewString()
	base := time.Now().UTC()

	var buf bytes.Buffer
	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
	// Preserve <, >, & in conversation/markdown text rather than \u-escaping them.
	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)

	var prevUUID string
	for i, turn := range turns {
		recUUID := uuid.NewString()
		ts := base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second).Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")

		rec := map[string]interface{}{
			"isSidechain": false,
			"userType":    "external",
			"cwd":         cwd,
			"sessionId":   newID,
			"version":     reconstructedClaudeVersion,
			"gitBranch":   "",
			"uuid":        recUUID,
			"timestamp":   ts,
		}

		// Linear chain: the first record roots the DAG (parentUuid null) and
		// carries provenance back to the source session.
		if prevUUID == "" {
			rec["parentUuid"] = nil
			rec["specstorySourceSessionId"] = data.SessionID
		} else {
			rec["parentUuid"] = prevUUID
		}

		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
			rec["type"] = "user"
			rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
				"role":    "user",
				"content": turn.Text,
			}
		} else {
			rec["type"] = "assistant"
			rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
				"id":          "msg_" + recUUID,
				"type":        "message",
				"role":        "assistant",
				"model":       reconstructedClaudeModel,
				"content":     []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
				"stop_reason": "end_turn",
				"usage":       map[string]interface{}{"input_tokens": 1, "output_tokens": 1},
			}
		}

		if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode reconstructed record: %w", err)
		}

		prevUUID = recUUID
	}

	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
		SessionID: newID,
		Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
	}, nil
}

```
