```text
95	// buildExchangesFromMessages groups GeminiMessages into exchanges
96	// An exchange starts with a user message and includes all subsequent agent messages until the next user message
97	func buildExchangesFromMessages(messages []GeminiMessage, workspaceRoot string) ([]Exchange, error) {
98		var exchanges []Exchange
99		var currentExchange *Exchange
100	
101		for _, msg := range messages {
102			switch msg.Type {
103			case "user":
104				// Start a new exchange
105				if currentExchange != nil && len(currentExchange.Messages) > 0 {
106					exchanges = append(exchanges, *currentExchange)
107				}
108	
109				currentExchange = &Exchange{
110					StartTime: msg.Timestamp,
111					Messages:  []Message{},
112				}
113	
114				// Build user message and handle referenced files extraction
115				userMsg, refMsgs := buildUserMessageWithReferences(msg)
116				currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, userMsg)
117	
118				// Add synthetic agent messages for referenced files
119				currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, refMsgs...)
120	
121			case "gemini":
122				// Agent message - add to current exchange
123				if currentExchange == nil {
124					// Create exchange if we don't have one (shouldn't happen normally)
125					currentExchange = &Exchange{
126						StartTime: msg.Timestamp,
127						Messages:  []Message{},
128					}
129				}
130	
131				// Add messages for agent response (may produce multiple messages for tool calls)
132				agentMsgs := buildAgentMessages(msg, workspaceRoot)
133				currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, agentMsgs...)
134				currentExchange.EndTime = msg.Timestamp
135			}
136		}
137	
138		// Add the last exchange if exists
139		if currentExchange != nil && len(currentExchange.Messages) > 0 {
140			exchanges = append(exchanges, *currentExchange)
141		}
142	
143		return exchanges, nil
144	}
145	
146	// buildUserMessageWithReferences creates a user Message and extracts referenced file content
147	// Returns the user message and any synthetic agent messages for referenced files
148	func buildUserMessageWithReferences(msg GeminiMessage) (Message, []Message) {
149		content := strings.TrimSpace(msgContent(msg))
150	
151		// Extract referenced file sections from user message
152		mainContent, refSections := extractReferencedSections(content)
153	
154		var contentParts []ContentPart
155		if mainContent != "" {
156			contentParts = append(contentParts, ContentPart{
157				Type: "text",
158				Text: mainContent,
159			})
160		}
161	
162		userMsg := Message{
163			ID:        msg.ID,
164			Timestamp: msg.Timestamp,
165			Role:      "user",
166			Content:   contentParts,
167		}
168	
169		// Build synthetic agent messages for each referenced file section
170		var refMsgs []Message
171		for _, refContent := range refSections {
172			refMsg := buildReferencedFilesMessage(refContent, msg.Timestamp)
173			refMsgs = append(refMsgs, refMsg)
174		}
175	
176		return userMsg, refMsgs
177	}
178	
179	// buildReferencedFilesMessage creates a synthetic agent message for referenced file content
180	func buildReferencedFilesMessage(content string, timestamp string) Message {
181		// Format the content with HTML escaping
182		formattedContent := formatReferencedFilesMarkdown(content)
183	
184		tool := &ToolInfo{
185			Name:  "referenced_files",
186			Type:  "read",
187			UseID: fmt.Sprintf("ref_%s", timestamp),
188			Input: map[string]interface{}{
189				"content": content,
190			},
191			FormattedMarkdown: &formattedContent,
192		}
193	
194		return Message{
195			Timestamp: timestamp,
196			Role:      "agent",
197			Tool:      tool,
198		}
199	}
200	
201	// formatReferencedFilesMarkdown formats referenced file content for markdown display
202	func formatReferencedFilesMarkdown(content string) string {
203		if content == "" {
204			return ""
205		}
206		return fmt.Sprintf("\n\n<pre><code>%s</code></pre>\n", html.EscapeString(content))
207	}
208	
209	// buildAgentMessages creates Messages from a Gemini agent message
210	// Returns multiple messages: one for thoughts, one per tool call, and one for text content
211	// Token usage is attached to the last message only to avoid double-counting in aggregation
212	func buildAgentMessages(msg GeminiMessage, workspaceRoot string) []Message {
213		var messages []Message
214		usage := convertTokensToUsage(msg.Tokens)
215	
216		// Add thinking content from Thoughts
217		if len(msg.Thoughts) > 0 {
218			thinkingMsg := buildThinkingMessage(msg)
219			messages = append(messages, thinkingMsg)
220		}
221	
222		// Add a message for each tool call
223		for _, toolCall := range msg.ToolCalls {
224			toolMsg := buildToolMessage(msg, toolCall, workspaceRoot)
225			messages = append(messages, toolMsg)
226		}
227	
228		// Add text content message if present
229		textContent := strings.TrimSpace(msgContent(msg))
230		if textContent != "" {
231			textMsg := Message{
232				ID:        msg.ID,
233				Timestamp: msg.Timestamp,
234				Role:      "agent",
235				Model:     msg.Model,
236				Content: []ContentPart{
237					{Type: "text", Text: textContent},
238				},
239			}
240			messages = append(messages, textMsg)
241		}
242	
243		// Attach usage to the last message only to avoid double-counting in aggregation
244		if len(messages) > 0 && usage != nil {
245			messages[len(messages)-1].Usage = usage
246		}
247	
248		return messages
249	}
250	
251	// buildThinkingMessage creates a Message containing thinking content from GeminiThoughts
252	func buildThinkingMessage(msg GeminiMessage) Message {
253		var thinkingText strings.Builder
254		for _, thought := range msg.Thoughts {
255			subject := strings.TrimSpace(thought.Subject)
256			if subject == "" {
257				subject = "Thought"
258			}
259			description := strings.TrimSpace(thought.Description)
260			if description == "" {
261				description = "(no description)"
262			}
263			fmt.Fprintf(&thinkingText, "- **%s** — %s\n", subject, description)
264		}
265	
266		// Trim trailing newline to avoid double-newline when markdown adds closing tag
267		text := strings.TrimSuffix(thinkingText.String(), "\n")
268	
269		return Message{
270			ID:        msg.ID,
271			Timestamp: msg.Timestamp,
272			Role:      "agent",
273			Model:     msg.Model,
274			Content: []ContentPart{
275				{Type: "thinking", Text: text},
276			},
277		}
278	}
279	
280	// buildToolMessage creates a Message for a single tool call
281	func buildToolMessage(msg GeminiMessage, toolCall GeminiToolCall, workspaceRoot string) Message {
282		// Convert GeminiToolCall to ToolInfo
283		toolInfo := convertToolCall(toolCall)
284	
285		// Extract path hints from tool input
286		pathHints := extractPathHintsFromTool(toolCall, workspaceRoot)
287	
288		return Message{
289			ID:        toolCall.ID,
290			Timestamp: toolCall.Timestamp,
291			Role:      "agent",
292			Model:     msg.Model,
293			Tool:      toolInfo,
294			PathHints: pathHints,
295		}
296	}
297	
298	// convertToolCall converts a GeminiToolCall to a ToolInfo
299	func convertToolCall(toolCall GeminiToolCall) *ToolInfo {
300		// Build output from tool results
301		var output map[string]interface{}
302		if len(toolCall.Result) > 0 && toolCall.Result[0].FunctionResponse != nil {
303			output = toolCall.Result[0].FunctionResponse.Response
304		}
305	
306		// If no structured output, try ResultDisplay
307		if output == nil {
308			if display := resultDisplayString(toolCall.ResultDisplay); display != "" {
309				output = map[string]interface{}{
310					"output": display,
311				}
312			}
313		}
314	
315		return &ToolInfo{
316			Name:   toolCall.Name,
317			Type:   classifyGeminiToolType(toolCall.Name),
318			UseID:  toolCall.ID,
319			Input:  toolCall.Args,
320			Output: output,
321		}
322	}
323	
324	// classifyGeminiToolType maps Gemini tool names to standard tool types
325	// Valid types: write, read, search, shell, task, generic, unknown
326	func classifyGeminiToolType(toolName string) string {
327		switch toolName {
328		case "read_file", "read_many_files", "web_fetch":
329			return "read"
330		case "write_file", "replace", "smart_edit":
331			return "write"
332		case "run_shell_command", "list_directory":
333			return "shell"
334		case "search_file_content", "google_web_search", "glob":
335			return "search"
336		case "write_todos":
337			return "task"
338		case "save_memory", "codebase_investigator", "delegate_to_agent":
339			return "generic"
340		default:
341			return "unknown"
342		}
343	}
344	
345	// extractPathHintsFromTool extracts file paths from Gemini tool call args
346	func extractPathHintsFromTool(toolCall GeminiToolCall, workspaceRoot string) []string {
347		var paths []string
348	
349		// Common path field names for Gemini tools
350		pathFields := []string{"file_path", "dir_path", "path"}
351	
352		for _, field := range pathFields {
353			if value := inputAsString(toolCall.Args, field); value != "" {
354				normalizedPath := spi.NormalizePath(value, workspaceRoot)
355				if !slices.Contains(paths, normalizedPath) {
356					paths = append(paths, normalizedPath)
357				}
358			}
359		}
360	
361		// Extract paths from shell commands (redirect targets, file-creating commands)
362		if command := inputAsString(toolCall.Args, "command"); command != "" {
363			cwd := inputAsString(toolCall.Args, "dir_path")
364			if cwd == "" {
365				cwd = workspaceRoot
366			}
367			shellPaths := spi.ExtractShellPathHints(command, cwd, workspaceRoot)
368			for _, sp := range shellPaths {
369				if !slices.Contains(paths, sp) {
370					paths = append(paths, sp)
371				}
372			}
373		}
374	
375		return paths
376	}
377	
378	// extractReferencedSections extracts "--- Content from referenced files ---" sections from a message
379	// Returns the main content (without markers) and the extracted reference sections
380	func extractReferencedSections(message string) (string, []string) {
381		const marker = "--- Content from referenced files ---"
382		var sections []string
383		remaining := message
384	
385		for {
386			idx := strings.Index(remaining, marker)
387			if idx == -1 {
388				break
389			}
390			before := strings.TrimRight(remaining[:idx], "\n ")
391			after := remaining[idx+len(marker):]
392	
393			next := strings.Index(after, marker)
394			var block string
395			if next == -1 {
396				block = strings.TrimSpace(after)
397				remaining = before
398			} else {
399				block = strings.TrimSpace(after[:next])
400				remaining = strings.TrimSpace(before + after[next:])
401			}
402	
403			if block != "" {
404				sections = append(sections, block)
405			}
406		}
407	
408		return strings.TrimSpace(remaining), sections
409	}
410
```

```text
1	package geminicli
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/sha256"
5		"encoding/hex"
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"log/slog"
9		"os"
10		"path/filepath"
11		"sort"
12		"strings"
13	
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
15	)
16	
17	var (
18		osGetwd = os.Getwd
19	)
20	
21	// GeminiPathError describes actionable filesystem failures when locating Gemini data.
22	type GeminiPathError struct {
23		Kind        string   // tmp_missing, project_missing
24		Path        string   // offending path
25		ProjectHash string   // computed hash when relevant
26		KnownHashes []string // hashes discovered on disk (optional)
27		Message     string   // user-facing explanation
28	}
29	
30	func (e *GeminiPathError) Error() string {
31		if e == nil {
32			return "<nil>"
33		}
34		return e.Message
35	}
36	
37	// HashProjectPath deterministically hashes an absolute project path using SHA256.
38	// On case-insensitive filesystems (macOS), it resolves the path to its canonical
39	// form with the correct case to ensure consistent hashing.
40	func HashProjectPath(projectPath string) (string, error) {
41		if projectPath == "" {
42			return "", fmt.Errorf("project path is empty")
43		}
44	
45		absPath, err := filepath.Abs(projectPath)
46		if err != nil {
47			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve absolute path for %q: %w", projectPath, err)
48		}
49	
50		// Resolve to canonical path with correct case (important for case-insensitive filesystems like macOS)
51		canonicalPath, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(absPath)
52		if err != nil {
53			// If getting canonical path fails, use absPath as fallback
54			slog.Warn("HashProjectPath: Failed to get canonical path, using absolute path",
55				"absPath", absPath,
56				"error", err)
57			canonicalPath = absPath
58		} else if canonicalPath != absPath {
59			// Log when the path was canonicalized (case was corrected)
60			slog.Debug("HashProjectPath: Resolved path to canonical form",
61				"original", absPath,
62				"canonical", canonicalPath)
63		}
64	
65		hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(canonicalPath))
66		return hex.EncodeToString(hash[:]), nil
67	}
68	
69	// GetGeminiTmpDir returns the path to the Gemini tmp directory
70	func GetGeminiTmpDir() (string, error) {
71		homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
72		if err != nil {
73			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
74		}
75		return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".gemini", "tmp"), nil
76	}
77	
78	// GetGeminiProjectDir returns the Gemini project directory for the given path.
79	// It calculates the SHA256 hash of the project path.
80	func GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
81		if projectPath == "" {
82			var err error
83			projectPath, err = osGetwd()
84			if err != nil {
85				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
86			}
87		}
88	
89		projectHash, err := HashProjectPath(projectPath)
90		if err != nil {
91			return "", err
92		}
93	
94		tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
95		if err != nil {
96			return "", err
97		}
98	
99		resolvedDir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, projectHash)
100	
101		slog.Debug("GetGeminiProjectDir: Computed Gemini project directory",
102			"projectPath", projectPath,
103			"projectHash", projectHash,
104			"tmpDir", tmpDir,
105			"resolvedDir", resolvedDir)
106	
107		return resolvedDir, nil
108	}
109	
110	// ResolveGeminiProjectDir locates the Gemini project directory on disk.
111	// It tries three strategies in order:
112	//  1. Basename hint — tmpDir/<project-basename> with matching .project_root (fastest, most common for new CLI)
113	//  2. Hash-based — tmpDir/<sha256-hash> (legacy Gemini CLI format)
114	//  3. Full scan — read every .project_root in tmpDir (handles suffixed dirs like my-project-1)
115	func ResolveGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
116		// Resolve projectPath once — used both for hash computation and .project_root matching
117		if projectPath == "" {
118			var err error
119			projectPath, err = osGetwd()
120			if err != nil {
121				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
122			}
123		}
124	
125		hashDir, err := GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
126		if err != nil {
127			return "", err
128		}
129	
130		tmpDir := filepath.Dir(hashDir)
131	
132		slog.Debug("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Checking for Gemini directories",
133			"tmpDir", tmpDir,
134			"hashDir", hashDir)
135	
136		if _, err := osStat(tmpDir); err != nil {
137			if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
138				slog.Warn("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Gemini tmp directory not found", "tmpDir", tmpDir)
139				return "", &GeminiPathError{
140					Kind:    "tmp_missing",
141					Path:    tmpDir,
142					Message: fmt.Sprintf("Gemini tmp directory %q not found. Run the Gemini CLI at least once or verify ~/.gemini exists.", tmpDir),
143				}
144			}
145			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
146		}
147	
148		// 1. Basename hint — check tmpDir/<basename> with .project_root match
149		canonicalProjectPath := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
150		if dir := findProjectDirByBasename(tmpDir, projectPath, canonicalProjectPath); dir != "" {
151			return dir, nil
152		}
153	
154		// 2. Hash-based — legacy Gemini CLI format
155		if _, err := osStat(hashDir); err == nil {
156			slog.Info("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Successfully resolved via hash directory", "hashDir", hashDir)
157			return hashDir, nil
158		} else if !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
159			slog.Warn("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Unexpected error checking hash directory",
160				"hashDir", hashDir, "error", err)
161		}
162	
163		// 3. Full scan — handles suffixed dirs like my-project-1
164		if dir, scanErr := findProjectDirByProjectRoot(tmpDir, projectPath); scanErr != nil {
165			slog.Warn("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Error during .project_root scan",
166				"tmpDir", tmpDir, "error", scanErr)
167		} else if dir != "" {
168			slog.Info("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Found project via .project_root scan", "dir", dir)
169			return dir, nil
170		}
171	
172		// Nothing found — build a helpful error
173		hashes, listErr := ListGeminiProjectHashes()
174		if listErr != nil {
175			hashes = nil
176		}
177	
178		var known string
179		if len(hashes) > 0 {
180			known = strings.Join(hashes, ", ")
181		} else {
182			known = "(none discovered)"
183		}
184	
185		slog.Warn("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Gemini project directory not found",
186			"hashDir", hashDir,
187			"knownHashes", known)
188	
189		return "", &GeminiPathError{
190			Kind:        "project_missing",
191			Path:        hashDir,
192			ProjectHash: filepath.Base(hashDir),
193			KnownHashes: hashes,
194			Message: fmt.Sprintf("No Gemini data found for this project (expected %q). Known project hashes: %s. Start a Gemini CLI session in your repo to create it.",
195				hashDir, known),
196		}
197	}
198	
199	// matchesProjectRoot checks whether dir contains a .project_root file whose
200	// content matches canonicalProjectPath. The stored path is canonicalized before
201	// comparison, but canonicalProjectPath must already be in canonical form (via
202	// canonicalizeProjectPath or spi.GetCanonicalPath).
203	func matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonicalProjectPath string) bool {
204		content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root"))
205		if err != nil {
206			return false
207		}
208	
209		storedPath := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
210		canonicalStored, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(storedPath)
211		if err != nil {
212			slog.Warn("matchesProjectRoot: Failed to canonicalize stored path, using as-is",
213				"storedPath", storedPath, "error", err)
214			canonicalStored = storedPath
215		}
216	
217		return canonicalStored == canonicalProjectPath
218	}
219	
220	// canonicalizeProjectPath returns the canonical form of projectPath for
221	// case-insensitive filesystem matching. Falls back to the original path on error.
222	func canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath string) string {
223		canonical, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(projectPath)
224		if err != nil {
225			slog.Warn("canonicalizeProjectPath: Failed to canonicalize, using as-is",
226				"projectPath", projectPath, "error", err)
227			return projectPath
228		}
229		return canonical
230	}
231	
232	// findProjectDirByBasename tries the most likely name-based directory first:
233	// tmpDir/<basename>. Returns the directory path if its .project_root matches,
234	// or empty string otherwise.
235	func findProjectDirByBasename(tmpDir, projectPath, canonicalProjectPath string) string {
236		basename := filepath.Base(projectPath)
237		candidate := filepath.Join(tmpDir, basename)
238	
239		info, err := osStat(candidate)
240		if err != nil || !info.IsDir() {
241			return ""
242		}
243	
244		if matchesProjectRoot(candidate, canonicalProjectPath) {
245			slog.Info("findProjectDirByBasename: Matched project directory via basename hint",
246				"dir", candidate, "projectPath", projectPath)
247			return candidate
248		}
249	
250		return ""
251	}
252	
253	// findProjectDirByProjectRoot scans tmpDir for any subdirectory whose .project_root
254	// file content matches projectPath. This supports Gemini CLI v0.30.0+ which uses
255	// project-name-based directories (possibly with numeric suffixes like my-project-1).
256	func findProjectDirByProjectRoot(tmpDir, projectPath string) (string, error) {
257		canonicalProjectPath := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
258	
259		entries, err := os.ReadDir(tmpDir)
260		if err != nil {
261			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
262		}
263	
264		for _, entry := range entries {
265			if !entry.IsDir() {
266				continue
267			}
268	
269			dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, entry.Name())
270			if matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonicalProjectPath) {
271				slog.Info("findProjectDirByProjectRoot: Found matching project directory via .project_root",
272					"dir", dir, "projectPath", projectPath)
273				return dir, nil
274			}
275		}
276	
277		return "", nil
278	}
279	
280	// ListGeminiProjectHashes returns the list of project hash directories currently on disk.
281	func ListGeminiProjectHashes() ([]string, error) {
282		tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
283		if err != nil {
284			return nil, err
285		}
286	
287		entries, err := os.ReadDir(tmpDir)
288		if err != nil {
289			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
290		}
291	
292		var hashes []string
293		for _, entry := range entries {
294			if entry.IsDir() {
295				hashes = append(hashes, entry.Name())
296			}
297		}
298	
299		sort.Strings(hashes)
300		return hashes, nil
301	}
302
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
488	func msgContent(msg GeminiMessage) string {
489		return decodeContent(msg.Content)
490	}
491	
492	func decodeContent(raw json.RawMessage) string {
493		if len(raw) == 0 || string(raw) == "null" {
494			return ""
495		}
496	
497		var s string
498		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &s); err == nil {
499			return s
500		}
501	
502		var parts []map[string]any
503		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &parts); err == nil {
504			var builder strings.Builder
505			for _, part := range parts {
506				if text, ok := part["text"].(string); ok && text != "" {
507					if builder.Len() > 0 {
508						builder.WriteString("\n")
509					}
510					builder.WriteString(text)
511				}
512			}
513			if builder.Len() > 0 {
514				return builder.String()
515			}
516		}
517	
518		var obj map[string]any
519		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &obj); err == nil {
520			if text, ok := obj["text"].(string); ok && text != "" {
521				return text
522			}
523			if len(obj) > 0 {
524				bytes, err := json.Marshal(obj)
525				if err == nil {
526					return string(bytes)
527				}
528			}
529		}
530	
531		return strings.Trim(string(raw), "\"")
532	}
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package geminicli

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

// geminiTimestamp formats a time the way Gemini chat records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
func geminiTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
}

// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Gemini CLI native chat session from the neutral
// SessionData so `gemini --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
//
// Gemini stores a session as a single JSON document: a top-level object with
// `sessionId`/`projectHash`/`startTime`/`lastUpdated`/`kind` and a `messages`
// array. Each message is `{id, timestamp, type, content}` where a user message's
// content is `[{text}]` and a gemini message's content is a plain string. Tool
// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`gemini` text messages. See
// docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
	if data == nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
	}

	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
	if cwd == "" {
		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
	}

	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
	if len(turns) == 0 {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
	}

	newID := uuid.NewString()
	base := time.Now().UTC()

	// Best-effort project hash; Gemini stores it but resume keys off the project dir.
	projectHash, _ := HashProjectPath(cwd)

	messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
	for i, turn := range turns {
		msg := map[string]interface{}{
			"id":        uuid.NewString(),
			"timestamp": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
		}
		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
			msg["type"] = "user"
			msg["content"] = []map[string]interface{}{{"text": turn.Text}}
		} else {
			msg["type"] = "gemini"
			msg["content"] = turn.Text
		}
		messages = append(messages, msg)
	}

	session := map[string]interface{}{
		"sessionId":   newID,
		"projectHash": projectHash,
		"startTime":   geminiTimestamp(base),
		"lastUpdated": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(len(turns)) * time.Second)),
		"kind":        "main",
		"messages":    messages,
	}

	var buf bytes.Buffer
	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
	enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
	if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode gemini session: %w", err)
	}

	// Filename: session-<YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM>-<first 8 of id>.json (Gemini convention).
	filename := fmt.Sprintf("session-%s-%s.json", base.Format("2006-01-02T15-04"), newID[:8])

	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
		SessionID: newID,
		Filename:  filename,
		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
	}, nil
}

// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed chat belongs in Gemini's store
// and, unlike the Claude/Codex resolvers, prepares the project directory: Gemini
// associates a tmp dir with a project via a `.project_root` marker, so for a
// project Gemini has never seen we create `~/.gemini/tmp/<name>/` with that marker
// and a `chats/` subdir. Returns `<projectDir>/chats/<filename>`.
func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
	if projectPath == "" {
		var err error
		projectPath, err = osGetwd()
		if err != nil {
			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
		}
	}

	// Reuse the project dir Gemini already associates with this project, if any.
	dir, err := ResolveGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
	if err != nil {
		dir, err = prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
		if err != nil {
			return "", err
		}
	}

	return filepath.Join(dir, "chats", filename), nil
}

// prepareGeminiProjectDir creates a Gemini project directory for a project that
// Gemini has not seen yet: ~/.gemini/tmp/<basename>/ (falling back to the hash dir
// if that basename is taken by a different project), with a `.project_root` marker
// and a `chats/` subdirectory.
func prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
	tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	if err := os.MkdirAll(tmpDir, 0o755); err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini tmp dir: %w", err)
	}

	canonical := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
	dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, filepath.Base(projectPath))

	// If the basename dir exists but belongs to a different project, key off the hash instead.
	if info, statErr := osStat(dir); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() && !matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonical) {
		if dir, err = GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath); err != nil {
			return "", err
		}
	}

	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(dir, "chats"), 0o755); err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini chats dir: %w", err)
	}

	// Write the project-root marker so Gemini maps this dir to the project on resume.
	rootFile := filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root")
	if _, statErr := osStat(rootFile); os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
		if err := os.WriteFile(rootFile, []byte(canonical), 0o644); err != nil {
			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to write gemini .project_root: %w", err)
		}
	}

	return dir, nil
}
```

```go
package geminicli

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }

func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
	fm := "Wrote `hello.fs`:\n\n\```forth\n.\" Hello, World!\" CR\n\```"
	return &schema.SessionData{
		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "gemini-cli", Name: "Gemini CLI", Version: "x"},
		SessionID:     "orig-gemini-123",
		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
			{
				ExchangeID: "orig-gemini-123:0",
				Messages: []schema.Message{
					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Write hello world in Forth."}}},
					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create hello.fs."}}},
					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Forth uses dot-quote for strings."}}},
					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "write_file", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Created hello.fs."}}},
				},
			},
		},
	}
}

// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native Gemini chat JSON, re-parses
// it through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is preserved.
func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
	data := reconstructSampleData()
	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")

	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
	}
	if out.SessionID == "" {
		t.Fatal("expected a fresh session ID")
	}
	if !strings.HasPrefix(out.Filename, "session-") || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".json") {
		t.Errorf("filename %q should look like session-<ts>-<id>.json", out.Filename)
	}

	var session GeminiSession
	if err := json.Unmarshal(out.Content, &session); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("reconstructed content is not valid GeminiSession JSON: %v", err)
	}
	if session.ID != out.SessionID {
		t.Errorf("session sessionId = %q, want %q", session.ID, out.SessionID)
	}

	regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(&session, "/tmp/proj")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
	}

	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
	}
	for i := range expected {
		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
		}
	}
}

// TestNativeSessionPath_CreatesProjectDir verifies that, for a project Gemini has
// not seen, NativeSessionPath creates the tmp project dir with a .project_root
// marker and returns a path under chats/.
func TestNativeSessionPath_CreatesProjectDir(t *testing.T) {
	home := t.TempDir()
	project := t.TempDir()

	originalHome := osUserHomeDir
	osUserHomeDir = func() (string, error) { return home, nil }
	t.Cleanup(func() { osUserHomeDir = originalHome })

	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath(project, "session-x.json")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
	}
	if filepath.Base(path) != "session-x.json" {
		t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
	}
	if filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(path)) != "chats" {
		t.Errorf("path %q should be under a chats/ dir", path)
	}
	// The .project_root marker should exist alongside chats/ and point at the project.
	rootFile := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(filepath.Dir(path)), ".project_root")
	content, err := os.ReadFile(rootFile)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("expected .project_root marker: %v", err)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(string(content), filepath.Base(project)) {
		t.Errorf(".project_root %q should reference the project path", string(content))
	}
}

func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {
	_, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(&schema.SessionData{SessionID: "x", WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp"}, spi.ReconstructOptions{})
	if err == nil {
		t.Fatal("expected error reconstructing a session with no content")
	}
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-17 15:14:21Z)**_

Gemini reconstruction is implemented, tested, and wired. Summary:

## What landed

**`pkg/providers/geminicli/reconstruct.go`** (replaced the stub)
- **`ReconstructSession`** — emits Gemini's native chat JSON: `{sessionId, projectHash, startTime, lastUpdated, kind:"main", messages:[…]}` with `user` messages (`content:[{text}]`) and `gemini` messages (`content:"…"`), fresh UUIDv4, filename `session-<ts>-<id8>.json`. Tool calls/thinking are already flattened to agent text by the shared core.
- **`NativeSessionPath`** — the one Gemini-specific wrinkle: Gemini maps a tmp dir to a project via a `.project_root` marker file, so for a project Gemini hasn't seen this method *prepares the store* (`~/.gemini/tmp/<name>/` + `.project_root` + `chats/`) and returns the chats path. It reuses the existing `ResolveGeminiProjectDir` when a dir already exists. I called this deviation out in the doc.

**Tests** — round-trip (reconstruct → re-parse via the Gemini forward parser → compare turns) and a `NativeSessionPath` test (mocks home dir, verifies the `.project_root` marker is created). `go test ./...`, `golangci-lint` — clean. Binary rebuilt.

The `resume` command needs no changes — it already calls `ReconstructSession`/`NativeSessionPath` polymorphically, so **Gemini now shows up as a working cross-agent target** automatically.

## Honest caveat — needs your real-agent confirmation

Same situation as Codex before you tested it: the round-trip proves *our* parser accepts the output, but whether **`gemini --resume` actually loads a reconstructed session and finds it via the prepared `.project_root` dir** is unverified. The two things most likely to need a tweak if it misbehaves:
- **Where the file lands** — whether Gemini's resume looks in the basename dir / `.project_root` I create, vs. the legacy hash dir.
- **Required fields** — I kept the JSON minimal (and deliberately left provenance *out* of the Gemini file to avoid any strict-schema rejection on the first try).

To test: from a project with a Claude (or Codex) session, run `./specstory resume`, pick the source session → target **Gemini CLI**, and see if it loads + a follow-up shows context carried over. Send a screenshot either way and I'll adjust if needed.

Want me to leave Cursor for a dedicated later pass (it genuinely warrants its own spike given the SQLite/blob/DAG format), or pick up Droid/DeepSeek next (both are flat-file like Gemini, so quick)?

```text
package droidcli

import "encoding/json"

// fdBlockKind describes the semantic type of a parsed session chunk.
type fdBlockKind int

const (
	blockText fdBlockKind = iota
	blockTool
	blockTodo
	blockSummary
)

// fdBlock represents a normalized slice of the Factory Droid transcript.
type fdBlock struct {
	Kind        fdBlockKind
	Role        string
	MessageID   string
	Timestamp   string
	Model       string
	Text        string
	IsReasoning bool
	Tool        *fdToolCall
	Todo        *fdTodoState
	Summary     *fdSummary
}

// fdToolCall captures a tool invocation plus its result.
type fdToolCall struct {
	ID         string
	Name       string
	Input      json.RawMessage
	RiskLevel  string
	RiskReason string
	Result     *fdToolResult
	InvokedAt  string
}

// fdToolResult contains the textual output of a tool.
type fdToolResult struct {
	Content string
	IsError bool
}

// fdTodoState tracks todo list snapshots emitted by the agent.
type fdTodoState struct {
	Items []fdTodoItem
}

// fdTodoItem represents a single todo entry.
type fdTodoItem struct {
	Description string
	Status      string
}

// fdSummary stores compaction summary text from Factory Droid.
type fdSummary struct {
	Title string
	Body  string
}

// fdSession aggregates parsed metadata and blocks.
type fdSession struct {
	ID            string
	Title         string
	CreatedAt     string
	WorkspaceRoot string
	Blocks        []fdBlock
	Slug          string
	RawData       string
	TokenUsage    *fdTokenUsage // Session-level token usage from settings file
}

// fdTokenUsage holds token usage data from the Droid settings.json file.
// This is session-level aggregate data (not per-message).
type fdTokenUsage struct {
	InputTokens         int `json:"inputTokens"`
	OutputTokens        int `json:"outputTokens"`
	CacheCreationTokens int `json:"cacheCreationTokens"`
```

```text
15	// nonAlphanumericDash matches any character that is not alphanumeric or a dash,
16	// mirroring the naming convention Factory CLI uses for session subdirectories.
17	var nonAlphanumericDash = regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9-]`)
18	
19	// projectPathToSessionDirName converts a project path to the factory session directory name.
20	// Factory CLI uses the same convention as Claude Code: non-alphanumeric characters (except
21	// dashes) are replaced with dashes, and a leading dash is prepended.
22	func projectPathToSessionDirName(projectPath string) string {
23		name := nonAlphanumericDash.ReplaceAllString(projectPath, "-")
24		if !strings.HasPrefix(name, "-") {
25			name = "-" + name
26		}
27		return name
28	}
29	
30	// resolveProjectSessionDir returns the factory sessions subdirectory for a specific project path.
31	// Returns "" if projectPath is empty (caller should watch all sessions).
32	func resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
33		if strings.TrimSpace(projectPath) == "" {
34			return "", nil
35		}
36	
37		// Canonicalize the path to match Factory CLI's real-path behaviour, resolving both
38		// symlinks and case differences (important on macOS's case-insensitive filesystem,
39		// where os.Getwd may return a different case than what the CLI recorded on disk).
40		realPath, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(projectPath)
41		if err != nil {
42			// Path may not exist yet; proceed with the given path.
43			realPath = projectPath
44		}
45	
46		sessionsDir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
47		if err != nil {
48			return "", err
49		}
50	
51		return filepath.Join(sessionsDir, projectPathToSessionDirName(realPath)), nil
52	}
53	
54	// isTargetProjectDir returns true if path is a session directory that should be watched.
55	// When projectPath is set, only the project's derived directory qualifies; otherwise
56	// all directories qualify (the no-filter / sync-all case).
57	func isTargetProjectDir(path, projectPath string) bool {
58		if projectPath == "" {
59			return true
60		}
61		expected, err := resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath)
62		if err != nil || expected == "" {
63			// Can't derive expected dir; allow everything to avoid missing sessions.
64			return true
65		}
66		return path == expected
67	}
```

```go
package droidcli

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

// droidTimestamp formats a time the way Factory Droid records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
func droidTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
}

// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Factory Droid native JSONL session from the neutral
// SessionData so `droid --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
//
// Droid stores a session as a `session_start` header followed by `parentId`-chained
// `message` records, each `{message:{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}}`. Tool
// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`assistant` text messages. The store is
// project-scoped (see NativeSessionPath). See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
	if data == nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
	}

	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
	if cwd == "" {
		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
	}

	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
	if len(turns) == 0 {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
	}

	newID := uuid.NewString()
	base := time.Now().UTC()

	title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
	if title == "" {
		title = "Resumed session"
	}

	var buf bytes.Buffer
	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)

	// session_start header (also carries provenance).
	if err := enc.Encode(map[string]interface{}{
		"type":                      "session_start",
		"id":                        newID,
		"title":                     title,
		"sessionTitle":              title,
		"owner":                     os.Getenv("USER"),
		"version":                   2,
		"cwd":                       cwd,
		"isSessionTitleManuallySet": false,
		"sessionTitleAutoStage":     "first_message",
		"specstorySourceSessionId":  data.SessionID,
	}); err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode session_start: %w", err)
	}

	var prevID string
	for i, turn := range turns {
		msgID := uuid.NewString()
		role := "assistant"
		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
			role = "user"
		}

		rec := map[string]interface{}{
			"type":      "message",
			"id":        msgID,
			"timestamp": droidTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
			"message": map[string]interface{}{
				"role":    role,
				"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
			},
		}
		// Linear chain: the first message roots the thread (no parentId).
		if prevID != "" {
			rec["parentId"] = prevID
		}

		if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode message: %w", err)
		}
		prevID = msgID
	}

	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
		SessionID: newID,
		Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
	}, nil
}

// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in Droid's store:
// ~/.factory/sessions/<cwd-encoded>/<filename>. The directory is not required to
// exist (the caller creates it).
func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
	if projectPath == "" {
		var err error
		projectPath, err = os.Getwd()
		if err != nil {
			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
		}
	}

	dir, err := resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath)
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	if dir == "" {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("droidcli: cannot resolve session directory for project path")
	}
	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
}
```

```go
package deepseektui

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

// deepseekTimestamp formats a time the way DeepSeek records do (RFC3339 micros, Z).
func deepseekTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000000Z")
}

// ReconstructSession rebuilds a DeepSeek TUI native session from the neutral
// SessionData so `deepseek --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
//
// DeepSeek stores a session as a single JSON document
// `{schema_version, system_prompt, metadata, messages}` where each message is
// `{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}`. Tool calls and thinking are already
// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData, so reconstruction emits only
// `user`/`assistant` text messages. The session is associated with a project via
// metadata.workspace. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
	if data == nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
	}

	cwd := opts.WorkspaceRoot
	if cwd == "" {
		cwd = data.WorkspaceRoot
	}

	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
	if len(turns) == 0 {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no content to reconstruct")
	}

	newID := uuid.NewString()
	now := deepseekTimestamp(time.Now().UTC())

	title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
	if title == "" {
		title = "Resumed session"
	}

	messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
	for _, turn := range turns {
		role := "assistant"
		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
			role = "user"
		}
		messages = append(messages, map[string]interface{}{
			"role":    role,
			"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
		})
	}

	session := map[string]interface{}{
		"schema_version": 1,
		"system_prompt":  "",
		"metadata": map[string]interface{}{
			"id":            newID,
			"title":         title,
			"created_at":    now,
			"updated_at":    now,
			"message_count": len(messages),
			"total_tokens":  0,
			"model":         "",
			"workspace":     cwd,
			"mode":          "agent",
		},
		"messages": messages,
	}

	var buf bytes.Buffer
	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
	enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
	if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode deepseek session: %w", err)
	}

	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
		SessionID: newID,
		Filename:  newID + ".json",
		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
	}, nil
}

// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in DeepSeek's
// store: ~/.deepseek/sessions/<filename>. DeepSeek sessions are not project-scoped
// by directory (the project is recorded in metadata.workspace), so projectPath is
// unused. The directory is not required to exist (the caller creates it).
func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
	dir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
}

```

```go
package droidcli

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }

func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
	fm := "Wrote `hello.c`:\n\n\```c\n#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void){ printf(\"Hello, World!\\n\"); }\n\```"
	return &schema.SessionData{
		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "droid-cli", Name: "Factory Droid", Version: "x"},
		SessionID:     "orig-droid-123",
		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
			{
				ExchangeID: "orig-droid-123:0",
				Messages: []schema.Message{
					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a hello world in C."}}},
					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create it."}}},
					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Need a main function."}}},
					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Create", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Done."}}},
				},
			},
		},
	}
}

// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native Droid JSONL, re-parses it
// through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is preserved.
func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
	data := reconstructSampleData()
	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")

	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
	}
	if out.SessionID == "" || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".jsonl") {
		t.Fatalf("unexpected id/filename: %q / %q", out.SessionID, out.Filename)
	}

	tmp := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), out.Filename)
	if err := os.WriteFile(tmp, out.Content, 0o644); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("write temp: %v", err)
	}
	session, err := parseFactorySession(tmp)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("parseFactorySession: %v", err)
	}
	regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(session, "/tmp/proj")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
	}

	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
	}
	for i := range expected {
		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
		}
	}
}

func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath("/tmp/some-droid-proj", "abc.jsonl")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".factory", "sessions")) {
		t.Errorf("path %q should be under .factory/sessions", path)
	}
	if !strings.HasSuffix(path, "abc.jsonl") {
		t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(path, "-tmp-some-droid-proj") {
		t.Errorf("path %q should contain the encoded project dir", path)
	}
}

func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {
	_, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(&schema.SessionData{SessionID: "x", WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp"}, spi.ReconstructOptions{})
	if err == nil {
		t.Fatal("expected error reconstructing a session with no content")
	}
}
```

```go
					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Listing files."}}},
					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Use ls."}}},
					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "shell", Type: schema.ToolTypeShell, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},

				},

// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native DeepSeek JSON, re-parses
// it through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is preserved.
func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
	data := reconstructSampleData()
	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")

	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
	}
	if out.SessionID == "" || !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".json") {
		t.Fatalf("unexpected id/filename: %q / %q", out.SessionID, out.Filename)
	}

	var session dsSession
	if err := json.Unmarshal(out.Content, &session); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("reconstructed content is not valid dsSession JSON: %v", err)
	}
	if session.Metadata.ID != out.SessionID {
		t.Errorf("metadata.id = %q, want %q", session.Metadata.ID, out.SessionID)
	}
	if session.Metadata.Workspace != "/tmp/proj" {
		t.Errorf("metadata.workspace = %q, want /tmp/proj", session.Metadata.Workspace)
	}

	regenerated, err := generateAgentSession(&session, "/tmp/proj")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("generateAgentSession: %v", err)
	}

	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
	}
	for i := range expected {
		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
		}
	}
}

	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath("/tmp/whatever", "abc.json")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".deepseek", "sessions")) {
		t.Errorf("path %q should be under .deepseek/sessions", path)
	}

		t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
	}
}

}
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-17 15:24:18Z)**_

Done. Droid and DeepSeek reconstruction are implemented, tested, and wired.

## What landed

**Factory Droid** (`pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct.go`)
- `ReconstructSession` — `session_start` header + `parentId`-chained `message` records (`{message:{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}}`), `user`/`assistant` roles, fresh UUIDv4.
- `NativeSessionPath` — `~/.factory/sessions/<cwd-encoded>/<id>.jsonl` (project-scoped like Claude; reuses the existing `resolveProjectSessionDir` encoder).

**DeepSeek TUI** (`pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct.go`)
- `ReconstructSession` — single JSON doc `{schema_version, system_prompt, metadata, messages}` with `user`/`assistant` text messages; **`metadata.workspace` = cwd** is what associates the session with the project (DeepSeek isn't dir-scoped).
- `NativeSessionPath` — `~/.deepseek/sessions/<id>.json`.

**Tests** — round-trip for both (reconstruct → re-parse via each forward parser → compare turns) + `NativeSessionPath` checks. One thing I verified along the way: Droid's forward parser runs `cleanUserText` (strips `<system-reminder>`, trims) on user text, so I kept the round-trip's user text clean. `go test ./...`, `golangci-lint` — all clean. Binary rebuilt.

Both auto-appear as cross-agent resume targets (the `resume` command calls the SPI polymorphically).

## Status

Reconstruction now works for **5 of 6** providers: Claude, Codex, Gemini, Droid, DeepSeek. **Only Cursor remains** (the SQLite `store.db` lift you set aside).

Same honest caveat as before: round-trips prove our parsers accept the output, but real `droid --resume` / `deepseek --resume` acceptance of a reconstructed file is unconfirmed — worth a quick real-agent pass like you did for Gemini/Codex when convenient. Two spots most likely to need a tweak if either misbehaves:
- **Droid** — whether it needs the `.settings.json` sidecar (I skip it) or any `session_start` field I left out.
- **DeepSeek** — whether resume keys off `metadata.workspace` the way I assumed for project association.

Want to do those confirmation runs, circle back to Cursor, or move on to Stage 2 (cross-project)?

```text
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
15		_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // Pure Go SQLite driver
16	)
17	
18	// BlobRecord represents a raw blob from the Cursor SQLite database
19	type BlobRecord struct {
20		RowID int             `json:"rowid"`
21		ID    string          `json:"id"`
22		Data  json.RawMessage `json:"data"` // Keep as raw JSON to preserve structure
23	}
24	
25	// extractFirstUserMessage extracts the full text of the first suitable user message
26	// Returns empty string if no suitable message is found
27	func extractFirstUserMessage(blobRecords []BlobRecord) string {
28		for _, record := range blobRecords {
29			// Try to parse the data to check role and content
30			var data struct {
31				Role    string `json:"role"`
32				Content []struct {
33					Type string `json:"type"`
34					Text string `json:"text"`
35				} `json:"content"`
36			}
37	
38			if err := json.Unmarshal(record.Data, &data); err != nil {
39				continue // Skip if can't parse
40			}
41	
42			// Check if this is a user message
43			if data.Role != "user" {
44				continue
45			}
46	
47			// Check if we have content with text
48			if len(data.Content) == 0 || data.Content[0].Type != "text" {
49				continue
50			}
51	
52			text := data.Content[0].Text
53	
54			// Strip <user_query> tags if present
55			text = stripUserQueryTags(text)
56	
57			// Skip if starts with <user_info> (may be JSON-encoded as \u003cuser_info\u003e)
58			if strings.HasPrefix(text, "<user_info>") || strings.HasPrefix(text, "\u003cuser_info\u003e") {
59				continue
60			}
61	
62			// Return the full text
63			if strings.TrimSpace(text) != "" {
64				return text
65			}
66		}
67	
68		return ""
69	}
70	
71	// extractSlugFromBlobs extracts a slug from the first suitable user message
72	func extractSlugFromBlobs(blobRecords []BlobRecord) string {
73		text := extractFirstUserMessage(blobRecords)
74		if text == "" {
75			slog.Debug("No suitable user message found for slug extraction")
76			return ""
77		}
78		slug := spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(text)
79		slog.Debug("Extracted slug from user message", "slug", slug)
80		return slug
81	}
82	
83	// validateCursorDatabase validates that the SQLite database has the expected Cursor schema.
84	// This ensures we're working with a genuine Cursor database and not some other SQLite file.
85	func validateCursorDatabase(db *sql.DB) error {
86		// Define the expected schema for a Cursor database
87		type tableSchema struct {
88			name    string
89			columns []string // Required columns
90		}
91	
92		requiredTables := []tableSchema{
93			{
94				name:    "blobs",
95				columns: []string{"id", "data"},
96			},
97			{
98				name:    "meta",
99				columns: []string{"key", "value"},
100			},
101		}
102	
103		// Validate each required table exists and has expected columns
104		for _, table := range requiredTables {
105			// Check if table exists
106			var tableExists int
107			err := db.QueryRow(
108				"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name=?",
109				table.name,
110			).Scan(&tableExists)
111			if err != nil {
112				return fmt.Errorf("failed to check for table %s: %w", table.name, err)
113			}
114			if tableExists == 0 {
115				return fmt.Errorf("invalid Cursor database: missing required table '%s'", table.name)
116			}
117	
118			// Validate columns exist in the table
119			// Note: pragma_table_info doesn't support parameter binding, so we use fmt.Sprintf
120			// This is safe because table.name comes from our hardcoded schema, not user input
121			for _, column := range table.columns {
122				var columnExists int
123				query := fmt.Sprintf("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pragma_table_info('%s') WHERE name=?", table.name)
124				err := db.QueryRow(query, column).Scan(&columnExists)
125				if err != nil {
126					return fmt.Errorf("failed to check column %s.%s: %w", table.name, column, err)
127				}
128				if columnExists == 0 {
129					return fmt.Errorf("invalid Cursor database: table '%s' missing required column '%s'", table.name, column)
130				}
131			}
132		}
133	
134		slog.Debug("Database schema validation passed")
135		return nil
136	}
137	
138	// getSessionTimestamp retrieves the session creation timestamp from the meta table
139	func getSessionTimestamp(db *sql.DB) (string, error) {
140		var rawValue string
141		err := db.QueryRow("SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key='0'").Scan(&rawValue)
142		if err != nil {
143			slog.Debug("Failed to get metadata from meta table", "error", err)
144			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get metadata: %w", err)
145		}
146	
147		// The value is hex-encoded
148		jsonBytes, err := hex.DecodeString(rawValue)
149		if err != nil {
150			slog.Debug("Failed to decode hex metadata", "error", err)
151			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to decode hex: %w", err)
152		}
153	
154		// Parse the JSON
155		var metadata struct {
156			CreatedAt int64 `json:"createdAt"`
157		}
158		if err := json.Unmarshal(jsonBytes, &metadata); err != nil {
159			slog.Debug("Failed to parse metadata JSON", "error", err)
160			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse metadata: %w", err)
161		}
162	
163		slog.Debug("Found createdAt in metadata", "createdAt", metadata.CreatedAt)
164	
165		if metadata.CreatedAt > 0 {
166			// Convert milliseconds to time
167			ts := time.Unix(metadata.CreatedAt/1000, (metadata.CreatedAt%1000)*1000000)
168			slog.Debug("Converted timestamp", "timestamp", ts)
169			// Return as ISO 8601 format
170			return ts.Format(time.RFC3339), nil
171		}
172	
173		// Return current time as fallback
174		return time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339), nil
175	}
176	
177	// ReadSessionData reads all blobs from a Cursor session's store.db file
178	// Returns the creation timestamp, slug, blob records in topologically sorted order, and orphaned blobs
179	func ReadSessionData(sessionPath string) (string, string, []BlobRecord, []BlobRecord, error) {
180		dbPath := filepath.Join(sessionPath, "store.db")
181	
182		slog.Debug("Opening Cursor CLI SQLite database", "path", dbPath)
183	
184		// Open the database in read-only mode
185		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dbPath+"?mode=ro")
186		if err != nil {
187			return "", "", nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open database: %w", err)
188		}
189		defer func() {
190			if err := db.Close(); err != nil {
191				slog.Debug("Failed to close database", "error", err)
192			}
193		}()
194	
195		slog.Debug("Successfully opened database", "path", dbPath)
196	
197		// Enable WAL mode for non-blocking reads
198		// This prevents readers from blocking the cursor-agent writer
199		if _, err := db.Exec("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL"); err != nil {
200			// Log warning but continue - not fatal if WAL fails
201			slog.Warn("Failed to enable WAL mode", "error", err)
202		} else {
203			slog.Debug("Enabled WAL mode for non-blocking reads")
204		}
205	
206		// Validate that this is actually a Cursor database with the expected schema
207		if err := validateCursorDatabase(db); err != nil {
208			return "", "", nil, nil, err
209		}
210	
211		// Get the session timestamp from metadata
212		createdAt, err := getSessionTimestamp(db)
213		if err != nil {
214			// Log but don't fail - use current time as fallback
215			slog.Debug("Failed to get session timestamp, using current time", "error", err)
216			createdAt = time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339)
217		}
218	
219		// Query all blobs
220		rows, err := db.Query("SELECT rowid, id, data FROM blobs")
221		if err != nil {
222			return "", "", nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to query blobs: %w", err)
223		}
224		defer func() {
225			if err := rows.Close(); err != nil {
226				slog.Debug("Failed to close rows", "error", err)
227			}
228		}()
229	
230		// Count total blobs for logging
231		var totalBlobs, jsonBlobs, skippedBlobs int
232	
233		slog.Debug("Starting to process blobs from database")
234	
235		// Collect all blobs and build a map for sorting
236		blobMap := make(map[string]*CursorBlob)
237		var allRowIDs []int // Collect all rowids for debugging
238	
239		for rows.Next() {
240			var rowid int
241			var id string
242			var data []byte
243	
244			totalBlobs++
245	
246			if err := rows.Scan(&rowid, &id, &data); err != nil {
247				slog.Debug("Failed to scan row", "error", err)
248				continue // Skip problematic rows
249			}
250	
251			// Collect rowid for debugging
252			allRowIDs = append(allRowIDs, rowid)
253	
254			// Log every single blob we process
255			idDisplay := id
256			if len(idDisplay) > 16 {
257				idDisplay = idDisplay[:16] + "..."
258			}
259			slog.Debug("Processing blob",
260				"rowid", rowid,
261				"id", idDisplay,
262				"data_len", len(data))
263	
264			// Parse references from the binary data
265			references := parseReferences(data, id, rowid)
266	
267			// Try to extract JSON from the binary data
268			jsonData := extractJSONFromBinary(data)
269			if jsonData != nil {
270				jsonBlobs++
271				blobMap[id] = &CursorBlob{
272					RowID:      rowid,
273					ID:         id,
274					Data:       json.RawMessage(jsonData),
275					RawData:    data,
276					References: references,
277				}
278			} else {
279				// If no JSON found, skip for now
280				// We may want to include these as reference-only blobs in the future
281				skippedBlobs++
282				// Uncomment this to see JSON detection in action when debugging the sqlite reader
283				idDisplay := id
284				if len(idDisplay) > 16 {
285					idDisplay = idDisplay[:16] + "..."
286				}
287				slog.Debug("Skipping blob (no JSON content found)",
288					"blob_id", idDisplay,
289					"rowid", rowid,
290					"has_references", len(references) > 0,
291					"reference_count", len(references))
292			}
293		}
294	
295		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
296			return "", "", nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("error iterating rows: %w", err)
297		}
298	
299		// Sort rowids for easier debugging
300		sort.Ints(allRowIDs)
301	
302		// Log all rowids we got from the database - check if 1555-1560 are present
303		slog.Debug("All rowids from database query",
304			"count", len(allRowIDs),
305			"min", allRowIDs[0],
306			"max", allRowIDs[len(allRowIDs)-1],
307			"rowids", allRowIDs)
308	
309		slog.Debug("Blob processing summary",
310			"totalBlobs", totalBlobs,
311			"jsonBlobs", jsonBlobs,
312			"skippedBlobs", skippedBlobs)
313	
314		// Perform topological sort on the blobs
315		sortedBlobs, orphanedBlobs, err := topologicalSort(blobMap)
316		if err != nil {
317			return "", "", nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to sort blobs: %w", err)
318		}
319	
320		// Convert sorted blobs to BlobRecord array (without References and RawData)
321		var blobRecords []BlobRecord
322		for _, blob := range sortedBlobs {
323			blobRecords = append(blobRecords, BlobRecord{
324				RowID: blob.RowID,
325				ID:    blob.ID,
326				Data:  blob.Data,
327			})
328		}
329	
330		// Convert orphaned blobs to BlobRecord array
331		var orphanRecords []BlobRecord
332		var orphanRowIDs []int
333		for _, blob := range orphanedBlobs {
334			orphanRecords = append(orphanRecords, BlobRecord{
335				RowID: blob.RowID,
336				ID:    blob.ID,
337				Data:  blob.Data,
338			})
339			orphanRowIDs = append(orphanRowIDs, blob.RowID)
340		}
341	
342		if len(orphanRowIDs) > 0 {
343			sort.Ints(orphanRowIDs)
344			slog.Debug("Orphaned blob rowids", "rowids", orphanRowIDs, "count", len(orphanRowIDs))
345		}
346	
347		// Log final DAG rowids before markdown generation
348		slog.Debug("Final DAG-sorted blob rowids before markdown generation:")
349		var rowids []int
350		for _, blob := range blobRecords {
351			rowids = append(rowids, blob.RowID)
352		}
353		slog.Debug("DAG rowids", "rowids", rowids, "count", len(rowids))
354	
355		// Extract slug from the final blob records
356		slug := extractSlugFromBlobs(blobRecords)
357	
358		return createdAt, slug, blobRecords, orphanRecords, nil
359	}
360	
361	// min returns the minimum of two integers
362	func min(a, b int) int {
363		if a < b {
364			return a
365		}
366		return b
367	}
368
```

```text
1	package cursorcli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/hex"
6		"encoding/json"
7		"log/slog"
8		"strings"
9	)
10	
11	// Protocol Buffer constants for Cursor's blob reference encoding
12	const (
13		// Protocol Buffer field tags for blob references
14		pbFieldTag1 = 0x0a // Protocol Buffer field 1 tag (wire type 2: length-delimited)
15		pbFieldTag2 = 0x12 // Protocol Buffer field 2 tag (wire type 2: length-delimited)
16	
17		// Blob ID encoding
18		pbBlobIDLength = 0x20 // Length byte indicating 32-byte blob ID follows
19	
20		// Blob ID validation
21		minNonPrintableBytes = 8 // Minimum non-printable bytes to consider valid blob ID (vs text data)
22	)
23	
24	// CursorBlob represents a blob with its references and content
25	type CursorBlob struct {
26		RowID      int             `json:"rowid"`
27		ID         string          `json:"id"`
28		Data       json.RawMessage `json:"data"`
29		RawData    []byte          `json:"-"` // Original binary data, not included in JSON output
30		References []string        `json:"-"` // IDs of blobs this blob references, not included in JSON output
31	}
32	
33	// parseReferences extracts blob ID references from binary data.
34	// Cursor uses Protocol Buffer encoding where references to other blobs are stored
35	// as length-delimited fields with 32-byte blob IDs. This creates a directed acyclic
36	// graph (DAG) structure that represents the conversation flow and message dependencies.
37	func parseReferences(data []byte, currentBlobID string, currentRowID int) []string {
38		var references []string
39		seenRefs := make(map[string]bool) // Track to avoid duplicates
40	
41		// Search the entire blob for references
42		// While this could potentially have false positives in message content,
43		// the entropy check should filter most of them out and the chance of
44		// false positives matching actual message IDs is astronomically low, so
45		// in practice any false positives will have no impart on the DAG structure
46		maxSearchLen := len(data)
47	
48		for i := 0; i < maxSearchLen-33; i++ {
49			// Check for Protocol Buffer field markers that indicate a blob reference
50			if (data[i] == pbFieldTag1 || data[i] == pbFieldTag2) && i+1 < len(data) {
51				// Next byte should be the length of the blob ID
52				if data[i+1] == pbBlobIDLength && i+34 <= len(data) {
53					// Extract the 32-byte blob ID
54					idBytes := data[i+2 : i+34]
55	
56					// Validate that this looks like a blob ID by checking entropy.
57					// Real blob IDs are random bytes with high entropy, while false positives
58					// from message content tend to be ASCII text with low entropy.
59					isLikelyBlobID := false
60					nonPrintableCount := 0
61					for _, b := range idBytes {
62						if b < 0x20 || b > 0x7E {
63							nonPrintableCount++
64						}
65					}
66					// A real 32-byte blob ID will have ~75% non-printable bytes due to randomness.
67					// We use a lower threshold to avoid false negatives while still filtering out
68					// most ASCII text that might accidentally match the Protocol Buffer pattern.
69					if nonPrintableCount >= minNonPrintableBytes {
70						isLikelyBlobID = true
71					}
72	
73					if isLikelyBlobID {
74						// Convert to hex string
75						idHex := hex.EncodeToString(idBytes)
76						if !seenRefs[idHex] {
77							references = append(references, idHex)
78							seenRefs[idHex] = true
79	
80							// Uncomment this to see the reference counting in action when debugging the blob parser
81							// Safely truncate IDs for logging
82							referencedIDDisplay := idHex
83							if len(referencedIDDisplay) > 16 {
84								referencedIDDisplay = referencedIDDisplay[:16] + "..."
85							}
86							currentIDDisplay := currentBlobID
87							if len(currentIDDisplay) > 16 {
88								currentIDDisplay = currentIDDisplay[:16] + "..."
89							}
90							slog.Debug("Found blob reference",
91								"in_blob", currentIDDisplay,
92								"rowid", currentRowID,
93								"references_blob", referencedIDDisplay,
94								"at_byte_offset", i)
95	
96							i += 33 // Skip past this reference
97						}
98					}
99				}
100			}
101		}
102	
103		return references
104	}
105	
106	// extractJSONFromBinary attempts to extract JSON data from binary blob data.
107	// Cursor stores JSON embedded in binary format. This function uses a proper JSON
108	// decoder to extract valid JSON objects, which is more robust than manual parsing.
109	func extractJSONFromBinary(data []byte) []byte {
110		// Convert to string for easier pattern searching
111		dataStr := string(data)
112	
113		// Look for JSON object patterns that indicate the start of JSON data
114		jsonStart := -1
115		jsonPatterns := []string{`{"id":`, `{"role":`, `{"type":`, `{"content":`, `{"`}
116	
117		for _, pattern := range jsonPatterns {
118			if idx := strings.Index(dataStr, pattern); idx != -1 && (jsonStart == -1 || idx < jsonStart) {
119				jsonStart = idx
120			}
121		}
122	
123		if jsonStart == -1 {
124			return nil // No JSON found
125		}
126	
127		// Use json.Decoder to properly extract the JSON object
128		// This handles escaped characters, nested objects, and other edge cases correctly
129		decoder := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(data[jsonStart:]))
130	
131		// Use json.RawMessage to capture the JSON without parsing its structure
132		var result json.RawMessage
133		if err := decoder.Decode(&result); err != nil {
134			// If decoding fails, log and return nil
135			slog.Debug("Failed to decode JSON from binary data",
136				"error", err,
137				"dataOffset", jsonStart,
138				"dataPreview", string(data[jsonStart:min(jsonStart+50, len(data))]))
139			return nil
140		}
141	
142		return result
143	}
```

```text
1	package cursorcli
2	
3	import (
4		"log/slog"
5	)
6	
7	// topologicalSort performs topological sorting on the blob graph
8	// Returns sorted blobs based on their reference relationships and orphaned blobs
9	func topologicalSort(blobs map[string]*CursorBlob) ([]*CursorBlob, []*CursorBlob, error) {
10		// Find the blob with the most outgoing references (likely the end of conversation)
11		var endBlob *CursorBlob
12		maxRefs := 0
13		for _, blob := range blobs {
14			if len(blob.References) > maxRefs {
15				maxRefs = len(blob.References)
16				endBlob = blob
17			} else if len(blob.References) == maxRefs && endBlob != nil {
18				// If tied, prefer the one with higher rowid (more recent)
19				if blob.RowID > endBlob.RowID {
20					endBlob = blob
21				}
22			}
23		}
24	
25		// If no blob has references, fall back to highest rowid
26		if endBlob == nil {
27			for _, blob := range blobs {
28				if endBlob == nil || blob.RowID > endBlob.RowID {
29					endBlob = blob
30				}
31			}
32		}
33	
34		// Walk backwards from the end through references
35		var sorted []*CursorBlob
36		visited := make(map[string]bool)
37	
38		if endBlob != nil {
39			// Safely truncate ID for logging
40			idDisplay := endBlob.ID
41			if len(idDisplay) > 16 {
42				idDisplay = idDisplay[:16] + "..."
43			}
44			slog.Debug("Starting backward traversal from end blob",
45				"id", idDisplay,
46				"rowid", endBlob.RowID,
47				"numRefs", len(endBlob.References))
48	
49			// Use recursive DFS to traverse backwards
50			var traverse func(blobID string)
51			traverse = func(blobID string) {
52				if visited[blobID] {
53					return
54				}
55	
56				blob, exists := blobs[blobID]
57				if !exists {
58					return
59				}
60	
61				visited[blobID] = true
62	
63				// First visit all referenced blobs (go deeper)
64				for _, refID := range blob.References {
65					traverse(refID)
66				}
67	
68				// Then add this blob (post-order traversal gives us the right order)
69				sorted = append(sorted, blob)
70			}
71	
72			// Start traversal from the end blob
73			traverse(endBlob.ID)
74		}
75	
76		// Collect orphaned blobs (unreferenced duplicates)
77		var orphaned []*CursorBlob
78		for id, blob := range blobs {
79			if !visited[id] {
80				orphaned = append(orphaned, blob)
81			}
82		}
83	
84		if len(orphaned) > 0 {
85			slog.Debug("Backward traversal complete, found orphaned blobs",
86				"connected", len(sorted),
87				"orphaned", len(orphaned))
88	
89			// Uncomment this for debugging DAG sorting, logs orphaned blob info
90			for _, blob := range orphaned {
91				// Safely truncate ID for logging
92				idDisplay := blob.ID
93				if len(idDisplay) > 16 {
94					idDisplay = idDisplay[:16] + "..."
95				}
96				slog.Debug("Orphaned blob",
97					"rowid", blob.RowID,
98					"id", idDisplay)
99			}
100		}
101	
102		slog.Debug("Topological sort complete", "totalBlobs", len(blobs), "sortedBlobs", len(sorted))
103	
104		return sorted, orphaned, nil
105	}
```

```text
12	// GetCursorChatsDir returns the path to the Cursor chats directory
13	// Returns the path and nil on success, or empty string and error on failure
14	func GetCursorChatsDir() (string, error) {
15		homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
16		if err != nil {
17			return "", err
18		}
19		return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".cursor", "chats"), nil
20	}

22	// GetProjectHashDir calculates the MD5 hash of the project path and returns the Cursor project directory
23	// Returns the hash directory path and nil on success, or empty string and error on failure
24	func GetProjectHashDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
25		// Get the cursor chats directory
26		chatsDir, err := GetCursorChatsDir()
27		if err != nil {
28			return "", err
29		}
30	
31		// Get absolute path of the project directory
32		absPath, err := filepath.Abs(projectPath)
33		if err != nil {
34			return "", err
35		}
36	
37		// Resolve to canonical path with correct case (important for case-insensitive filesystems like macOS)
38		canonicalPath, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(absPath)
39		if err != nil {
40			// If getting canonical path fails, use absPath as fallback
41			canonicalPath = absPath
42		}
43	
44		// Calculate MD5 hash of the canonical path
45		hash := md5.Sum([]byte(canonicalPath))
46		projectHash := hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])
47	
48		// Build and return the project-specific hash directory path
49		return filepath.Join(chatsDir, projectHash), nil
50	}
51	
52	// GetCursorSessionDirs returns all session directories within a project hash directory
53	// Returns a slice of session directory names (UUIDs) and nil on success, or nil and error on failure
54	func GetCursorSessionDirs(hashDir string) ([]string, error) {
55		// Check if the hash directory exists
56		if _, err := os.Stat(hashDir); err != nil {
57			return nil, err
58		}
59	
60		// Read the hash directory to get session subdirectories
61		entries, err := os.ReadDir(hashDir)
62		if err != nil {
63			return nil, err
64		}
65	
66		// Filter to only directories
67		var sessionDirs []string
68		for _, entry := range entries {
69			if entry.IsDir() {
70				sessionDirs = append(sessionDirs, entry.Name())
71			}
72		}
73	
74		return sessionDirs, nil
75	}
76

78	func HasStoreDB(hashDir, sessionID string) bool {

81		return err == nil
82	}
```
